IN:FLUX is a sci-fi tabletop game set on Earth. The game involves four unique factions that fight for control of a map. Players expand across the map and vie for control of territory. A simplified user experience allows for fast play. The game has an economy which is simple enough to allow for focusing on strategy. Modular map tiles and various factions bonuses allow for re-play value. Players can upgrade their tech tree and play a bluffing game with opponents. Different technologies have different synergies.

You'll enjoy this game if you like: Area Control, Strategy, Combat, Damage, Territory, Economy, Empire

Air Combat 1960 - 2000

Missile Threat is a simple yet comprehensive modern air combat game, covering every major air conflict from 1960 to 2000, and designed for use with miniature aircraft.

Missile Threat is aimed at providing a realistic air environment and short games of less than 2 hours (including preparation).

What does Missile Threat offer?


     Simple yet accurate mechanics: Missiles actually track aircraft in-game, forcing you to conduct defensive breaks or last-ditch missile defenses.
     Fixed-wing aircraft, helicopters, ground forces, naval vessels and air defenses all play an important role
     Stats for over 250 different aircraft
     11 Conflicts covered: Vietnam, Arab-Israeli wars (Six-Day, Yom Kippur and 1982 Lebanon war covered), Indo-Pakistani Wars, South African Border War, Ogaden War, Iran-Iraq War, Falklands, 1991 Gulf War, Bosnia / Kosovo, Eritrean-Ethiopian War, and the Second Taiwan Strait Crisis as a bonus conflict. Many of the longer conflicts are split into periods for more accuracy as units and tactics evolve.
     Each conflict has a tailor-made aircraft and ordnance list for each faction, as well as accurate options for ground assets like SAM sites and AAA.
     Flight plan "bidding" system: Players must write a flight plan before the game starts, indicating when aircraft will enter and leave the game. Will you try to get your ground attack aircraft in before the enemy fighters arrive? or wait until later? Will you send your fighters in early because you know the enemy will try to get their bombing done early?
     Complete air environment: Ground assets like AAA, SAM sites and various radars are an important aspect of the game, allowing weapons like the AGM-88 HARM to have a use and for SEAD flights to have a purpose.
     Pilots can be shot down, wounded, captured by enemy ground forces, picked up by on-call SAR helicopters, or make their way back to friendly lines on foot. Rescuing downed pilots is important for victory.
     4 Mission types to fly: Combat Air Patrol, Strike, Combat Search and Rescue, and Close Air Support. CAS missions will see an active land battle being fought while you attempt to support friendlies.
     Detailed yet simple rules for Naval Vessels and Submarines. Naval Vessels can launch and recover helicopters and fixed-wing aircraft, and could potentially be caught unprepared for combat...
     Forward Air Controllers, Recon, AWACS, AEW and Anti-Submarine Warfare aircraft all have a purpose in-game Campaign System provided so each game can have an effect on the next: Wiped out all the enemy SAM sites? I guess you won't need to focus on SEAD as much any more!


Missile Threat is the culmination of 8 months of research and play-testing. Markers are provided for the various missiles, ground assets and ground forces, as well as naval vessel markers for the Falklands.

—description from the publisher

description:
The queen has dismissed her court cook and is searching for a new one. Travel through a beautiful fantasy world and gather the right ingredients to serve the Queen the best dishes of the entire country.

Players each choose a fantasy cook pawn to play with. They all have 6 actions to play during each round in which they have to travel, harvest ingredients and prepare meals.

Each player receives recipe cards, which show the ingredients he has to collect in order to cook the recipe. Depending on the amount and level of difficulty of the ingredients, some cards will be worth more than others.

Block entrances and exits, make the glutton eat the collected ingredients of other players, let it rain to make the crops grow faster, go to the market to exchange ingredients, be the first to provide 50 tables with food and win the game.

Players split into two teams. Each round a player puts his device into his mouth and flips the sand timer. He draws a card and tries to say the word written on it as many times as he has to in order his team finds the word! When he is out of time he gathers the cards found by his team. The team with the most cards wins at the end of the game!

The Kickstarter edition and Kickstarter Deluxe edition both come with exclusive components not found in the retail edition including 5th player components (retail supports up to 4 players) and the mountain expansion.


The Frostrivers tribe dwells along the Great Frozen River. Its members live in harmony with Nature. The tribe obeys the laws of the Four Elders, themselves guided by the most venerable of the Elders, the Neta-Tanka. When the Neta-Tanka enters the twilight of life, the Frostrivers gather at the Sacred Grounds to designate a successor. During this ceremony, each clan presents a young leader who will need to demonstrate generosity and the ability to provide for the tribe in order to become the new Neta-Tanka.

Gameplay:
When it is your turn, place a worker on the board. All the players place their workers, one at the time, until everybody has placed all their workers.

Then, in the order of the turn, the players trigger all their workers.

Think ahead, because some actions require other actions to be triggered beforehand! For example, if you want to get pelts, the "Hunt" action must have been triggered earlier in the turn. And when you skin the game to get the pelts, then the meat becomes available for the other players. It is crucial to remember the turn's order when you place your workers, or they might have nothing to do when you resolve your actions!

Another key point of the game is that the spots on the board are linked with each other. Triggering 2 actions that are linked with each other gives you a boost (by improving those actions, or giving you extra resources, ...).

There are different ways of scoring points in the game. You score points by:

     Building tents for your tribe
     Feeding your tribe
     Handcrafting objects
     Raising the highest Totem for the deities
     Showing generosity to your opponents


The player with the most points at the end is declared Nētā-Tanka!

—description from the publisher

Each species has special advantages and disadvantages, and some species introduce new resources into the game. description:
UNDEAD ROAD is a cooperative card game for 1 to 2 players*, where the participants cross an inhospitable road, represented by the deck of cards, in which they must survive to endless waves of zombies as much as possible, managing the limits resources they will find in a strategic and convenient way for the group.

UNDEAD ROAD is defined as a "Endless survival game". Each game lasts until the players are defeated by the zombies in the deck. The goal is to endure the most "waves" as possible. Each wave is the full use of a deck. When all the cards of the deck have been played, are shuffled again and start another "wave".


    Each deck allows games of 1 to 2 players. You can play games of 3 to 4 players with an additional deck. You can play games of 5 to 6 players with a third deck, taking out the Resources Cards of it.


How to play:

In Undead Road, players (up to two with each gamebox set) collaborate each other trying to go as far as they can through a common deck of cards that represents a dangerous road, full of danger and zombies.

Each player has a card that represents a survivor. That card is used to record the life points of the survivor (turning the card clockwise) and equipping up to a maximum of two objects (one on each side of the card).

During his turn, each player draws a card from the deck and all the players try to resolve the situation that the card presents. From the decks can come out zombies, objects of different types (weapons, healing stuff, ammunition, resources) or multiplying cards that force you to draw huge number of cards at once. Players must agree together how to solve the situation (eliminate the zombies, equip or use the objects, exchange them, etc).

Gradually, the game ends up beating the players. The idea is to try to go through the entire deck as many times as possible.

You are a jester in the royal court, that means it's your duty to entertain. As an amateur acrobat in a poorly suited chamber, you ply your trade: tumbles. Can you please the court?

Folly is a meeple-rolling game, whereby you accumulat "interest" points, and press your luck.
Points are accumulated for rolling your jester-meeple, and appealing to the various members of the court; when the show is over, collect points from only one member of the court.

There are 4 modes of play: solo, 2-player co-op, multi-player competitive, and multi-team co-op competitive.

- Description from Designer

description:
Get the Gummies is the simple, yet strategic, trick-taking card game in which you try to score as many points as possible by grabbing all the adorable gummy bears you can get your hands on, while sabotaging the progress of your opponents!

How To Play
Shuffle the deck. Deal each player 10 cards. Set the remaining cards aside. The player to the dealer's left has first turn and begins the hand's first trick.

Each trick consists of every player placing 1 card into the center of the playing surface, in clockwise order. The first card played determines the trick's dominant color. The next cards played must be the same color, unless a player doesn't have one (in which case any card must be used). The player with the largest gummy bear of the dominant color takes the trick. Players score 1 point for every card taken, as well as bonus and penalty points listed on special cards. The trick taker choses the first card to play for the next trick. Special cards may not be played first in a trick. If a player beginning a trick has only a special card left to play, the trick is void and he or she is assessed a -5 point penalty.

When all tricks for the hand have been played, the points scored by each player should be tallied and recorded on paper. Several hands are to be played to allow points to accumulate.

The first player to score 100 points, wins!

boardgame: Hitler Strikes North: The 1940 Invasion of Norway
description:
Hitler Strikes North is a remake and facelift of a classic game from the 1980's by Quarterdeck Games, and licensed to Hobby Japan. Many of the changes from the original reflect research I have conducted since the original game design and are reflected from our co-authored book Hitler Strikes North. Errors have also been corrected. The game map shows portions of the North and Norwegian Seas.

In April, 1940 World War II was locked in a "Sitzkrieg" - a phony war. There were naval activities and limited air operations, but the Allied Armies faced Germany on her border where little was going on beyond training and planning. Attention was drawn north to Norway because of vital Swedish high-grade iron ore resources feeding both side's weapons industry through the ice-free port of Narvik and brought further into focus with the Russo-Finnish "Winter War" that had just concluded. Great Britain, with ally France, had a preponderance of ships and naval support, even if the Kriegsmarine (German Navy) was more modern. Britain was on the defensive, though she had advanced plans for invading Norway to cut off the supply of iron ore going to Germany. But the British did not know when or with what that the Germans might try to break raiding warships out into the Atlantic to attack her vital convoy routes, or marshal an attempt to overwhelm a tragically weak and under armed Norway.

Hitler Strikes North allows you to explore the various possibilities of this highly charged strategic situation.
Can you break through the vaunted Royal Navy to unleash your raiders?
Can you successfully use all the elements of modern war - air, sea, and land to swiftly subjugate an entire nation in the world's first modern "Joint" operations?
Can you, as the Allies, respond to the German thrust with your forces to stop the German objectives?
Hitler Strikes North not only has these historical scenarios but also includes optional and hypothetical units, including the Danish navy, that allow you to play alternative plans and operations.

—description from the publisher

description:
Nom Nom Yum! is the deliciously fun card game in which you try to collect an impressive stash of 10 yummy food items, while strategically interrupting the progress of your opponents!

How To Play
Shuffle the deck and deal 5 cards to each player. Set the remaining cards between all players, face down, as the game deck. Directly beside the deck shall be a singular discard pile. The player to the dealer's left has first turn.

For every turn, the player draws up to 6 cards (the hand limit) and plays 1 card face-up into his or her personal "food stash" (a double decker set of 10 card slots) on the playing surface. If the player has more than one of the same food in-hand, he or she may play any or all of them during a single turn- the only way to play multiple cards in a turn. Cards may not be picked up from the discard pile.

When a card is played, if an opponent has the food item in his or her stash, that player must discard 1 of them. For example, if a player puts the "Pizza" card into his or her stash, any opponent who has that card must discard it.

If the game deck exhausts at any point, shuffle and reuse the discard pile. Play continues clockwise.

The first player to create a full food stash of 10 cards, in total, wins!

Deadly Concoctions is the lethal card game in which you mix heart-stopping, madness- inducing, soul-crushing potions, in an effort to kill your opponents.

How To Play
Separate the "Life" cards (Mind, Heart, Spirit) from the deck and give one of each to all players, who are to set them face-up on the playing surface.

Shuffle the deck and deal each player 6 cards. Set the deck face down between all participants. The player left of the dealer takes the first turn.

For each turn, a player draws up to the hand limit of 7 cards [from any combination of atop the deck or discard field), and discards 1 card into an open field of cards on the playing surface (concluding the turn). If, prior to discarding, a player matches a "Potion" card with the 2 "Ingredient" cards shown on it, the 3 cards may be played on any opponent's Life card. When this occurs, set aside all 4 cards to remove them from play. If one loses all life cards, he or she is dead and out of the game. A player may attack only once per turn, and only the Life form targeted by a potion. "Secret Ingredient" cards are wild. Use them in place of ingredients needed to mix a potion. The "Serum X" card is a complete potion that, by itself, may attack any Life form.

The sole surviving player, wins!

Как стать Жучиной (How to become a Bug) is about the best bug-scout academy graduates, who are trying to pass their final examination in a wilderness camp. Tutors set the difficult task for them: the young bug-scouts should search the wilderness and find the firewood for the alarm bonfires. The squad who lit their bonfire first would be the winners. The problem is while searching for the wood all the colourful bug-scouts got all abroad. And now every beetle claims about what bonfire should be lit first. To work out the winner fair and square bug-scouts settled the distinct rules of lightning the bonfires. Meanwhile the goal remains the same: whoever light his alarm bonfire is the winner!

The game consists of 6 decks consisting of the identical cards, but with different images on the back of the card, 6 "bonfire" cards representing goals and 30 small "ember" cards to count victory points. During game players place his or her cards in the shared stacks face down. When every player put 3 of his cards on the table, the stacks are revealed, and the bugs are counted to match certain color-combination printed on one of the "bonfire" cards. Whoever have this bonfire card gets victory points for the round. 3 kind of active cards affect other cards in its stack to add a strategic component to the game.

boardgame: Combat Patrol: World War II – Supplement for the South Pacific.
description:
Provides a comprehensive set of rules for skirmishes set in World War II. This supplement provides guidance for adapting the rules to the unique conditions and tactics of fighting in the South Pacific. The Japanese began the campaign with elite forces at high morale. US Marine forces also began the campaign at a high level of training and morale, while with the exception of the 25th Infantry Division, the US Army began the campaign with little experience and poor training. Most of this changed after Guadalcanal, where elite Japanese forces and US Marines experienced heavy losses. By that time, US Army National Guard divisions had greatly improved. Throughout the fighting in the South Pacific the Australians were widely regarded as having provided the most proficient and effective infantry. Fighting in the South Pacific was not homogeneous.

Each campaign and island provided a unique set of circumstances. 
This supplement adds Japanese morale result tables, Banzai Charges, Night Infiltration, Die in Place, Hidden Japanese Positions, with a detailed vehicle and weapons list.

The requirement for new action decks for the Japanese is not required but makes the game easier.

Aprenda Jugando is a game where you can demonstrate what you know and increment your knowledge of Art, Natural science, Social science and Sports and share them with friends and family.

The objective of the game is manage to get all 6 rings, one of each color, answering questions right.

Tower Capture combines the strategy of checkers and chess with the luck involved in a quick roll of the dice. The idea of Tower Capture is to simply capture the tower of your opponent by moving across the board of white and black hexagons. However, the movement of your 10 pieces is partially controlled by the two dice, which determine whether you move to a white or a black space, and whether you move through a red, yellow, or blue path. To win, make it to the space adjacent to the opposing tower, and capture opponent's pieces along the way by landing on the same space.

—description from designer

The Invitation is a three to five player cooperative tabletop game where the players explore a derelict mansion and fight to avoid madness while completing the chosen scenario. Each scenario presents a plot, objective, and special rules for the gameplay. Certain scenarios introduce special mechanics that change the dynamic of the game. For example, the second scenario introduces a traitor mechanic in which one player secretly works against the rest of the players to accomplish his/her own goals.

The exploration of the house is based on a randomly ordered deck of Room Tiles, so each play through is different in how the house is laid out. Items, enemies, and events are also encountered in a random order, which also serves to differentiate each play through and adds to the replayability.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
This can be played either as a memory game where players turn over cards to match identical pictures of lovable and friendly monsters and the player with the most cards wins. 
It can also be played as a card game where like crazy-eights where you are collecting sets of matched cards.

For 1 or More Little Monsters Ages 4 and Up.  Contains 72 Match-up Monster Cards.  No reading required.

boardgame: Enso Koi: King of the Pond
description:
Navigate your school of Koi Fish across the Koi Pond to dominate the pond!

Enso Koi is a game of decisiveness, deep strategy, and introspection.

2-6 Players with a 1 player-skirmish mode. 20-40+ minute game duration.

Players go head to head or in teams to out maneuver the other Koi fish.

Uses stones to blockade sections of the pond, and bring koi fish back to play by catching Lotus flowers.
Enso Koi is easy to learn but takes generations to master! (( Just ask the fish ))

This strategy game is best played with a nice fresh cup of tea.

Intellector - chess XXI century

It is similar in many respects to the ancient "game of Zion." But Intellector more than classical chess - is a completely new abstract puzzle game:

Each of the pieces has a move that is much like pieces in chess, in terms of the 'feel' of the game, but with the hexagon board it will offer unique features to explore.

The game is played on a hex board of 59 spaces.

The goal of the game is to capture the Intellector pieces, again think of it as the king piece.
   
    Agressor - first striker; can be moved only in the dark hexagon located on a straight line connecting the vertices (corners) of dark hexagons.

Progressor - moving forward;walks straight ahead (through the line) to an adjacent hexagon, that is, only those of the hexagons, which are closer to the "baseline" of the enemy in relation to the hexagon in which it is located.

The Liberator - goes through one of the hexagons, which adjoins the faces of its hex

Defenssor - defender;  goes to any of the adjacent hexagons, which adjoins the faces of its hex.

    Dominator - can be moved to any of the hexagons located in the direction of forming a series of hexagons contiguous faces.
 Intellector - smart, literally from the Latin word "understanding." It goes on any neighboring hexagon, which is in contact with the face through the hexagon, in which it is located

Scrollhammer is an "open source" published set of miniatures rules that derives many concepts from Setting: Warhammer Fantasy Wargames while using a setting derived from the Elder Scrolls media franchise.  The game was developed on a public forum and accepted input from users through two major revisions until a final publication date.

Play uses many methods common to traditional wargaming.  Movement and distances are presented in "inches" and determined with the use of a tape measure.  Modified six-sided dice rolls are used to determine many outcomes.  Various arena tokens are used to denote alterations to the battlefield that occur during play.

Each player controls the units of an opposing force.  Forces are built using a points pool.  As these calculations can become rapidly tedious, numerous armies and forces are provided by a variety of forum users.

Each unit in each force is represented by a model that is placed on the play surface.  Models have a paper record that tracks various statistics and play values such as speed, armor, ammunition, and so forth.  Units may make attacks on opposing units as long as they have the range, remaining ammunition or power, and line of sight.  Successful attacks deal damage that may destroy, disable, or partially disable targets.

Play proceeds in alternating player turns, each half-turn being sub-divided into movement, fire, and assault phases.  Victory conditions are set before the scenario begins and typically involve force destruction or "capture the flag" goals.  Rules disputes are resolved by a simple die throw.

—user summary

Scrollhammer 2nd Edition is a miniatures wargame with a setting derived from the Elder Scrolls media franchise.  It is notably distinct from Scrollhammer because Scrollhammer 2nd Edition uses a completely original move and combat system.  The game has gone through repetitive playtest/revision cycles, leading to numerous (iterative) versions, each published as a static PDF from 2013 through 2017.

Scrollhammer 2nd Edition focuses primarily on squad-based skirmishing where opposing players control a force composed of individual units, each unit represented on the play surface by a model.  Each model has a corresponding data card.  Scenarios may use either pre-determined setup or a deployment zone method.

Models should conform to a 25mm standard; the play surface is assumed to be flat (terrain is indicated but not created).  Distance and movement are performed in "inches", determined by tape measure.

Each turn is divided into two half-turns, one for each player.  Initiative determines which player acts first.  During each turn, every model may take two Actions (during the controlling player phase) and take one Reaction (during the opposing player phase).  Attacks may be made on any other model that is in range and which is in "line of sight" of the attacker.  Attacks and other opposed situations are resolved by a modified six-sided die roll.  Attacks may destroy, disable, or partially disable the target.

Special rules cover command influence, cover, missile and thrown weapons, dual-wielding combat weapons, blast attacks, and so forth.  The game utilizes a magic system as an organic component; magical effects are circumscribed and are "low powered" in scope.

Scenarios use a pre-determined victory condition and play proceeds until the condition is achieved.  Many pre-built units are presented in the rules (custom unit creation is supported but complex).

—user summary

Sacremento is a redevelopment of Take five by the same author. Like its predecessor, the game is played on a 6×6 board with 4 chess pieces: two bishops, a knight, and a rook. However, these tiles are printed with playing card values: aces, 3 royals, and the numbers 10–7 in four suits. Play proceeds just like Take Five, but the goal is to build the best poker hand.

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from FC Augsburg, a soccer club in Bavaria, Germany.

Before the game starts, all players receive a “fish” tile which indicates the color of their fish. Then the players each receive a “coral” card which they place, coral side up, in front of them and which will serve as the starting point for their school of fish.

Shuffle the rest of the cards and divide them into four approximately equal stacks, fish bone side up. Turn over the top card of each of the stacks and place it in front of its stack.

Throughout the game, the players will each form a school of fish in front of them. They must all try to ensure that at the end of the game, their own color of fish is in the majority

On a player's turn they will choose two cards from among the four visible cards from the stacks. Then they will place one of them in their school of fish and the other in an opponent’s school. At the end of their turn they will flip over two cards from the stacks so that there are always four cards visible.

There are different kinds of cards - coral, fish, octopus, and shark - which are placed adjacently (not diagonally) to one or more cards with additional rules depending on the type of card.

The game ends when three of the stacks are finished. Players then each count their points:
  
1 point for each fish of their color present in their school as well as in their opponents’ schools.
3 points if in their own school of fish they have a majority of their own color of fish.
1 point for each octopus in their school.
2 points for the player with the most sharks in his school of fish

The player who has earned the most points wins the game. In case of a tie, to determine the winner: the one with the most fish in his school wins the game.

In every city, there is one witch who's the most popular, most powerful and above all the most wicked. Wickedest of Them All pits 2-4 players against each other as they take on the roles of some of the most ruthless modern witches of all time, each one with one goal, to be the WICKEDEST OF THEM ALL!

Players choose a witch and draw cards. Their goal is to see which character has the highest wicked rank by the end of the game. Each witch has special powers and three attributes. Magic, Wicked, and Status. Status is used to play minions and magic is used to cast spells.

Actions: a Player can perform one action on their turn. They may summon a minion, use a character action, cast a spell, or have a minion attack. Playing equipment or minions do not count as actions and can be played at no "cost" during a turn.  A character can have up to 2 pieces of equipment in play.

Spells
Each spell has a number magic rank, in order for a witch to use a spell her magic rank must be equal to or higher than the spells magic rank.

Status and minions
A witch can call forth a minion in her player's hand if the witch has an equal or higher status than the minion.

Spells and minions as actions
Can be cast to affect the game, cast on minions or victims or cast on other spells as actions. Minions have an attack rank and can damage other minions and victims doing damage to their target.

Gaining wicked: Some spells grant bonuses to wicked as do destroying victims and minions. All minions are worth one wicked and victims are worth an amount of wicked equal to their "goodness rank".

End Game.
When the deck is depleted, the player whose character has the highest wicked wins.

From the game instructions: The object of this game is for the players, using dice and playing pieces, to travel on the gameboard from a residence square to the bank and shopping locations shown on their shopping lists. The first player to return to his residence with the travel cards for all locations contained on his shopping list wins the game!

A copy of Wing Leader: Supremacy 1943-1945 is required to play Wing Leader: Eagles.


(From GMT's website:)

Wing Leader: Eagles is the second expansion for GMT’s Wing Leader system. Eagles adds more aircraft, and more thrilling scenarios from the late war. Fly Hs 129 tank busters against the Soviet armored spearheads. Lead Ki-45 bomber-killers attacking B-29s over mainland Japan. Direct Coastal Command Mosquitos to strafe Luftwaffe airfields in Norway. Launch Me 163 rocket fighters against swarms of B-17s.

New Aircraft will also include the German Me 210 and He 177, the Soviet Yak-3, Yak-9U and La-7, the Japanese Ki-44 and Ki-100 fighters, as well as key late-war Swedish aircraft, including the J 22, B 17 and B 18. Counters for Finnish and Hungarian fighters make their debut in the game.

In addition to new aircraft and scenarios, Wing Leader: Eagles features a new campaign game, which recreates the 5th Air Force campaign against Rabaul in late 1943. The campaign comes with its own 11 x 17” campaign map, and will confront the American and Japanese players with tough decisions on targeting, force planning and raid execution in the lead-up to the invasion of Bougainville.

Wing Leader: Eagles brings the major air combats of the late war right to your gaming table!

description:
A balanced strategy game with attitude that's designed to get your drinking friends into card games and your card game friends drunk.

Hammered Heroes is a kickass battle card game that is great for any gaming night. Why waste time with friends playing silly games like quarters or king's cup, when you could play something with a little more brains, and a lot more treachery?

Hammered Heroes is a great way to introduce new friends to tabletop, and the initiated will find plenty to love as well.

Your numbered HAMMERED HEROES compete with your opponents' numbered heroes, for QUEST CARDS. CHEAP SHOTS are used by players to disrupt your opponents' plans, or increase your chances of winning.

After FIVE ROUNDS, the player with the most QUEST CARD POINTS wins. But be wary, CURSES and PARTY FOULS abound!

—description from the publisher

Tali is the historic Roman version of Knucklebones. Featuring the tactile feel of simulated Bones and upgradeable to metal historically based coins.  Helping bring History back to life.  Tali, recreates the knucklebones game of gambling as the Roman's used to play it.  Two games in one, Tali includes the historic rules played 1000 years ago as well as a micro role-playing game version with more player options and complexity.

Knucklebones is the ancestor to dice, before there were dice, the knuckle bones of sheep (or sometimes goats) would be used as it had a regular shape of four possible sides to land on.

The Romans would sometimes mark the knuckle bones with different roman numerals on each side, I,III, IV and VI.  The roman numerals would then be used to score the throw of the bones.

Players ante into the game similar to modern gambling games.  Once to play and then for each round.  Every round each player will get to throw the bones.  You score when getting at least one VI.  The player who achieves either a Venus or the highest total will win the round and the coins in the round pot.

The Venus throw will win the game pot and the round pot (each face, I, II, IV, and VI).

A micro role-playing game has been added to the historic base game, in which each player selects a historic character from 1000 AD to play.  Each character is given a rich background and a set of powers / capabilities to help them win in Tali.

Are you feeling lucky?  Are the gods with you tonight?

boardgame: Batman Miniature Game (Second Edition): The Book of Magic
description:
The Batman Miniature Game is a tabletop skirmish game featuring the powerful heroes and evil villains of the DC universe.
Will you join the Dark Knight against the mobsters who terrorize Gotham? 
Or will you join his enemies to bring chaos to the streets and take back the night?

The Book Of Magic contains all the rules required to include Magic in your games of the 2nd edition Batman Miniatures Game. Includes 24 spell card.

A copy of the Main 2nd edition Batman miniatures game is required to use this expansion ruleset.

As a member of Congress, your sworn duty is to serve your constituents. Serve them well and they may re-elect you for another term. But a higher prize awaits: the presidency of the United States! The American government is built upon a system of checks and balances. Things get done only when everyone works together. At the same time, only one can rise to the highest office in the U.S. Will it be you? Only time - and the American people - will decide! God bless America!

In Congress: The Board Game, you and your fellow senators compete to earn the most nomination points (“NP”) in order to secure the nomination for the U.S. presidency. This is accomplished by passing bills that reflect your interests and agendas over three terms. You must also support the interests of your state’s residents or risk defeat in your midterm elections, harming your chances in the presidential race. The game is over at the end of the third term and the winner is the player with the most NP.

Each session consists of four phases:

Committee Phase - players bid for 'speakership' and select a bill.

Amendment Phase - everyone takes turns adding riders and/or playing action cards.

Voting Phase - the bill is either voted through in simple majority or killed.

Election Phase - if this is the final session of the term, players receive campaign donations and roll for their election.

After the third term and final election, secret agendas are revealed and NP is counted. The most NP wins, and in case of a tie, richest senator wins.

—description from the publisher

description:
Highways & Byways is a casual family game - great for board gaming couples and families! Race across the United States in your first used car. You're on a mission to explore all places beautiful and forgotten before your vacation is over.

Start the game by picking a Start Space and a Vehicle. Each Vehicle gives you different abilities, such as moving a little faster under certain conditions or immunity to negative events. Plan your route by choosing byways. Then travel across the country a little bit every day, planning around highway construction, moody weather, and your car's pesky tendency to break down!

The first player to travel all their Byways and circle back to their Start Space is the winner. Each player's route is determined by Byway Cards, which are drafted at the beginning of the game. After routes are chosen, players receive a hand of five Event Cards, one of which is drawn by the clockwise player right before the player can move. Whatever is written on that Event Card - good or bad - happens to the player who is about to move. Players can then travel up to six spaces per turn, trading in each unmoved space for the ability to discard an unwanted Event Card and draw a replacement.

Each time the game comes back around to player 1, a new Construction Card is flipped. Construction Cards block off some of the fastest roads in the game. After five Construction Cards are drawn, players pass their Event Cards.

description:
Escape The Fest! (ETF!) is a multiplayer set collection card game for 2-6 players (13 and up) and takes 30-60 minutes to play.  In ETF! players (attendees) take turns trying to complete objectives (responsibilities) in order to leave the festival and not lose themselves in it. The first attendee to take care of all their responsibilities or with the most points at the end of the game is the winner.

Each round (day) brings new rules to observe. Some of the other attendees may be looking for the same responsibilities as you making it harder for you to take care of them. Other attendees may just continue to remind you of how many other responsibilities you have, causing you to stay on the festival grounds longer. You may even make some drastic choices that can help or harm you on your journey. All these things will ensure that no two festivals will feel the same.

—description from the designer

»Hot Six« … is a trick-taking game, where you try to guess your tricks. This might seem traditional, but the deck is quite innovative: All the suits are made up of a different amount of cards. Additionally, you might want to avoid the »hot SIX«.

Deal all the cards and reveal a new trump-suit for this round. Then, a game round consists of 3 phases:
a) Card swapping: Everybody swaps a card with their right and left neighbour.
b) Betting: Count to three. Then everybody indicates with their fingers, how many tricks they want to make this round.
c) Playing: One player leads a trick, the others have to follow suit.

After the last trick, add up the scores for every player:

+ 1 point per trick

+10 points, if you won your bet
-6 points, if you have the »hot 6«
When a player has 50 or more points, the game ends and the player with the most points wins.
NOTE: You'll find a 2 player-variant here on BGG.

–––––––––––––––– GERMAN –––––––––––––––––––

Heiße 6 … ist ein Kartenspiel, bei dem man seine Stiche voraussagen möchte. Dies wird etwas erschwert durch die Tatsache, dass alle Spielfarben eine unterschiedliche Anzahl an Karten haben. Außerdem gilt es die »heiße 6« zu vermeiden.
Eine 2-Spieler-Variante findet sich hier auf BGG.

–––––––––––––––– FRENCH –––––––––––––––––––

Hot 6 est un jeu de plis, où vous essayez de deviner les plis que vous allez remporter. Cela peut sembler traditionnel, mais le jeu est assez innovant : toutes les couleurs de cartes ont un nombre différent de cartes. De plus, vous devez essayer d‘éviter d‘avoir le „hot 6“.

–––––––––––––––– DUTCH ––––––––––––––––––––

Hot 6 is een slagenspel waarbij je het aantal slagen dat je gaat halen probeert te voorspellen. Dit lijkt misschien op een traditioneel slagenspel, maar het kaartendeck is innovatief: alle kleuren hebben een verschillend aantal kaarten.

boardgame: Mistborn: House War – Promo Card Pack 1
description:
Add even more intrigue with these five promotional cards for use with Mistborn: House War!

This set of promo cards includes:
“The Stallion” card: a very confused kandra whose antics can transform Favor into Disgrace

2 Terris Stewards cards whose efforts yield extra Favor for those who collaborate with their peers

2 Augur cards whose Allomancy allows players to reset their resources at an opportune time.

NOTE: Mistborn: House War Promo Card Pack 1 is already bundled in every copy of the Mistborn: House War Prestige Edition; if you’ve purchased the Prestige Edition, you do not need to also purchase this product.

Classic charades game in a tin.

Split your group into 2 teams.  Each team selects a guesser (everyone gets a turn guessing) and the rest of the teams acts out the:

- Action
- Song
- TV Show
- Film
- Landmark
- Event
- Book

Use the timer provided to get through as many charades within the two minutes as possible.  Teams take it in turns to act, the non acting team must keep score of how many correct answers the guesser obtains.  After every person on both teams has guessed the winner is the team with the most correct guesses/points.

Not a peep among the team members acting.   You lose a point if you speak or "mouth" any words to each other or the guesser.

Use of objects (or even people) is definitely permitted.  You may use generic charades actions, but remember you're against the clock so you may prefer to dive straight in...

Ready, Set, Go!

Source:  Game instructions.

In Act of Treason, players take on the role of Royal Advisors tasked with ruling a Kingdom in the wake of the Kings death. Players are given a secret loyalty card, assigning them as a Traitor, Loyal, or the Heir in hiding. Loyals win by rooting out and eliminating the Traitors, or passing five Quests to safely herald in the new King. Traitors win when four Quests fail, or by finding and assassinating the Heir. Players will scheme, deceive, and persuade in order to complete their objectives.

Each round, players contribute Knowledge cards in secret towards a Quest. Knowledge that matches the colour of the Quest will increase the chance of it passing, whilst off-colour cards increases the chance of failure. Players may vie for powerful Court cards, plot assassinations, or simply pass their action while they garner information. Play continues until the Loyals win and the Heir is safe, or the Traitors win and the Kingdom is left to ruin. As the game progresses, tensions will rise. Players will resort to accusations and assassinations in a last ditch effort to save the Kingdom, or force its destruction.

Act of Treason was inspired by social deduction games like Mafia, Werewolf, and Battlestar Galactica. It takes many aspects of these games and seeks to amplify what makes them so much fun: the social interaction, suspicion and deception! In Act of Treason, the only thing that stands between you and victory is your ability to read, bluff, and persuade others.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
In post-World War II Germany the scramble is on to mete out the fate to the Reich's most brilliant -and infamous- technical minds. Play as the Americans or Soviets racing to assemble the superior scientific team.  Or you can play as the Nakam vigilante group hunting down the worst perpetrators of the war. Lastly, you can play as an ODESSA Network operative trying to smuggle high value German officers to safety in South America.

Players take simultaneous turns in a frenetic division of characters that have a range of values, special powers, and bestow  infamy tokens. That infamy has to be carefully managed as it can be important information that boost your score, but too much much infamy will tank your score.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to the near future. Welcome to Spillover, the first expansion for Peak Oil.

Spillover enriches and diversifies Peak Oil by adding variant cards, which change the fundamental rules of the game, and new Demand, Risk, and Security markers. You need the base game to play.

The variant cards introduce new ways to play Peak Oil. They're divided into the categories General, Agents, PR, Endgame, and Scoring, depending on the aspect of the game that they affect. In each game, you can use up to one from each of the five categories, allowing for up to 42 millions of different combinations (made up number).

The new cardboard markers can be used with or without the variant cards. There are new refineries to ship oil to, new dangers to be found on the shipping routes and new Security markers.

Actor. Plot. Set.  Combine one of each to make a movie.  Did someone have a better idea than you?  Don't worry, you can stab them in the back and take all their points if you "know people" in the Industry.  After all, it's showbiz.

What The Film?! is a fun party game where you get to come up with crazy movie ideas based on the hand you're dealt. On a player's turn from their hand, a player lays down an actor, set, and plot card. With 300 cards the mashups are endless. Not only are you competing with your friends, you are also fighting the clock. After cards have been played, you have exactly 36 seconds to pitch your movie magic to the table.

Voting is done simultaneously, so you don't have the time to persuade anyone at the table, no one like beggars in the film industry! On the count of three, everyone hands their colored award token to who they thought had the best movie. The player with the most points keeps their cards as points, everyone else discards the cards they played.

We know not everyone is a director at heart, for that reason we added Industry Cards. There are two types of Play Immediately's and Play and Discard and contain good and bad things that can occur on your turn or someone else's. For instance, one card can have you stealing the rounds points or another, causing you to pay union dues, which cause you to lose cards.

After six rounds, the person with the most points is declared the winner, and just like that, you're ready to receive your director of the year award!

In Reliquium each player takes control of one of four warring factions: Chaos Legion, Alliance of Nature, Order of Light and Mountain Brotherhood in struggle to achieve uncontested domination. Path to victory lies through conquering new lands and getting access to more resources, managing troops, and calculated use of legendary warriors from Bestiary.

Players start with 3 castles already built (one of each type). 
Each castle gives player an action based on type of territory it occupies:
Plains - march or issue attack order
Mountain - recruit or issue support order
Forest - build a castle or summon legendary creature.

Players resolve actions one at a time, cycling through all players until no more actions left. After all players are done then combat resolves. Afterwards, play continues to the next round.

The player who manages to build all 9 castles first wins the game.

It's time to play, Star Wars Family Feud! Are you ready to take on the challenge? Survey says, yes! One of the most popular games in the world now has a twist. The entire family can experience the fun of Family Feud as they never have before through the magic of one of the greatest movie franchises of all time, Star Wars.

Based on a national survey, Cardinal Games placed the top answers on 150 Face Off cards. Figure out the most popular answers and rack up the points. Face off in three rounds, then play the Fast Money Bonus Round with 50 Fast Money cards. The team or player with the highest score wins and is crowned the Star Wars Family Feud champion.

So get ready to test your Star Wars knowledge, and may the Force be with you!

boardgame: A Wrinkle In Time: A Daring Adventure Game
description:
A Wrinkle In Time: Daring Adventure Game is a cooperative dice game that captures the journey of the 2018 theatrical adaptation of the Madeline L'Engle novel.

In the game, players take on the role of the story's main characters — Meg, Charles Wallace and Calvin — and work together to solve mathematical puzzles, decode secret messages, and tesser to new locations to rescue Mr. Murry and get home. The engineering of a hexaflexagon is behind the "Flex Enfolders", which when used in conjunction with dice rolls must sum up to progress through five movie-based locations, or levels.

A coding portion of each round relies on players' abilities to communicate and lay out an "operation chain" that aims to generate a successful result, after which they will use the color filtration of Mrs. Who's "Quizzing Glasses" to reveal Mr. Murry's hidden whereabouts.

Rune Society is a unique and fast-paced strategic card game set in a world of forbidden magic.  In Rune Society you will take an 18 card deck and face off against friend or foe in a variety of tournament style competitive formats.

Rune Society is designed to have the strategy and versatility of a collectible trading card game without the need to chase down rare and expensive cards.

One copy of Rune Society is enough for up to 4 players to each build unique decks and compete against one another in a variety of tournament-style formats.

Players can take those same 4 decks and face an elite Nemesis from the "Rune Society" Universe with the purchase of a Nemesis Deck expansion pack.

Fans of strategic card game tournament play can also combine multiple decks for even more ways to play!

Magik Realm is a game designed to recreate the experience of the AH classic Magic Realm with newer and simpler game mechanics.  It is a completely separate game with its own rules and components.  The biggest differences are not needing to choose goals (Victory points are scored) and not needing to record a plan for each day’s actions.

Players choose one or more characters to play and move those characters through the game area of clearings on a 4 x 4 grid of tiles.  They will fight and avoid monsters that appear on the map, look for treasure sites and the treasures therein, meet with natives to buy and sell, hire muscle, and take on quests.  They gain Victory Points by discovering treasure sites, treasure, killing monsters, fulfilling quests, and learning new spells.

The game lasts thirty days.  Characters can perform a certain number of actions each day based on their current health.  The actions include: Move, Sneak, Search, Parlay, Rest, Magic, and Combat.  Every two days, an Occurrence chart is used to randomly determine which monsters and natives appear on the map and which ones leave.  At the end of the thirty days, characters add up the Victory Points they scored and a winner is determined.

—description from the designer

The invaders are coming! Hordes of nasty pests are threatening to invade your country. They can sneak in on container ships or even in the baggage of air passengers. Once here, they will secretly grow and spread, damaging your crops and wilderness areas.

Fortunately you and your biosecurity team have the skills to stop them in their tracks! Intercept them before they arrive, or find them quickly and eradicate. Can you bust every invasion?

The Invasion Busters board depicts a simplified version of a real biosecurity system (based on that of New Zealand). A deck of cards represents invasive species which may arrive, grow and spread. Players co-operate to allocate dice to different biosecurity activities, then roll the dice to see if their actions have been successful. At the end of the game points are gained for the species successfully excluded or eradicated, and points are lost for any species which have become established. Players compare their score to a scale printed in the rulebook to see how well they did.

Invasion Busters was developed specifically for use in the classroom, to help teach concepts of invasion, biosecurity and population dynamics to 8-12 year olds, but has also been enjoyed by their teachers and other grown up invasion busters.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: In 1937, Walt Disney fulfilled a dream and created the world’s first fully animated feature film: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs. Now, over 50 films later, celebrate Disney Animation in this collector’s edition Monopoly game. From Pinocchio and Peter Pan to The Little Mermaid and Frozen, players can imagine and recall the beloved stories, magical characters, and unforgettable family memories. In this edition, players move around the Disney-themed gameboard, building hotels and houses, buying Disney Movies, Vehicles, Magical Objects, and creating new Disney family memories.

Pixies VS Gremlins is a light-hearted strategy card game. The objective is to control enough magic rune stones to summon the magnificent and fearsome Wolpertinger! To do that, players choose a faction and send out their minions! Once a player has enough strength to capture a rune stone, its theirs.

But things are seldom so easy. Players will challenge each other by engaging in combat, unleashing mischievous spells, wicked traps and powerful gadgets.

The primary game objective is to capture more rune location cards than your opponents. To capture these cards, each player must choose and deploy some of their minions, which are acquired from their faction deck.

—description from the publisher

Hold onto your overalls! Join Gru and his family, includes his Minions in the Despicable Me UNO!

Play the Mutated Minion card make things a little wild.

And don't forget to yell "UNO!" before you play your last card!

The game is pretty much identical to traditional UNO except there are two special Evil Minion rule cards and two customizable wild cards. Watch for the Evil Minion wild card; draw that card and call out a color. The next player must keep drawing until that color card turns up.
family: Versions & Editions: Versions with IP after the original release

description:
Downeast Dealer is a fast-paced card game of strategy for 3 to 5 players based on the old Yankee obsession with finding a bargain. Players run auctions, make bids on collectable items, and challenge other players to deals.

Everyone begins with cash and a few bad checks. Play begins with the auction of an item you might find in a classified section of the newspaper, on Craigslist, or in the uniquely Maine publication, “Uncle Henry’s.” Players take turns auctioning these Classified Items to each other and bidding on items to add to their own collection.

Each Classified Item has a point value. The most sought after Classified Item, the Moose Trophy, is worth 100 points. Lesser items, like the Blueberry Rake, are worth as little as 5 points.
A player only earns the points indicated on a Classified Item when they collect the complete set of four cards. When two players each hold the same Classified Item card, one player may “Downeast Deal” to try to capture the set.

Downeast Deals are when bluffing becomes a major element of the game. Players might offer large sums of cash in a Downeast Deal, or they might just throw down a bunch of bad checks. There’s no telling until the deal is done – and the savviest dealer takes the collection (or perhaps a large pile of cash!).

Play continues with auctions and deals until all of the sets are complete. The final scoring depends on the point value of the cards a player has – and the number of sets the player has. Having a moose trophy to hang on the wall is cool – but having a fly-fishing rod in your hall AND a lobster trap in your living room AND an anchor in your cellar is even cooler. Right?

Of course, collectors always run the risk of crossing paths with the village ne’er-do-well, and Downeast Dealer is no different. Keep your collection safe from the Thief. If you don’t, he may run off with your prized heirloom chest!

boardgame: Exodus Trading Card Game: Crystal Forge
description:
Crystal Forge is the 3rd set in the Exodus TCG.

Set Description:

Guild Wars on the Horizon: 
Ever since the galaxy-sized nebula creatures collided in the skies over Eeventide, there's been a surplus of stardust & other magical compounds that rained down during the aftermath. This excess supply has led to Drifters far and wide forging new creatures. The emergence of 2 new Guilds, Treeforge & Shardveil, brings all to the brink of war.

Shardveil, their ice not limited to any part of the color spectrum, seeks to bring a halt to the chaos by permanently freezing the world. Treeforge and their vast forests of living trees & mushrooms fight to uproot the spreading ice. Which Guild do you choose?

A Competitive Expansion (Set 3) Crystal Forge:
With new Rarity mechanics, new creature types, & new effects like Reveal and Freeze, the Crystal Forge expansion set introduces all-new cards with new themes & new artwork all jam-packed into 2 new decks that are ready-to-play & fully customizable! None of these cards can be found in other sets!

Details:

This set consists of 2 Competitive Decks. The Treeforge Competitive Deck revolves around Treefolk & Mushfolk and the Shardveil Competitive Deck features ice creatures of all colors, shapes and sizes called Shardfolk. These decks are geared for advanced players who are already familiar with the basics of the game and heavily feature type and rarity mechanics. The decks can be played standalone or customized, and many players purchase 2 of each deck to get a playset (x2 copies) of each card since the set contains 100% new cards. This set contains extremely powerful Ability creatures that combo well with their respective types, and both decks feature a completely blinged-out 20-card foil energy deck with new artwork and a new foil treatment.

Two new Guilds are given official recognition with this set, with the emergence of 2 new Guild Leaders. Oliver Rose leads the Treeforge Guild and Crystallus is the self-proclaimed leader of the Shardveil Guild.

Set Breakdown:

This set consists of 43 cards, including:
8 Rares
15 Uncommons
20 Commons
In addition to introducing new mechanics like freeze and reveal, Existence Games wanted to spoil players by giving them a full foil energy deck in these decks for no extra cost.

Creature Types:
This set introduces the following creature types:

Mushfolk
Storm
Shark
Phoenix

Fox in the Henhouse is a micro-game for 2-4 players that can be played in 10-20 minutes. Each player is trying to accumulate the most Eggs (points) from their Hens. The Fox is a tricky scoundrel that shows up in all the wrong places and chases off the Hens and destroys their Eggs! It’s your job to keep the Fox out of your Henhouse and keep your Hens safe so they can produce as many Eggs as possible.

In Fox in the Henhouse, each player has only 1 card in their hand and two face down cards in front of them. The two face down cards are called your Henhouse. Hen cards have 1-4 Eggs shown on them. You are attempting to gather the most Eggs into your hand and Henhouse by the end of the Week (7 rounds). In addition to each player's hand and Henhouse are two face down cards in the center of the play area called the Field. On your turn you can select a card from anywhere on the table (cannot take a card from someone's hand).  Once you have selected a card, you can choose which of the two cards you now hold to keep and which to put back, then other players take turns following the same format of selecting a card and placing a card.  If a player selects a Fox card they must keep it in their hand unless they are also holding one of the 4 Fox repelling cards (The Farmer, The Farmwife, The Farm Dog or The Rooster).  The 4 Fox repelling cards also have special abilities when played face up to help you manipulate the cards in play.  At the end of the Week all players total up their Eggs and take the number of Egg tokens they have accumulated.  After 4 weeks have been played then the player with the most Eggs wins!

Expand your mix and build your music collection with DropMix Discover Packs. Each pack includes 5 DropMix cards that can create a full song mix and more music mixing possibilities with the DropMix Music Gaming System (sold separately). There are 30 DropMix cards available to collect in each series featuring songs from top artists. Each pack of 5 sold separately.

Dropmix: Series 3 Discover Packs is a collection of 6 such packs. While the packaging of each pack is identical, they can be identified by the card showing through the front window of each pack.

Expand your mix and build your music collection with DropMix Discover Packs. Each pack includes 5 DropMix cards that can create a full song mix and more music mixing possibilities with the DropMix Music Gaming System (sold separately). There are 30 DropMix cards available to collect in each series featuring songs from top artists. Each pack of 5 sold separately.

Dropmix: Series 4 Discover Packs is a collection of 6 such packs. While the packaging of each pack is identical, they can be identified by the card showing through the front window of each pack.

boardgame: Too Many Bones: The Great Gathering Promo Card
description:
Too Many Bones promo card. 
Shuffle this card into the encounter deck and proceed as normal

Promo 001
It is a 'Special Encounter' card, The Great Gathering.

boardgame: Too Many Bones: Book of Secrets Promo Card
description:
Too Many Bones promo card.

Promo 004
It is a permanent 'Trove Loot' card. 3 Lever, 4 Trip, 5 Force.
Card reads, "After each Battle, you gain an additional Training Point."
Each player needs to collect six specific pieces on the wheel-shaped board. Roll and move.
A game about moving through the forest collecting trash. Roll-and-move.

In Vs System 2PCG, players choose a main character, build a deck with exactly sixty cards, and attempt to KO the other player's main character in a game of superhero battles.

Vs System 2PCG: Deadpool & Friends showcases 55 new cards that are Deadpool and Deadpool-related. Like his friends. Including Main Characters, Supporting Characters, Plot Twists, and Locations. Vs System 2PCG: Deadpool & Friends can be combined with any other Vs System 2PCG sets.

This is not a stand-alone game.

In VS System 2PCG, players choose a main character, build a deck with exactly sixty cards, and attempt to KO the other player's main character in a game of superhero battles.

VS System 2PCG: Brotherhood of Mutants introduces 55 new cards that focus on the rival of the X-Men! This set includes Main Characters, Supporting Characters, Plot Twists, and Locations. VS System 2PCG: Brotherhood of Mutants can be combined with any other VS System 2PCG sets. A single box contains a full play-set of all cards.

This is not a stand-alone game. A copy of Vs. System 2PCG: The Marvel Battles is required to play.

In Vs System 2PCG, players choose a main character, build a deck with exactly sixty cards, and attempt to KO the other player's main character in a game of superhero battles.

Vs System 2PCG: New Mutants showcases the cast of the nostalgic New Mutants comics and introduces new mechanics, Main Characters, Supporting Characters, Plot Twists, and Locations. Vs System 2PCG: SHIELD vs Hydra can be combined with any other Vs System 2PCG sets. A single box contains a full play-set of all cards.

This is not a stand-alone game. A copy of Vs. System 2PCG: The Marvel Battles is required to play.

This game's box cover and spectacularly illustrated board provide a thorough tour of WWI Germany's military-industrial complex, from a coal mine and iron and steel making through the production of massive artillery pieces and warships.

In play, it is a basic spin-and-move game, but the artwork by Johann B. Maier made for effective wartime propaganda at the time and remains today a vivid historical picture of the role of heavy industry during the Great War.

This is a mid-19th century party game subtitled I Hope I Don't Intrude, and takes its name from the popular comedy play Paul Pry, which premiered in 1825.

The game consists of a deck of 13 cards. Once card has the instructions, and list of 12 questions labelled A through L. Each letter corresponds to an answer card with possible responses numbered from 1 through 15.

One player has custody of the cards, and reads a question. The remaining players in turn pick a number from 1 to 15, and the player with the cards then reads off the corresponding answer.

For instance, to question L ("What most occupies your thoughts?"), potential answers range from "The fashions" and "My dinner" to "My own virtues" and "In building air castles". Presumably the answer generating the most laughs wins.

Snitch is a fast-paced game with social deduction and semi co-operative mechanics where players try to win the most money.

Each round one player selects the number of cards (characters) that must then be matched from the groups' cards (characters)- these are drawn from the deck and the players negotiate which cards (characters) they will contribute.

Each player contributes one card towards the pattern. If the players collectively complete the required pattern, everyone wins - if not, the group loses. Winners gain loot, the losers gain nothing - with the amount of loot determined by the number of cards matched. After 8 rounds, the winner is the player with the most loot.

There's always a chance though that one player was the Snitch - trying to make the group fail in order to win by themselves! The Snitch causes others to miss out on the loot, and steals some of their existing loot. Being the snitch at the right time is key to winning the game!

Players have a limited number of Snitch cards - when will you Snitch on your friends? When will they Snitch on you?! Everyone has to Snitch at some point...
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Pirate-themed expansion for Card Goblins, containing 15 cards. This expansion was released with the game during its crowdfunding campaign.

Shuffle these cards into the main deck and proceed as normal.

Tiberius Claudius Nero's father was lucky to escape execution by Octavian Caesar after the siege of Perusia in 40 BC. His parents took the infant Tiberius and fled from the blood-chilling vengeance of the future Emperor Augustus. As they made their perilous escape from Italy nobody gave a dried fig for the boy's prospects. So how could he possibly have become Augustus' heir and successor?

It is 11BC and the succession plans of Roman Emperor Caesar Augustus have suffered a calamity: his trusted friend, son-in-law and Heir-apparent, Marcus Vipsanius Agrippa, has died suddenly. There is a vacuum into which several talented men wish to step. Although they don’t yet know it, all of Augustus’s male heirs are also destined to die before him, and the man who succeeds in replacing Agrippa at Augustus’s right hand will ultimately become the Emperor of Rome, the most powerful man in the World.

The players take the roles of real Roman Noblemen, each having recently finished a term as Consul of Rome. This, plus ambition, qualifies them for the most prestigious jobs in the service of Augustus and the Empire.

Each of the 8 turns represents 3 years. In each turn Augustus asks the Nobles to do 6 jobs. The 6 jobs are depicted by Career Cards. The Nobles will use their influence with Augustus to get the first choice of the jobs. The jobs will help the Nobles in several areas that any Roman Noble must be seen to excel at, in order to rise above their fellows. They do this by placing cubes into the boxes of the main board as prescribed by the Career Cards. Those boxes include commanding Rome's Legions, Conquering new provinces, Governing the existing one, serving the gods of the State religion, helping Augustus in Rome, and hopefully being permitted to ride at Augustus' side through the Forum in a Triumph. Each Noble will try to marry into Augustus's family. And with the right collection of cubes a Noble can also be adopted as Augustus's Son.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Wings of War: World War 2 – Dewoitine D520
description:
Take your Wings of War game into the third dimension! Each historically-accurate, fully-painted miniature represents an aircraft from Wings of War: The Dawn of World War II. All miniatures come complete with maneuver deck and variable-altitude stand.

This French fighter aircraft, used largely for training purposes, saw limited combat.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Age of Empires: Expandable Card Game – Nautical Expansion
description: Age of Empires: Nautical is the only published expansion for the Age of Empires II ECG. Journeyman Press printed the expansion on April the 16th in 2001. The expansion comes with 160 cards and introduces the four (4) new civilizations - Franks, Japanese, Saracens, and Vikings; additionally, included are "Water Rules" and new scenarios.

You blatantly lied on your CV and got hired by the world's best Gear Moving Factory. It is your first day at the job and the boss is smelling something fishy about the new recruits. He places you in the Ultra Modern Testing Grounds to test your skills. The one who ends up the best will start working... for others, tough luck.

Pick up gear tiles as quickly as possible and try to make right connections and fill up the board... 1-4 players, virtually no setup time and 1-3 minutes per round.

During a game, each player picks up gear tiles from the common pool and adds them to their own board. Each tile must be connected (diagonally or orthogonally) to the engine tile via coloured squares. If a player successfully connects 3 goal tiles with the engine tile so that goal tiles turn in the right direction, he scores 3 points for each. Each empty space on the board is worth -1 point at the end of the game.

Players must be careful, invalid connections might make whole sections of the board invalid and empty before the scoring. No two tiles may be connected via different colours, and a tile connected with two tiles that turn in two different directions is also invalid.

A round lasts between 1 and 3 minutes (players choice, depends on skill). The player with the best score is the winner.

Players may also choose to use or not use both sides of the gear tiles that easily show which gear turns in which direction. Using both sides makes the game much easier and it is recommended for the first games.

Metro City Meltdown is a co-operative disaster management game for 1-4 players, which takes around 1 hour to play.
Taking place over 8 days, can you and your team protect and serve a city gone crazy and leave it whole at the end of your term? Sounds easy? Budget restraints, wild weather  and angry citizens all contribute to the city’s decline. 
Oh and don't get me started on the 100 foot tall dinosaurs or flying saucers...

The game takes place over 8 game days and players take the roles of city officials trying to protect the city from damage, rioting and disease.

Players use their city budgets to put crews into the city blocks to counter the news events that bring further disaster and ruin to the streets.

If you can survive to the end of your watch and have most of the city still up and running you win as a team. But lose too many city blocks or too many citizens to disease rioting or other dangers and you are fired and removed from office.

Each Player has a set of budget dice and there are federal budget dice which are divided among players according to the needs of the city.

Your budget can be assigned to actions to counter damage that takes place during the game. (Each round event cards dictate the headlines for that round and the damage that is caused). 
These actions could be to put out fires, heal the diseased, control unruly citizens repair infrastructure and reconnecting utilities. Be careful though you also need budget to move your work crews around the city.

If any of these issues get out of control they can spread very quickly. Losing 3 city blocks or all of the damage counters of any type results in instant dismissal from the City Disaster council and disgrace for your team.

Watch your HQ as well if that burns down or is damaged beyond repair the same result can take place.

With 4 difficulty levels this game can be a light entertaining game right through to nearly impossible odds. This might seem repressive but beating the game on Expert level is a great boost.
 Solo play can be played in the normal way but the Solo story mode involves scenarios that build upon each other.

boardgame: Star Wars: Angriff auf den Todesstern
description:
The rebels are planning an attack on the Death Star, the
ultimate weapon of the Empire. Of course, a fleet of enemy spaceships immediately appears to the rebels way. Help the rebels to defeat these spaceships to end up to finally reach the Death Star.

Galactically cool dice game in which the STAR WARS fans help the rebels to their "Death Star Attack".

—description from the publisher (translated)

Die Rebellen planen einen Angriff auf den Todesstern, die ultimative Waffe des Imperiums. Natürlich stellt sich den Rebellen sofort eine Flotte von gegnerischen Raumschiffen in den Weg.
Helft den Rebellen diese Raumschiffe zu besiegen, um am Ende bis zum Todesstern vorzudringen.

Galaktisch cooles Würfelspiel, bei dem die STAR WARS Fans den Rebellen bei ihrem "Angriff auf den Todesstern" helfen.

—description from the publisher (German)

boardgame: Million Dollars But... The Game: Community Pack
description:
Million Dollars, But... The Game Community Expansion Pack #1 comes with 100 new cards to either play standalone or combine with your core game to expand the fun.

75 new gold rule cards, 25 new black trigger cards, and one game rules card for standalone play. Each card is labeled with the community symbol for easy sorting.

The community cards feature the contributing community member's username, city, state, and country.

PAM is an educational game for younger children. A set of 12 unique illustrated 3x3 grids features images of things that are Plants (P) or Animals (A) or Minerals (M) and the players must match the images on the grid with a similar image on a randomly drawn card. The first child to match three images in a row on his or her 3x3 grid is declared the winner. The cards include an illustration plus a written description to enhance reading skills.

Includes grids, cards, score chips, and instruction sheet. Can be played solitaire. Adult supervision is not required for older children.

Ski the Mountain is simple to learn, fun to play and can be enjoyed by families and experienced gamers alike.  An exciting game that will give you a ski fix without leaving your sofa.

Ski the Mountain is a highly competitive card game for up to 4 players, perfect for après ski. Players collect runs attempting to be the first to ski various combinations  or visit mountain restaurants. Watch out though there are lots of obstacles and distractions that can heed or hinder your progress.

Game contains over 100 cards of 7 different type, with photographic illustrations of skiing and mountains.

The aim of the game is to maximize the points scored by collecting ski runs.  Each turn players can take a piste cards.   These represent blue, red or black ski runs and in combination or alone allow players to take a run card. Using these cards players collect ski runs that are shown on the piste map.  Collecting the right combination of ski runs maximizes the points players score.

Points are scored by:
1. Using piste cards, players take a run card, each run has a varying score.
2. Across the piste map there are targets to reach, these are illustrated cards of mountain restaurants or skiing features.  By collecting the right run card these targets can be completed to add to players point totals.
3. Each player has a Grand Tour card that accumulate points as specific runs are collected.
4. There are also combined special runs on the piste map, that players race to complete.  The add varying amounts to players point totals
5. Finally there are the highly prized Bonus cards that add points for being the first to collect 3 or 4 blue, red or black runs or visit mountain restaurants.

During the game players will also be given Event cards.  These cards depict skiing related events that give player advantages or can stop your opponent.  Either way they add lots of fun to the game.

The game is fast and highly competitive with lots of laughter as players block or hinder each other, as they race to Ski the Mountain.

boardgame: Heroes of Normandie: South Lancashire Regiment
description:
A fan-made 'Print & Play' punchboard that expands the Commonwealth Army of Heroes of Normandie. Made by Devil Pig's Games French forum moderator, GDuprez. Approved by Devil Pig Games' Yann and Clem as an 'official product'.


     one regular recruitment tile of the South Lancashire Regiment (3rd Infantry Division, 8th Infantry Brigade, 1st Battalion) with its recruitment options (Vickers Platoon, Fire Platoon, Bren Platoon).
     one High Command Recruitment Tile with Lt.Col. Gordon Duffin and his 'Artillery Spotter' ability.
     a few common Commonwealth units: a Daimler Armoured Car, a 6 Pounder AT Gun and a Sergeant NCO.
     a few other options 


______________________________

Quoting GDuprez, January 2018:
"As you know, during the past months the Pigs have been extremely busy (that’s putting it mildly): finalization of the card game KS as well as that of Age of Towers, add to that of course, the creation of Black Reach.

Meanwhile, since HoN and SoN were lying somewhat dormant, I tried to create units, tiles, markers and other options. Thanks to Clem and Yann who generously gave precious advice and granted access to the rich graphic library of Devil Pig, I have been equipped with both intel and means to embark on this adventure which is in all quite new to me."

Game covers battle of Węgrów, one of the most important skirmishes of January Uprising in the Russian Partition of Poland. It uses "Napoleon" system.

Battle took place February 3, 1863 near Węgrów in east Masovia. On January 22, Polish partisans, under the command of the Władysław Jabłonowski and Jan Matliński liberated a town from Russian forces. Here Poles established a military base, in a few days gathering almost 3,500 soldiers, mostly poorly armed, with about 500 scythe armed volunters, known as "Kosynierzy" (Scythemen).

Russians decided to recapture Węgrów with quick attack of the combined force forces of a 1,000-strong infantry unit with artillery, led by colonel Georgi Papaafanasopulo. Poles, threatened by encirclement, decided to retreat from the town. About 500 scythemen were ordered cover the Polish retreat. Scythemen attacked the Russian left flank and routed Russian cavalry squadron and cannons. Thanks to their action, biggest part of the Polish forces managed to left the town, but almost all attacking scythemen were killed by Russian infantry fire.

Game covers whole battle in three scenarios.

boardgame: British vs Pirates: Volume 2 – South Sea Alliance
description:
Full faction add-on introducing the South Sea Alliance faction to expand British Vs Pirates: Volume II with in addition to the French Navy and Buccaneers factions.
