description:
All Change! is one of several games playable with the Katarenga board and pieces. This game is invented by the author of Katarenga.

Players place their pieces on all the green spaces on their side of the board. Players move the pieces according to the Katarenga rules of movement. The winner is the one who is able to first occupy all the green spaces on the opposite side of the board.

In the variant Chameleon you can vary between colours of origin and destination. For example from all the blue spaces in your half to all the yellow spaces on the opposite side of the board.

Game based on Disney's movie and series Descendants.

Players must travel along the path and end up memorizing a spell to defeat "Maléfica".

4 kind of tests: drawing in the air, mirror mirror, skill with Tourney's ball, and mimicking.

Player throws the dice, falls in a place and must do what a card says, according the color of the "square" where they landed. If you fail, you must go back to the "square" you were coming from.

First to reach the last "square" and memorizing three random weird words that make a spell wins.

Thin-card covered booklet of rules for a campaign game based on the Aerial Dogfight Rules 1916-18 - to be used to follow a campaign for just a single squadron v squadron or involving multiple squadrons or 'airforces'.  It uses map movement for aircraft until combat occurs or an objective is reached where individual aircraft (presumes scale models but could use counters) then are placed on a table for tactical play. Most of the rules are concerned with expanding the dogfight game to a multi-day campaign using maps but some are for expanding the play on a table with aircraft to include bombing, AA fire, ground attack and balloon-busting.

11 pages of rules cover squadron types and sizes, pilot experience, wounds, campaign points, heavy bomber campaigns, navigation (optional), map movement, visibility, AA, bombs, ground attack, balloon-busting, replacements and repairs, weather (optional).

Rest of the booklet consists of 24 pages of aircraft charts (one aircraft per page), several sheets for orders, templates for a movement protractor, a wind indicator and counters.

Two map sheets are loose in the centre - one small map with a simplified (made-up) version of the Western Front and a larger one with SE England and Western Germany on opposing sides.

Left right forward, another goal!
Who will be the first to dribble his balls forward
and to reach the hoped for goals? .. Beware of the opponents,
who stop the run by a nasty foul play ..

A entertaining quick game with coasters. Fun for everyone!
 Game play: The running coasters are mixed well and then placed in any arrangement as a large playing soccer-field (always edge to edge, as a grid). The start- and goal-coaster should be layed out to the two ends of the soccer-field. Every player chooses his color and put his 3 balls onto the start coaster. The players has to roll a dice and move their balls into the goal. Each time the ball reaches a new field, the player has again 3 attempts with the dice. Note: Players on one same field can be tackled (foul-play) and the playing fields with the number 1 gives the chance to score with a fast 11-Meter-Tor (goal). The first player who has moved all their own balls into the goal, wins.

—description from the publisher (translated)

    


Links rechts vor, noch ein Tor!
Wer schafft es als Erster alle seine Bälle nach vorne zu dribbeln 
und die erhofften Tore zu erzielen? .. Vorsicht vor den Gegenspielern, 
die durch ein fieses Foulspiel den Lauf aufhalten wollen ..

Ein kurzweiliges schnelles Spiel mit Bierdeckeln. Spaß für jedermann!

—description from the publisher (German)

Spielablauf: Die Lauffelder werden gut gemischt und dann in beliebiger Anordnung als große Spielfläche (immer Kante an Kante, als Raster) ausgelegt. Das Start- und Torfeld werden dann an beiden Enden der Spielfläche passend angelegt. Jeder Spieler wählt eine Spielerfarbe und legt seine 3 Bälle auf das Startfeld. Die Spieler müssen einen Würfel werfen und ihre Bälle ins Tor bekommen. Jedes Mal, wenn der Ball ein neues Feld erreicht, hat der Spieler erneut 3 Versuche mit dem Würfel. Hinweis: Spieler auf einem Feld können sich foulen und die Spielfelder mit der Zahl 1 können ein schnelles 11-Meter-Tor ermöglichen. Der erste Spieler, der alle Bälle im Tor hat, gewinnt.

New playing element is introduced in the game. It will enrich naval battles and increase unexpectedness.

A Captain card is attached to the player's selected ship giving them special abilities pending on which captain card was played.

Contents: 4 Captain's Cards

Note: Originally created for Treasures of the Old Pirate (a lighter version of the game Admiral geared to ages 6+), the Captain's Cards can be used for Admiral, but an extra set would be needed beyond 3 players.

Dragons vs Unicorns is a 2 - 4 player deck building game.
This is a very fast paced deck builder, game time under 30mins.

Game play:

In Dragons vs Unicorns, you race against your opponents to gather reputation and hire Mercenaries from the Mercenary guild.

Each turn you will play out your hand, activate abilities, and draft cards into your deck. Place enchantments for powerful upgrades, send out your Mercenary meeples for more abilities, and destroy your opposition!

Keeping track of your life total is breeze with the dual dial life counters, and keep an eye out for a chance to activate Elemental synergies! Build the strongest army, and help protect the kingdom!

Elemental synergies can be activated by playing cards with matching elemental types, each of the 4 elements, excel at a particular task. 
 Life - will heal you and allow you to draw more cards  
Darkness - will allow you to destroy your opponent's hand
Fire -will allow you to draw more cards, and deal extra damage for each other fire card. 
Water - will allow you to remove unwanted cards, making your deck more effective.

Mix and match more than one element to build decks that synergize in unexpected ways.

Each element has cards that can act as mounts allowing you to send our your mercenary meeples, to gain even more effects. Place enchantments to shield you and your meeples from your opponents. Is your opponent's mercenary meeples, causing you grief? Destroy them!

To win, drop your opponents health to "0", empty out the mercenary guild, or bring your life total over 99. The path to victory is varied, which will your choose?

Game includes everything needed for 2-4 players!

Utility is a card game played with small, square cards. Each player starts with a utility plant card, a house card, and a plain road card connecting the two. The utility plant and the house each have three utilities coming from them: gas, water, and electricity. The goal of the game is to be the first player to use utility tile cards (which each have segments of gas pipes, water lines, and electrical wires) to connect each of the utilities from the utility plant to the house.

Each utility card has a unique combination of gas pipes, water lines, and electrical wires, so players must use spatial organization skills, puzzle solving ability, and strategic planning to ensure completion of their system before their opponent.

The game is generally played with 2 players, but it can be played solo if desired. With two copies of the game, up to 4 players can play.

How to play:

Setup: Each player begins with a utility plant card and a house card, connected by a plain road. The remaining cards are shuffled and placed face down in a stack.
 Objective: The goal of the game is to either be the first player to connect all three utilities (gas, water, and electricity) from their utility plant to their house OR to have fewer tiles placed than the opponent when the deck runs out of cards.
 Gameplay: Three cards are drawn off the top of the deck and placed face up in front of the deck. Players take turns choosing one of these cards and either adding it to their utilities OR replacing a previously-played card. When replacing cards, the card replaced becomes one of the three cards in front of the deck. When playing a new card, a card off the top of the deck is drawn to become one of the face-up cards in front of the deck. Cards may be placed adjacent to any card in play. Cards with roads can (but are not required to) be played on top of the starting road card.

Connecting Utilities: Utilities do not have to align when placing a new card. In order to win, there must be a complete path from the utility plant to the house for all three utilities, and branches/alternative paths may remain unfinished. Water and gas align at the edges of the cards, and electricity aligns at the corners.

Ending the game: The game ends when one player connects all three utilities from the utility plant to the house (that player is the winner) or when the last face-down card is drawn from the deck, in which case players count their utility cards and the player who has fewer cards in play wins.

description:
Of War and Men is a WWII based, squad-level card game that introduces the individual heart of a soldier into the framework and strategies of war. Build effective fireteams and create or discover the land as you cross the war torn map and try to outflank, outmaneuver, and out gun the enemy. As any good leader, you must consider the individual strengths and weaknesses of your soldiers to make sure your Mission is a success.
 In one Mission, you must rescue or capture the Resistance soldier who has recovered secret plans and get them safely back to your camp. In another, you must parachute into the landing zone and capture the bridge in order to win the day. This 2-4 person game can be played within an hour, or track your soldiers across an entire campaign, awarding medals for courage and valor, while determining the ability of green troops coming in to replace lost heroes. Replay-ability is high since each encounter will vary based on Mission, fireteams, map builds, and general unpredictability of war. 
 But these aren't just pieces on a board. You aren’t just sending in soldier #2, but rather, you are sending in Corporal “Pokey” Johnston. His bio informs you that he's a down home roots kind of guy from Colorado. He hasn't been able to hit the broadside of a barn on this mission. However, you've been saving that "Act of Valor" card and you decide to play it with Corporal Johnston and he turns the tide of battle. These aren't just names on a roster. These aren't just numbers on a map. These are your men. These are your brothers in arms.

—description from the designer

description:
There are two cards face up at the same time, the players look for a certain object on them and then have to decide in a flash whether they see the object on one, two or no card at all.

Who is the quickest to discover the hidden things?

If a player is correct he gets one card. The other cards are discarded.

The first player to collect three cards wins.
—description from the designer
Cap10 is a combination of book and game.

It's time once again for the Harvest Festival in the Kingdom of Hybras Isle. As the citizens of the Kingdom prepare for the annual celebration, it's up to you, the players, together to pay respects to the Ancestors' Tree, where the Luck of the people can be stored for the coming year.

Players decide when the game ends before beginning: First to X Coins (victory points that are also a resource in the game); Play X Rounds and whoever has the most Coins wins; Play until all Coins are taken from the Bank and the one with the most wins.

Each player draws a hand of three villagers at the start of each round. On their turns, players play a card, add their villager's luck value to the communal score, follow the effect of their card, then draws 1 card from the deck and passes to the next player.

But be careful - even luck has its limits, and you can't gather too much at a time without breaking the old tree. You can't play a card if it would raise the score over 99, and if you can't play any cards at all, you're out.

The last player left standing wins their prize for the round, the deck is shuffled back up, and the fun begins anew!

Die Hitman is a tactical solitaire assassination game, where you play as a hitman trying to take down the Target, an evil man of many underhanded deeds.

The Target moves about the town, while crooks and cops lurk about. Hiding in the crowds, the hitman must work to find the opportune time to hit the Target. The hitman has an arsenal of guns, bombs, and poison to attempt to get the target, but one false move and it is game over!

The game is setup by laying out 25 cards in a 5x5 grid that makes the town. The Target starts at a random location, but he moves throughout the night. With proper intel and planning, the hitman looks to get the upper hand and strike the Target at the right moment. But even with the Target right under his nose, if the hitman strikes at the wrong time the heat can be on him in an instant.

The hitman only gets so many turns, until the Target will escape to the Helipad. During that time period, the Alert Level gets higher causing the cops and crooks to move further attempting to find and corner the hitman.

Die Hitman plays in less than 30 minutes.

Die Hitman contains:
25 Location cards
19 Target cards
5 Tool cards
2 Player Aides
Dice and counters.

This supplement includes new rules for Necromunda: Underhive (2017).

Gangs of House Orlock: Rules and background for the House of Iron.

Hired Guns and Hangers-on: Adds additional characters to your gang.

Underhive Perils: Rules for using the Badzone Delta-7 tile set.

Dramatis Personae: Pre-generated profiles for a cast of characters that bring the underhives to life.

A trick-taking card game of cave drawings before the hunt!
Before the great hunt, shamans of the tribe gather in the cool dark of the sacred caves. There, in ochre and ashes, they draw images of their ancestral spirits on the walls in the hopes of great bounty. Who will find the greatest prizes?

Aurochs is a trick taking, point scoring card game for 3 or 4 players. Each round, each player in turn plays a card from their hand. The person who played the highest ranking card takes all the cards played that round and sets them aside for later scoring. Rounds continue until all cards are played, then they are scored. The first player to end a round with over 200 points is the winner.

789 is a quick dice game with pattern recognition.

The goal of the game is quite simple: you try to win tokens.

Components: one card, two dice and twenty five tokens.

These Nations give each player control of a specific European nation during the Age of Discovery! Each deck features a custom mix of cards unique to that nation.

Each Expansion pack also contains the cards and rules needed to add add the nation to the game as an AI non-player participant! This means you can play 1500 with your friends or solitaire against 1 or more non-player decks!

Non Gratus Rodentum (also known as Tunnel Rats) is a solitaire dungeon crawl where you generate and explore Vietcong tunnel mazes during the Vietnam war, rolling dice on the tables provided and drawing an abstract representation of the tunnels to explore, fighting enemies and avoiding traps. The game, that comes as a pdf, can be played as a campaign in three acts, where you explore tunnels of increasing complexity, gaining points to spend on recruits and equipment. Surviving the three acts (for a total of 12 tunnels) means you have survived the war, and you can make it home alive.

Cruxy is a stimulating, exciting party game for young and old from 7 years. Game sense: from letters and syllables to form words to any word formations, in which as many words intersect. The winner is the one who has built its field with the most words and cross words at the end.

Card C-Quence is a light family speed game. It consists of a deck of 144 cards and a game board. This game is unique in that it only contains Hearts, Spades, and wild cards.

The deck is shuffled and each player is dealt 20 cards face down. Players stack them in order without looking at them to form their "stockpile". The Top card of the stock pile is turned face up.

Each player is then dealt an additional 5 cards which form their hand. The remainder of the cards are placed in the center spot on the game board. The other 4 spots on the board are for community piles 2 for hearts and 2 for spades.  The player to the left of the dealer goes first.

On your turn you may play any number and combination of cards from your hand, stockpile, or discard pile to their respective community spaces on the board. Wild cards may be played in place of any card in matching suit. Community piles must start with the number 1 and build consecutively to the number 5.

Once a pile is completed place it to the side and start a new one. If you run out of cards in your hand immediately draw back up to 5.  When you play the top card of your stockpile turn up the next card. You may play a card as long as you have one available to play or until you choose to stop playing cards.

Each player also has their own separate discard piles one for hearts and one for spades. To end your turn you must discard one card to its respective discard pile. Play continues with then next player in a clockwise fashion.

At the beginning of your turn draw back up to 5 cards. If the draw pile runs out shuffle the completed community piles to form a new draw pile and place it in the center of the board. The first player to exhaust their stock pile scores 0 points. The other players score 1 point for each card left in their stockpile. When a player reaches 30 points the game ends. The player with the lowest score wins.

The game may also be played in teams and requires 4 players. All rules remain the same except during you turn you may play from your partners stockpile and discard piles. You may not play from your partners hand. play continues until both players on the same team exhaust their stockpiles. Scores are kept by teams and not by individuals by adding the number of cards/points from both stockpiles.

Card C-Quence is a perfect mix of strategy and luck. Be the first to exhaust your stockpile and maintain the lowest score.

An expansion as well a compilation of Billionaire Banshee, this game has you choose positive (perk) and a negative (quirk) to make the right dating match for you, based on the judgement of  other players.

Mega Billionaire Banshee includes the base game of Billionaire Banshee, the Sexy Foil Pack expansion, and 10 ThinkGeek exclusive cards!

The Sexy Foil Pack cards may be removed before playing with teenagers. With the Sexy Foil Pack cards included, Mega Billionaire Banshee is recommended for age 18 and older.

Contents:
    208 main game cards
    20 Sexy Foil Pack cards
    10 exclusive cards from ThinkGeek

Animal Bird Fish is an educational card game for children of all ages that helps to prepare children for reading and identifying different animals.

The main way to play is called "Lucky Sevens":
All the cards are dealt out to all the players. The dealer starts by playing a number 7 of any group; Animal, Bird, or Fish, the next player must follow suit/group and play a number higher or lower. Or he may play a 7 of any group. If you cannot play you must pass. When a player runs out of cards he is the winner. He receives a score equal to the number of cards left in the other players hands. It has a bit of a trick-taking mechanic. Players play to an amount agreed upon before the start of the game.

Because the cards are numbered it can also be used to play Rummy, Snip and Snap - which is similar to war, and Rally-up - a game where players try to get 12 consecutive cards of one group by passing 2 cards face down to the player on their left.

boardgame: The Order of Vampire Hunters: Swarms Expansion
description:
This expansion for The Order of Vampire Hunters can be added to any Hunt and contains:

6 Highly detailed plastic Swarm Miniatures:
• 2 Mists.
• 2 Swarms of Bats.
• 2 Swarms of Rats.
6 Swarm Encounter Cards.
6 Vampire Activation Cards.

Most vampires cause problems for the Hunters by attacking and causing wounds. But every hunt is played against the clock and the 3 new swarm creatures create difficulty in other ways. They may reduce Hunter movement and prevent certain actions, slowing the progress hunters make towards the hunt's objectives.

Ogleis is a dying planet whose surface is covered with a thick layer of ice, in the subsoil remains an extremely hostile biome immersed in the darkness or survival is pushed back into these last entrenches. This planet has only a rare source of energy the phospho-substance and one resource: the mutagen which is the main nutrient and a powerful vector of mutations.

Glow in the darkness is a game halfway between the wargame card driven and the 4x, it's also a semi-cooperative horror game. Players start the game with 12 units, 3 source cities and 7 action cards. Dynamic gameplay: during its turn of play the player makes 2 actions chosen from a variable choice of programmed action

Players embody evolved alien entities whose race is on the verge of extinction. Each player leads one of the 4 alien factions that try to guarantee their survival by plundering the genetic material (material token) of the opposing factions. The first player to acquire 4 material tokens wins the game. At the beginning of the game, the abilities of each faction are symmetrical (which also makes it easier to understand the game).  Each player develops his own faction's abilities during the game based on his strategy of adapting and his playing affinities, the game then takes on a highly asymmetrical twist.

Players must conquer energy Source-cities, manage their energetic and opposing networks in order to increase their power. Players must also move their units carefully to guard against hostile reactions from the fauna and flora.

Glow in the darkness has mechanisms and a unique gameplay including:
- a common semi-hidden deckbuilding for the improvement of factions
- a common action system with semi-hidden orders (which favors manipulation between players)
- a controlled "action anomaly"promoting bold strategies
- the morphe system for the specialization of faction capabilities
- a sequential combat resolution system  
- a biome with an autonomous and interactive evolution, including a mechanism of procedural generation associated with a comparative and adaptive mechanism of fauna and flora.
- major evolution of the scary game (based on recent scientific studies concerning: fear)
- the original and subtle management of resources through the construction of energy networks

A game for 2-4 players 14+ years old. Category: intermediate - expert

Textile is a competitive tile-laying game for two players. Each player is assigned either black or white color, and each tile has a black and white pattern. Legal tile placement requires that edges of the new tile that touch previously-played tiles must match the same color. A player color will score points when an enclosed shape of that color is created, regardless of which player placed the tile. After each player has placed 15 tiles, the player with the highest score wins.

boardgame: Eden: Survive the Apocalypse – Burn Out
description:
Burn Out is the first expansion for Eden, the post-apocalyptic skirmish game. Burn Out gets the better of Eden the game. It starts with its original universe and its efficient gameplay, and it adds what it is necessary to get into the game by another aspect.

In Burn Out, setting up a game is simple, making the game easier for beginners. Games are scripted and the rules allow a better interaction with the scenarios. Simply, you read the scenario, set up the game table, and the game begins!

This also allows you to create your own scenarios and so to play on crazy tables ! You have always dreamed to play on a dock filled with containers, in city in ruins or the footbridges of an industrial complex ? With Burn Out you can !
The accessibility of this game will delight novice players, while additional rules will give new sensations to veterans.

Finally, Burn Out is also a campaign with more than ten scenarios, during which your fighters will get experience and become more and more powerful ! But be careful, at the end of a game, the wounds of your fighters remain on their card.. Your opponent, if he is weaker than you, will be able to take artifacts. So, be careful to well manage your resources, the world of Eden is violent and ruthless !

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Hägar der Schreckliche: Auf zu neuen Ufern!
description:
On to new shores! Hägar the Horrible is on the way again and trying to conquer new dominions. It's about fame, gold & barley juice .. The competition does not sleep, so be ready for great deeds. One Viking, one aim, the game!

A entertaining quick game with coasters. Fun for everyone!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Game play: All coasters are mixed well and placed facedown as a general draw pile. The active player draws a coaster and lays it open. A single coaster always consists of 4 individual landscape fields (land, forest or water). One of these landscape fields must always be laid out to fit an already lying landscape field. After placement, put 1 of your own markers (Hägar or Gold) on any landscape field of this (just applied) coaster. After the last placement (all coasters and markers are laid out), the starting player rolls the dice once and places it on the corresponding field of the board (everything here counts twice). A final round of flipping markers begins. At the end of the game, any landscape where Hägar markers are located will score for itself and score points for the players. Victory points are available for adjacent gold or winning possible dominions.

—description from the designer (translated)

Auf zu neuen Ufern! Hägar der Schreckliche ist wieder unterwegs und versucht neue Herrschaftsgebiete zu erobern. Es geht um Ruhm, Gold & Gerstensaft .. Die Konkurrenz schläft nicht, also sei bereit für große Taten. Ein Wikinger, ein Ziel, das Spiel!

Ein kurzweiliges schnelles Spiel mit Bierdeckeln. Spaß für jedermann!

—description from the publisher (German)

Spielablauf: Alle Bierdeckel werden gut gemischt und verdeckt als allgemeiner Nachziehstapel bereit gelegt. Der aktive Spieler zieht einen Bierdeckel und legt diesen offen aus. Ein Deckel besteht immer aus 4 einzelnen Landschaftsfeldern (Land, Wald oder Wasser). Eines dieser Landschaftsfelder muss beim Anlegen immer ganz passend zu einem bereits liegenden Landschaftsfeld angelegt werden. Nach dem Anlegen wird 1 eigener Marker (Hägar oder Gold) auf ein beliebiges Landschaftsfeld dieses (gerade angelegten) Deckels platziert. Nach dem letzten Anlegen (alle Deckel und Marker sind ausgelegt) würfelt der Startspieler einmalig den Würfel und platziert diesen auf das zugehörige Feld des Spielplans (hier zählt alles doppelt). Eine letzte Runde des Umlegens von Markern beginnt. Am Ende des Spiels wird jede Landschaft, auf denen sich Hägar-Marker befinden für sich gewertet und bringt entsprechen Siegpunkte für die Spieler. Siegpunkte gibt es für angrenzendes Gold oder dem Gewinn möglicher Herrschaftsgebiete.

—description from the designer

To the limit! Popeye beats his way .. Nobody can stop him with the power of spinach, or not? Brutus gets in the way, but Olivia is at Popeye's side. Go with Popeye so far you can to score high!

A entertaining quick game with coasters. Fun for everyone!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Game play: The active player mixes all coasters and places the stack facedown in front of him. The active player now covers up coaster to coaster until he voluntarily or forcibly stops. Lots of coasters show a certain number of spinach cans, which the player adds over the individually uncovered coasters (from coasters to coasters).

Background: Each spinach can boost Popeye and bring more and more victory points! Only with the appearance of Popeye, the players can freely stop and hold his points. Oliva helps to increase the points quickly, Brutus breaks all points and the player has to stop. If a player achieves 54 or more victory points at the end of a complete round, he finishes the game. The highest number of victory points wins.

—description from the designer (translated)

Bis ans Limit! Popeye schlagt sich durch .. Mit der Kraft des Spinats kann ihn keiner aufhalten oder doch?

Brutus steht im Weg, aber Olivia ist an Popeyes Seite. Gehe mit Popeye soweit Du kannst, um stark zu punkten!

Ein kurzweiliges schnelles Spiel mit Spieldeckeln. Spaß für jedermann!

—description from the publisher (German)

Spielablauf: Der aktive Spieler mischt alle Deckel und legt diese als Stapel verdeckt vor sich ab. Der aktive Spieler deckt jetzt so lange Deckel nach und nach auf, bis er nicht mehr kann oder will. 
Viele Deckel zeigen eine bestimmte Anzahl an Spinatdosen, die der Spieler über die einzelnen aufgedeckten Deckel (von Deckel zu Deckel) aufaddiert. Hintergrund: Jede Spinatdose stärkt Popeye und bringt mehr und mehr Siegpunkte! Nur bei einem Auftauchen von Popeye kann der Spieler freiwillig aufhören und seine Punkte festhalten. Oliva hilft die Punkte schnell zu erhöhen, Brutus macht alles zunichte und der Spieler muss aufhören. Erzielt ein Spieler am Ende einer kompletten Runde 54 oder mehr Siegpunkte, endet das Spiel. Die höchste Siegpunktanzahl gewinnt.

—description from the designer

"Great Minds List Alike"

Listit is a party game with hilarious discussions as you find out all kinds of things about your friends’ thoughts and prejudices as you play. The game has more than 500 witty, weird and wide-ranging questions – with no correct answers whatsoever.

How do you play?

There are five topics to choose from. The first player selects a topic, picks up a card and reads the corresponding question aloud. All players then write individual lists of 5 answers to the question and score a point for every answer shared with other players – no matter what that answer might be! If you're certain someone will list "Trump" in “Worst presidential haircuts”? Star that answer for a three-point bonus, if you’re right – or a three-point penalty, if you’re not.

—description from the publisher

GET ON THE GO! Live the life of a high-flying trader in the fast-paced world of real estate.

Get ready to play the game of your life as MONOPOLY, the world's favourite in board game, teams up with Officeworks in this unique limited collector's edition.

Officeworks’ history dates back more than 15 years to1994 when the first store opened in the Melbourne suburb of Richmond. Today,  Officeworks is Australia's largest office products and solutions retailer and supplier with operations across the country employing over 6000 Team Members.

Whether you're male or female, eight or eighty, the Officeworks MONOPOLY limited edition a must have! It's your chance to build your own network and make MONOPOLY millions by wheeling and dealing amid the fun and excitement of Officeworks.

Do you have what it takes to win the game?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Rivals for Catan: Deluxe – Promo Card Pack
description:
A small promo card pack containing the nine promo cards that were included inside Rivals for Catan: Deluxe

This promo card pack was available at the Catan Studio booth during various game conventions including BGG.CON 2016.

One complete game consists of two game rounds. The first round is a hiking tour played with 1 die, the second a bicycle tour with 2 dice. Each player starts with a score of 10 points. On the hiking tour the players have to draw an event card if they get on a "DJH" space. If a player rolls a 1, one point is deducted from his score. On the bicycle tour an event card is drawn after each roll and a point is deducted by the roll of a double.

The first player to finish the bicycle tour gets a Bonus of 5 points, the second player gets 3 points. The player with the most points wins the game.

Munchkin 9: Jurassic Snark takes munchkins to a long-gone era to fight dangers they've never encountered. With this 112-card expansion added to any Munchkin base game, you can wear primeval armor, ride dinosaur steeds, and fight munchkinivorous monsters.

In That's How We Do It! players will do some ridiculous things while laughing uncontrollably! If you draw an Always card you will do the wacky thing on your card every time it's your turn. A Whenever card directs you to do what the card says whenever another player does something specific. One wacky thing definitely leads to another in this zany game of craziness!

SET UP

     3 players randomly choose a prop card
     Shuffle the action cards and put them facedown in a pile within reach of all players
     Decide how many rounds to play. With 4-5 players, 8 rounds are recommended. With more players, less rounds. With less players, more rounds. When every player has picked an action card that is one round. 


PLAY

     On your turn, draw an action card from the top of the deck and place it face up in front of you. Each time you draw an action card, add it to the row of action cards in front of you.
     Read the action card aloud to all the players very carefully. Each card has a point value 1-12 based on the zaniness of the action.  
     There are two types of action cards:
         Always-if you draw an always card, do the zany thing immediately. Then repeat that zany thing every time it's your turn. 
         Whenever-if you draw a whenever card, do nothing right now. However read the card carefully because you will need to do whatever zany thing is on the card whenever another player does a specific zany thing.
    



     Don't forget to read the Prop Cards and pass them around throughout the game. There are 3 different prop cards:
         Funny Glasses-when you have this card, wear the glasses until someone stands for any reason. Then pretend you're a teacher and say something really smart. Then pass the glasses and card to the player on your left. 
         Microphone-when you have this card, hold the microphone until someone sings. Then sing into the microphone along with them. When you're done singing, pass the Microphone and Card to the person on your left. 
         Engagement Ring-when you have this card, wear the ring until someone kisses anything. Then run over, get down on one knee, and give the kisser the ring.
    



     Once during the game, each player can pick one of his or her action cards and give to another player. But remember that in addition to giving that player something really zany to do, you are also givin g him or her points. And that might cause you to lose the game. 
     It's ok to remind players if they forget to do one of the things on the action cards in front of them. After all, the whole point of the game is to see everyone act really silly!


WINNING

     After the agreed upon number of rounds, players add up the points on the action cards. The player with the highest score wins.

Goblin Tower is a worker placement game by designer Patrick James that also includes push your luck, gambling, and resource management elements.

Players control a faction of Goblins attempting to build their tower faster than the other factions by recruiting more goblins and gathering resources in order to build tower rooms, which each enable the faction new perks. Players also use their faction specific abilities and epic side quests to expedite the building. The first faction to build six rooms will top their tower with a devastating catapult to destroy the competition and win the game!

—description from the publisher

Players represent Cambodian garment workers participating in a Rotating Savings and Credit Association (ROSCA). The objective is for the player collectively to acquire 5000 loy (the games money) in order to buy a factory. The player who makes the largest contribution winning.

The game is a roll and move game combined with auction mechanism for the ROSCA. It is 'semi-cooperative' in that one player going bankrupt ends the game in a draw.

Startup Burnout is a cooperative and black humor board game that will test your ability to manage the time and resources of your small business.

There are only two options: grow or die.

They can play from 1 to 4 players from 13 years and the games last approximately 40 minutes.

...And remember: when a businessman dies, others may try to win in the next three days. That is, before the body smells in the office.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Endeavor: Age of Sail – Charter Companies Mini-Expansion
description:
This is a complete mini-Expansion for Endeavor: Age of Sail that includes six brand new Charter Company tiles!

During the building phase, if you have at least 6 presence in a region, you may establish that region's Charter Company instead of constructing a normal building, and add its tile to your mat.

The six different Charter Companies provide a strong attribute boost and a unique double action. They are very powerful, but choose wisely; you may only establish one per game!

This mini-expansion will be included in Kickstarter editions but will be made available to purchase by itself for the retail edition.

2 Naves are disputing the dominion of the space and for this they will have to face a Battle in the space.
The game consists of scrolling data and choosing values to rotate the central disc of the Game Board and with this you get benefits that can be: Ammunition (Red World), Fuel (Blue World) and Trading (White World). You can also shoot shots at the opposing ship and avoid space area with asteroids.
The first player to deal 3 damage to the opposing ship will be the winner, with 1 damage occurring when the ship takes 3 shots.
To make the Game, you will use 1 Butter Cover / Margarine that will be the central disc of the Game Board.
Who will be the winner in this dispute?

2 Naves estão disputando o dominio do espaço e para isso terão que enfrentar uma Batalha no espaço. 
O jogo consiste na rolagem de dados e escolha de valores para girar o disco central do Tabuleiro de Jogo e com isso conseguir benefícios que pode ser: Munição (Mundo vermelho), Combustível (Mundo azul) e Negociação (Mundo branco). Poderá também dar tiros na Nave adversária e evitar Área do espaço com Asteroides.
O primeiro jogador a causar 3 danos na Nave adversária será o vencedor, sendo que 1 dano ocorre quando a nave leva 3 Tiros. 
Para fazer o Jogo, será utilizado 1 Tampa da Manteiga/Margarina que será o disco central do Tabuleiro do Jogo.
Quem será o vencedor nesta disputa ?

A quiz game about the Danish constitution.
There are five categories:

    Folketinget,
    Regeringen/Kongehuset,
    Domstolene,
    Frihedsrettighederne,
    Dannebrog.


The game was mainly developed for use in education.

Fazes is a card game for 3 – 8 players. It has rules similar to Rook, Phase 10, Hearts, and other rummy like card games. The object of the game is to get the least amount of points by the end of the game. There are 6 fazes, each with its own set of rules. The last faze called “madness” combines all of the rules from previous fazes. Fazes is a fun and challenging card game for all skill levels.

Starting Fazes
Each player is dealt an equal amount of cards. The player with the green 2 begins play in each faze by leading with that card. Play continues in a clockwise direction. Players must play the color that is led. After everyone has played a card, the highest numeric card of the color played will take the hand.

Trump
Players who are out of the color led will have to play a trump card if they have it.  Red cards are TRUMP in fazes 1 & 6 and when a red card is played it wins the trick. For example, if a player leads with a yellow card and the next player is out of yellow, they have to play a red card, if they have it, which trumps the trick. If two or more players have to trump any trick, the highest numeric red trump card wins the trick.

Faze 1 : Red Trump
Each red card is worth 5 points. If a player is out of the color that is led and they have a red card, they must play it, which trumps the trick. Once all of the tricks are won, count up each red card, and write down your points. Shuffle and deal all the cards for faze 2.

Faze 2: Tricks ‘R Pointr
Each trick  worth  10  points. Trump  is  not  active  in  this  faze.  The  highest  numeric  card  of  the color  led  wins  the  trick  and  10  points.

Faze  3: Yellow Pointers
Each  yellow  card  is  worth  10  points. Trump  is  not  active  in this  faze.  The  highest  numeric  card  of the color  led  wins the trick. Each yellow card in the  trick  is worth  10  points.

Faze  4: 13’s
The  #13  in  each  color  is  worth  25  points. Trump  is  not  active  in  this  faze. The  highest  numeric  card  of  the  color  led  wins  the  trick.

Faze  5: Last Trick
The  person  who  wins  the  “last  trick” get’s 50 points deducted from their score.

Faze  6: Madness
Each  objective  and  rules  of  fazes  1  -­‐  5  are  in  play!    Red  cards  (5  points  each)  are  trump  and have  to  be  used  if you don’t have the card that is led.    Every  trick  is  worth  10  points  plus  the  value  of  pointer  cards  that  could  be  included  in  the  trick.  Yellow  cards  are  10  points  each.  The  13’s  are  25  points  each.    Special  note  -­‐  the  red  13  is  worth  30  points  (5  points  for  being  red,  25  points  for  being  a  13).  The  yellow  13  is  worth  35  points  (10  points  for  being  yellow,  25  points  for  being  a  13).  And  finally,  whoever  wins  the  last  trick  get’s minus  50  points  from  their  score! The  player  winning  the  last  trick  must  still  account  for  any  points  within  the  trick  itself.

SHOOT    THE    MOON
If  a  player  wins  all  of  the  points  in  any  faze  they  have “Shot  the  Moon!”  The  reward  for
“Shooting  the  Moon” is  all  other  players  receive  the  total  points  of  that  faze! For  example,  if  a  player  in  faze  4  receives  all  four  13’s,  then  all  other  players  have  100  pts added  to  their  score!

The object of the game is to have the lowest score at the end of all 6 Fazes.

A fun, fast-paced combination of strategy and luck for great times with family and friends! You may think you have the upper hand until it happens… Tough Luck! This card game is sure to bring fun to any gathering! For 2 to 4 players or buy 2 decks for up to 8 players!

Object of the Game: Be the first to play all of the cards in your hand and in front of you!

Game Setup: Choose how many players you have and deal the cards according to the directions below.

2 Players: The dealer deals both players 29 cards. Without looking at your cards (or letting the other players look at them) pick 8 cards out of your hand and place them in front of you face down.   Place one card face up on each of the cards you just placed face down.

3 Players: The dealer deals all players 19 cards. (Discard any one card.)  Without looking at your cards (or letting the other players look at them) pick 6 cards out of your hand and place them in front of you face down.  Place one card face up on each of the cards you just place face down.

4 Players: The dealer deals all players 14 cards. (Discard any two cards.)  Without looking at your cards (or letting the other players look at them) pick 4 cards out of your hand and place them in front of you face down.  Place one card face up on each of the cards you just place face down.

5 Players: The dealer deals all players 23 cards. (Discard any one card.)  Without looking at your cards (or letting the other players look at them) pick 8 cards out of your hand and place them in front of you face down.  Place one card face up on each of the cards you just place face down.

6 Players: The dealer deals all players 19 cards. (Discard any two cards.)  Without looking at your cards (or letting the other players look at them) pick 6 cards out of your hand and place them in front of you face down  .Place one card face up on each of the cards you just place face down.

7 Players: The dealer deals all players 16 cards. (Discard any four cards.)  Without looking at your cards (or letting the other players look at them) pick 5 cards out of your hand and place them in front of you face down.  Place one card face up on each of the cards you just place face down.

8 Players: The dealer deals all players 14 cards. (Discard any four cards.)  Without looking at your cards (or letting the other players look at them) pick 4 cards out of your hand and place them in front of you face down.  Place one card face up on each of the cards you just place face down.

HOW TO PLAY:

First… some simple rules:

Only after a player plays all of the cards in their hand can they play the cards that are face up in front of them on the table.

And…

Only after a player plays all of the cards that are face up in front of them on the table can they play the cards that are faced down on the table. You flip over and play these cards one at a time (no peeking at the other cards!) — Keep these ‘faced-down’ cards  face-down until all cards in your hand and the cards facing up in front of you are gone.
In a 2 player game if you flip over a REVERSE card, flip over and play another card… If the card you played after the REVERSE has a lower value the the card played previous to the REVERSE card… “Tough Luck”!  You get to pick up all of the cards in the ‘played pile’.  If you play a higher value car it is the other players turn.

The first player puts a low-value card from their hand (or any card value they choose) on the table face up on the ‘played pile’. (2 is the lowest value and 12 is the highest value card.)The next player places a card down from their hand that has an equal or higher value than the card previously played on the played pile.

If a player has more than one of the same card they can play as many of those same valued cards during his turn.
Example: If a player has two “5” cards in his hand, they can play both of the cards if they want to at the same time.
When 4 cards of the same value are played consecutively in turn those 4 cards and all cards in the played pile are no longer played in the game and go into the discard pile.
If the next player cannot play an equal or higher valued card onto the played pile from their hand, that player picks up all of the played cards on the played pile and adds them to their hand.
The player that picks up all of the cards from the played pile has “Tough Luck™”! They must play the cards in their hand before they can go back and play from the cards in front of them.

End of Game: The first player that runs of out cards wins the game.

It is possible that your last card may be a RESTART — Play it and win the game, a RESET — Play it and win the game or a REVERSE — Play it and win the game!

Special Play Cards:

REVERSE:  The REVERSE cards reverses the play of the game (counter-clockwise to clockwise, etc).  If a player plays a REVERSE card the next player has to match or beat the card they just played.  If you play a REVERSE card your turn is finished. In a 2-Player game if you play a REVERSE card you get to go again.

RESET: The RESET cards clears the pile of cards that everyone is playing on.  These cards are no longer used in the game and go into a discard pile.  If you play a RESET card your turn is finished.

RESTART: The RESTART cards resets the deck to whatever value card the player chooses.  The player plays the RESTART card and then can play whatever card they want to from their hand.

Game Play Variation:Try playing with less cards in front of you and more cards in your hand or vice-a-versa to add a different challenge!

English:
Panda Attack!
You are allowed to shake the dice cup after the active player looked at the throw.

German:
Panda Attacke!
Du darfst den Würfelbecher kräftig durchrütteln, nachdem der aktive Spieler sich den Wurf angeschaut hat.

A tongue-in-cheek card game that has you racing against your opponents to reach 50 years of marriage first.

Think you've got what it takes to make it all the way to 50 years of marriage? In this card game players compete to see who's marriage can withstand the challenges many couples face- such as naughty children, money trouble, pesky in-laws and even the much feared sex drought! But don't worry, for each challenge there is a solution.

Game play is pretty simple. The game can be played with up to 4 individuals or 3 pairs(6 players total). Each turn involves drawing one card and playing one card. To win you must play year cards(1 year, 2, 3, 4, 5 or 10 years)to reach the total of 50 years before your opponents do. Before you can play any year cards you must play a married card. Once you're officially married you can start accumulating years.

On your turn you draw a card: 

     from the top of the discard pile or 
     the top of the deck


Then you choose ONE of the following as your play a card action: 

     Playing a card on yourself such as a Year card, Solution card or Immunity card(which protects you from specific Setback cards).
     Playing a card on an opponent such as a Setback card or Divorce card to slow down their progress. 
     Or if you're unable to do either of those, simply discard a card. 


If you have either a divorce card or setback card played on you, you can not play any new year cards till those cards have been resolved. Marriage cards resolve Divorce cards, Solution cards resolve Setback cards of the same color.

—description from the designer

1815, the world has never been so small. The gears of the Industrial Era are shaping the old English Kingdom into an Empire so big that the sun is always shining on it.

The tempting wealth of the colonies represent a golden opportunity for you to increase your status but if you really want to be ambitious, you must dare to manipulate the political scenario and get the best advantages, otherwise you’ll learn a bitter lesson: one plot is worth more than a million pounds!

Contenders will build ships to reach distant places like Canada, Australia and India. They will establish colonial houses and bring their workers in order to grow in power and wealth. This power assumes the form of influence towards the crown and it is the crown that can best serve all player’s interests.

During three rounds, players will select actions to perform both in England and in the colonies. It is a very demanding scenario, divided in two, with all players splitting their attention and trying to be as sharp and efficient as a context like this obliges.

You’ll need influence to perform actions in England but, to get this influence, money needs to be spent in the colonies. Invest wise, collect more!

Money and status quo combined represent the wealth hold by each player and, in the end of the game, the wealthiest player will be called victorious.

—description from the publisher
