boardgame: Isabel: The War of Castilian Succession
description:
Isabel is a two-player game portraying the War of Castilian Succession (1475-1479). It is a free print-and-play game: all necessary files are posted here.

The death of King Enrique IV in 1474 created a domestic crisis in the Kingdom of Castile. The two contenders for the throne were Isabel, Enrique’s half-sister, and Juana, the 13-year-old daughter of the late monarch. Both women counted with powerful allies in their fight for the throne: in 1469 Isabel’s had wed Fernando, heir to the Crown of Aragon, and Juana married King Alfonso V of Portugal during the early stages of the war. In Isabel, the players assume the role of Isabel and Juana and their respective allies as they compete to garnish domestic and international support for their cause. The players face a multifaceted conflict fought as much in the arenas of Spain and the African coast as in the undecided hearts of the Castilian nobility and population at large.

Over four rounds, the players compete by maneuvering troops on the Iberian Peninsula and the Atlantic coast, and by gaining the alliance of various noble houses, public opinion, and international powers. Isabel incorporates streamlined mechanics that are attentive to the historical circumstances of the conflict. Every turn, players face multiple strategic choices which, taken secretly, require tactical reconsideration made in reaction to the opponent’s chosen actions. Isabel forces the players to constantly consider the risk-reward of their choices. With a limited amount of available resources, players have to decide when to sacrifice assets to obtain substantial one-time benefits.
 
—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Ever since we saw our first ninja movies in the 80's we’ve all been wondering –Cowboys or Ninjas?  On the one hand Cowboys have their trusty six shooters and faithful horses to help them.  On the other hand, Ninja are well versed in the dark arts, possess secret knowledge and skills, and have no qualms about murdering you in your sleep if they think they might lose the show down at sun down.

In Face Off Duels you are installed as the leader of one of these mighty factions and over three rounds of tactical combat you get to figure out which group, which leader, is the greatest of all time.

What separates Face Off Duels from other fighting card games is a unique scoring system that forces players to not only play their opponent, but the board as well.  The result is a quick game that, like a great puzzle, you will want to return to again and again.  The Face Off scoring system plus the magical whimsy that is the Power Deck means no two games of Face Off Duels are the same.

Face Off Duels present you with multiple paths to victory, a myriad of strategic options, and a sense of humor that is way more engaging than this blurb.  In order to win, you must be able to plan ahead, bluff when appropriate, and occasionally pull that one card off the top of the deck.  If you can do all these (and add seven plus five in your head) you just might be able to lead your chosen faction to glory.

An abstract strategy game on a board with three concentric circles; these include eight evenly spaced holes per circle forming spokes, plus a central hole. Each player has twelve pegs which are placed in the holes at the start of the game, leaving the central hole clear. The pegs begin on opposite sides of the board. To move, a peg is placed in a vacant hole.

The aim is to take all the opponent's pegs. To take a peg, there must be an empty hole on the opposite side of it, so it can be jumped, and multiple jumps can be made.

The game was deisnged for blind and visually impaited players. The pegs are different shapes as well as colours, and the circles are cut into the board. The rules are in English and Braille.

Electronic version of Hedbanz Charades.  Score points by guessing the card on your headband with the help of your teammates as they give clues based on one of three randomly selected modes each round; You can ask "yes" or "no" questions (or from a small selection of provided questions), listen to your teammates describe what's on the card or watch them act it out.

Game play continues until both teams have performed all three game modes.

Cities in America at the turn of the century are racing to be the biggest and the best cities across the country!  Their skylines are growing with buildings so tall they appear to touch the sky - skyscrapers!  Players act as building companies in these different cities, trying to build up their own city's skyline to be the best and most diverse.

Each player is building a skyline of skyscrapers using cards from a standard 54-card deck.  On their turn, players have three options: Draft cards into their hands; Play cards to their tableau (their "skyline"), or Pass (for the rest of the game).  Cards played must follow certain rules, and only optimal placement will net players the most points!

The game ends once all players have passed (usually after the draw deck has run out and player have no more cards in-hand to play).  Players then earn points for tallest towers, widest skyline, most towers, and any mid-game bonuses they achieved, but lose points for any cards left in hand, or face-down "City Street" cards they played.  After totalling up all scores, most points is the winner!

On the face of it, it is a golden age; King Arthur and Queen Guinevere are married and ruling with wisdom and impartiality, the Kingdom is stable and prosperous and Evil seems to have been driven from the land.

All is not well however; ambitious Lords constantly scheme at the Royal banquets to get in with those in good standing with the Royal couple.  The illicit relationship of the young lovers, Tristan and Isolde, threatens to start a war with their neighbours and then there’s the rumours about Queen Guinevere and Lancelot...

Players play as ambitious Lords in the time of King Arthur, all vying to be seen sat next to the most influential people in court at King Arthur’s latest banquet at Camelot.

Gameplay involves playing a Scheme card from you hand that will allow your character card to move in various ways, or allows you to move the other members (King's Knights, Guests or VIPs) of the banquet.  Moving one character, however, may cause the character it displaces to also need to move in a specific way, and moves may chain beyond your control! For example, moving a Knight may require that knight to automatically move straight to the Royal Row, which may then displace a VIP such as Isolde who must move to be next to Tristan, who may then displace another Knight, etc.

You have until the end of the final banquet course (or "round", which varies depending on chosen game length) to manoeuvre yourself into the best position to gain points from adjacent guests AND to hopefully complete your secret quest for bonus points.  The player with the most influence points wins (and becomes the most influential Lord of the land)!

Each game also has a random or specifically chosen common quest that changes the rules of the game slightly, which all players are affected by.

When playing with less than 4 players, AI players called "Rogue Nobles" operate the "missing" players, moving around automatically and likely screwing up your well laid plans!
family: Theme: King Arthur / The Knights of the Round Table / Camelot

description:
The most dangerous TV show ever has returned with new gameplay in Room 25: VIP.

A very important person is coming to upset everything. As with the suspicion mode in the Ultimate expansion, this VIP is part of the team of prisoners and will be revealed after distributing the roles. Forced to move, the VIP does not program his actions, thus becoming a dangerous and subtle player.

Room 25: VIP also includes five new rooms.

As council members lose their heads, new members step up to take their place.  Gain the trust of new allies and buildings of the realm of Blackthorn.  Win the King's Favor to avoid a beheading, but beware... there are new ways of Casting Doubt on your fellow Council Members.

This expansion for Council of Blackthorn will add 4 new Council Members, new faction cards -  some with lower trigger requirements, and new events.

—description from the publisher

Battle Stations is one of several games playable with the Katarenga board and pieces. This game is invented by the author of Katarenga. 
In this game you start with an empty board. It consists of two phases.

Stations - You each in turn place a pawn anywhere on the board. 
The battle - With all 16 pawns in place, you each in turn capture an opposing pawn following the normal rules of movement in Katarenga. If you can capture you must. If there are multiple capture possibilities, you can choose. If you can't capture, you must pass. You keep on passing until either you can capture again or both players have to pass. 
The game is won by whoever has the most pawns left on the board or, in case of a tie, by the second player.

The rules suggest to score one point per remaining pawn followed by the switching of the first player.

Gears of Glory is a game about a group of adventurers racing their automobiles, those incredible new inventions that go faster than a horse and carriage.

To prove their worth, both drivers and their vehicles need to survive the grueling route across mountains, lakes, forests and villages. They will face fuel shortages, overheating motors and exhaustion.

In each turn of the game, a player has two actions and may spend their all-purpose racing cards to move, explore, repair, refuel, or even push, if they must. They must visit villages to refuel, climb mountains to gain fame and reach checkpoints in their quest for glory!

The game ends when one player reaches the final checkpoint, at which point, the other players have a limited number of turns to also reach it. The player who finished first is not necessarily the winner, as players collect fame points through various feats, including reaching the most mountaintops or visiting the most map tiles.

The game contains 8 double-sided modular map tiles, 110 cards, 4 custom-shaped wooden cars and fuel tokens, several player boards and bonus tiles, among other components.

boardgame: Starport Scum: Space Adventure on the Wrong Side of Hyperspace
description:
Starport Scum is a tabletop game of fierce space adventure, space pirates and salvage crews looking for the next big break.

Drawing on sources such as Traveller, Firefly, Trigun and Borderlands, it's a game about getting by on the outskirts of civilization, always on the look out for that next job.

Combining miniatures war gaming with role playing game touches, Starport Scum is playable as a conventional miniatures game, a narrative RPG or a hybrid beast combining the best of both worlds.

Kobolds & Cobblestones is a skirmish wargame for rumbles between gangs in the city of Ordinsport's seedy underbelly. Players hire gangs of criminals, thugs and enforcers from a number of classic Fantasy races, and attempt to take control of the underworld and establish themselves as the city's kingpins.

Forgoing dice and measuring sticks, the game instead relies on playing cards. All distances are measured in card lengths or widths, combat is resolved by poker hands, and magic by blackjack. Players alternate activating models. Game objectives are by scenario - the rulebook provides eight different ones, ranging from "Hide and Seek" to "All Out Brawl".

The game has a strong emphasis on campaign play, with players recruiting their gang, pitting them against one another, and seeing them grow (or wither) from game to game. Interestingly, the money you use between games to recruit can also be used during games to encourage your fighters, or bribe the enemy's. Money can also be stolen from enemy gangers during a game.

Each game pits two players with their gangs against one another, but many players can participate in the same campaign, meeting various opponents across it.

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 2 – Curtiss P40 Warhawk
description:
The Curtiss P-40 Warhawk was an American fighter and ground attack aircraft used by the air forces of 28 nations during WW2, serving as an air superiority fighter, bomber escort and fighter bomber. The P-40 performed surprisingly well as an air superiority fighter, at times suffering severe losses, but also taking a very heavy toll on enemy aircraft.

Designed to be used with the popular air combat game system created by Andrea Angiolino and Pier Giorgio Paglia, each WW2 Wings of Glory Special Pack is a ready-to-play large size model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other WW2 Wings of Glory game product. In each pack you will find all you need to play with the airplane: a special base with gaming stats, a variable altitude flying stand, and a specific deck of maneuver cards, as well as specific rules and components necessary to use the airplane.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: This War of Mine: Tales from the Ruined City
description:
War knows no end. Its victims are counted in thousands and each day we lose hope, little by little. Food is becoming scarce and our shelter is falling apart — yet we keep struggling.

New faces can be seen around the city: the friendly ones at the makeshift market or in neighboring houses, the hostile ones in the night with blinding flashlights and clubs and knives. We seek new paths to travel through the ruins — sometimes even underground — just to avoid sniper fire and loot anything of value. It's never much, yet we keep struggling. We have no other choice.

Tales from the Ruined City is the first expansion for This War of Mine: The Board Game, a story-rich game of survival in a war-torn city.

The expansion introduces a range of game modules to enrich the base game:

- Emira is the 13th Character complete with a personal background; you may introduce her in any playthrough
- Desperate Measures cards empower you whenever you lose anyone from your group
- Farmers module features the Market, where your choices affect the way people perceive you; it also gives you a chance to interact with people from outside the city
- Sewers are full of unknown dangers and thrilling narrative, providing you with options for additional Scavenging runs
- Five Scenarios offer many hours of thrilling gameplay; each features unique rules and components and winning one of them gives you access to a reward envelope with special legacy content

Face atrocities of war and prove that from horrible desperation comes great courage.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: This War of Mine (Awaken Realms)

description:
Escape the Alps is a solitaire game that uses grid movement, dice rolling, and resource management.

Synopsis:
You are a captured WWII Airborne Ranger imprisoned by the German SS in a castle in the Alps. You've been put through hell and still you've only given your name, rank and serial number. As they take a break from interrogating you, you manage to break open the window to the room and climb out onto the ledge. You immediately notice the 500ft drop to the rocks below and feel the cool chill air of the Bavarian Alps. You work your way to the face of the mountain and look up to see sheer rock hundreds of feet straight up with occasional patches of snow covering section of the mountain. You have two choices; climb to freedom with no turning back or get recaptured and await certain death from the SS. Your choice is clear, climb the mountain and escape the Alps.

Your goal is to climb up multiple rock faced cards using dice to manage your movement direction.  On each rock face card are hazards, benefits, rest points and location markers.  You must climb through natural rock conditions as well as snow conditions.

Certain dice arrangement can gain you another movement die or allow you to modify existing direction die.

Each game is different due to 12 individual rock face configuration and marker locations.

WINNING CONDITION:
Successfully manage your climbing piece up the face of a mountain to the top without falling. You must manage your climbing route and try to pass through specific zones while trying to avoid losing as little fatigue as possible.

LOSING CONDITION:
Become too fatigued or lose your grip and fall off the rock face; losing the game immediately.

—description from the designer

In Chaparral – The West Expasion, the players will find new elements that will increase the strategy of the game.

Four new characters - Mayor, Reve, Teacher and Undertaker to change the gameplay with the twelve original characters, creating a whole new combos, besides the Miner and Railtrain Chief get new actions.

With this addition plus new resource Wood, a train station, new construction cards, and eight new goals will improve the game experience and make the game more fun and strategic.
This is one of many thematic boxes for the trivial pursuit. It focuses on Science & nature (the green questions) and is known for its challenging difficulty.

Year 700 A.U.C. (53 A.C.). The Roman Republic, strong under the lead of Caius Julius, is waging war over Gaul. His personal power is ever increasing, shaking the very foundation of the Republic, while Senators tremble in fear.
This is the time in which great men can show their value and fools their weakness.
But that is the front line, not here. You are somewhere away from glorious battles and sieges, reckless attacks and everything good that life can give to a soldier of Rome. Here, stranded in namely captured and loyal lands. Captured, but flow of supplies is not as safe as expected. Loyal, but tax collection is resisted. Your job is ensuring someone else can show off with well-fed, well-equipped and well-paid soldiers. At least for now.
Your long-time rival has recently arrived here, tasked with your same mission. A weak man may see a threat, but a strong man sees a chance. Prove your tactical prowess and bravery. Make a fool of him, running around chasing his tail. It’s time to outwit him and show your worth to the future Imperator. Get the promotion you deserve, strive for glory.

Quaterni Lapilli is a strategy game about tactical control of resources during Gallic Wars. The players are low-ranked army leaders, trying to outshine each other to get a field promotion. Using double sided and ever shifting tactic cards, they must seek control of supplies lines by deploying their soldiers where needed while keeping barbarians in check.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Endless Pass: A Viking Saga – The Nine Hunter Promo Card
description:
This is a promo card for Endless Pass: A Viking Saga.

This promo card is a new Nine hunter card. It depicts two "Viking loses 1 health" symbols.

To play with this card, during setup players should either:

1) substitute it for the weakest hunter (the one resistant to the Storm Hammer) to make the Nine more powerful and dangerous

or

2) select The Nine at random from the 10 hunter cards to add more variation to the game.

Either way, you should always only play with 9 hunter cards total.

This promo card was released in Game Trade Magazine #217 (March 2018).

La Bataille Electorale ("The Electorale Battle") is a game about an election in the Canadian province of Québec circa 1977 during the rise of separatism. It was independently produced and distributed, made in limited numbers and sold only in Québec. Played on a stylized map of the province with historically named but hypothetical electoral ridings, two to five players battle for votes to get elected in the 100 districts and win the opportunity to form the government.

Players roll dice and move their token on the game track, which can direct them to place their markers (50 apiece) in ridings or get game cards (like “Imponderables”) to effect the game.
Made when the Parti Québécois was coming to power, 1977 was an exciting and contentious year in Canadian politics. To simulate this, the players represent five different hypothetical political parties that are based on their historical counterparts:


     Parti de l’Unite Nationale (based on the Union Nationale)
     Ralliement Social-National (based on the Ralliement National)
     Rassemblement des Travailleurs Quebecois (based on the Rassemblement pour l’Independance  Nationale)
     Parti de la Souverainete Quebecoise (based on the Parti Quebecois)
     Action Federale (based on the Liberal Party of Quebec)


—user summary

boardgame: A Game of Thrones: The Card Game (Second Edition) – Someone Always Tells
description:
Someone Always Tells is the sixth Chapter Pack of the "Flight of Crows" cycle, and it brings a fitting conclusion to the major themes explored in this cycle. As with the other expansions, you’ll find new cards with the Bestow keyword, an attachment and a location devoted to the faith of The Seven, and cards that reward players for forging alliances between the factions. You’ll also discover plenty of recognizable characters and locations from the saga that you can add to your decks, such as Mace Tyrell, Qyburn, and the Great Sept of Baelor.

—description from the publisher
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: A Game of Thrones – The Card Game (LCG)

boardgame: A Game of Thrones: The Card Game (Second Edition) – The Shadow City
description:
The Shadow City marks the beginning of the fifth cycle of Chapter Packs — the "Dance of Shadows" cycle. Here, you'll find the story advancing into the pages of A Dance with Dragons and the return of a beloved mechanism from the game's first edition: Shadows. Throughout this expansion and the rest of the cycle, you'll find characters, locations, attachments, and events emblazoned with the Shadow keyword. Assassins, spies, conspirators, and secrets hide in the twilight, ready to spring into view. Every faction must embrace the darkness and the path of shadows—or fade into obscurity.

—description from the publisher
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: A Game of Thrones – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
Promo tiles unlocked as part of the 2017 Kickstarter Campaign. It comes as two punch boards.

It includes two crop tiles: Cactus ("Opuntia ficus-indica") and The Lake ("Lacus Ludus" - literally 'Game Lake'), together with a token used with the Cactus tile. They can be added to the existing crop tiles to give more variety at game setup.

It also includes fertilizer tokens. These add a new mechanic to the game where fertilised crop tiles yield more return.

The rules are listed on the last page of the rule book of the (kickstarter) base game.

This 19th-Century game may be the earliest ever based on a specific Napoleonic battle, with the board showing a fairly detailed map of the area around the city. (While undated, it probably pre-dates Parker Bros. 1895 Waterloo.)

The game is for 2 - 7 players, each of whom plays the role of one of the Coalition commanders advancing on Napoleon's central position: Blucher or von Bulow for the Prussians, von Schwartzenberg or von Klenau for the Austrians, Benningsen or Prinz Eugen for the Russians, or Karl Johann Bernadotte of Sweden.

Each player has a unique track toward Leipzig as well as an illustrated player card with a biography on the front and rules governing each of the red-colored points along that player's track. Each turn, a player throws three D6, and if the result is 9 or higher, advances his piece one space. Each red space has an additional requirement that can lead to penalties or bonuses. Each track also has a battle to be won along the way (with the map noting the defending French commander). Moving past the battle requires a roll of 10 or higher on 2D6.

Players put 6 marks in the pot to start, and may lose or win money from the pot along the way. The first player to reach Leipzig (space 14) wins whatever pot remains.

boardgame: Arkham Horror: The Card Game – Threads of Fate: Mythos Pack
description:
You've journeyed deep into the rainforests of southern Mexico and made the discovery of a lifetime. Now, returning home, a peaceful respite is in order—but your relaxation is cut short when an uninvited guest arrives and tells you that the artifact you uncovered is not what is seems and now the entire Earth is in danger. Her wild claims suddenly become more believable as your world erupts into chaos. Three tasks stand before you at once: you must protect the relic from falling into the wrong hands, solve the mystery of a missing friend, and determine whether to trust your mysterious guest. The stakes are high and time is short. Which will you pursue?

Threads of Fate is the first Mythos Pack in The Forgotten Age cycle for Arkham Horror: The Card Game. This new scenario throws you into the investigation like never before with three separate Act decks that are each in play simultaneously. With few leads and a limited amount of time before the trail runs cold, you must weigh your evidence to decide which leads to follow and which to ignore. Your choices will permanently shape your adventure throughout The Forgotten Age cycle, so you must choose wisely.

Includes: 
60 standard size cards (63.5mm x 88mm): 22 character abilities and 38 scenario/encounter cards 
Rules sheet
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: Arkham Horror – The Card Game
Game: Arkham Horror TCG – The Forgotten Age
Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)

description: Agricola: Bubulcus Deck contains 120 occupation and minor improvement cards for use with the revised edition of Agricola.

“A competitive collectable battle game where Bosses summon their minions and powerful effects to become the baddest Boss in the multi-verse.”

Battle Bosses has you controlling a gigantic Boss, utilizing dice to gain Energy and Crystal resources to unleash devastating moves, spawn Minions to send to battle, and play innovative cards to gain tactical advantage specific to your Boss and Minions.

At the beginning of each game, both players combine and set up their tiles to create a single battlefield, put their boss in the starting space, and draw 5 cards from their deck. On their turn, each player rolls 4 dice which will give them energy and crystals, sometimes even triggering Overload, a critical hit that allows you to reroll dice for extra bonuses. Energy must be spent the turn it is earned to utilize Boss abilities such as movement, creating minions, and unleashing their ultimate move, as well as extra abilities earned by playing cards. Crystals on the other hand, can be saved up or used right away towards cards to unleash additional special moves and devastating effects. Loyal minions are collateral damage as the last Boss left standing wins.

Through our Kickstarter, the Deluxe Tier offers Arena Mode, which boasts MOBA-inspired gameplay for 4 or 6 players, where bosses respawn when defeated and two teams compete to destroy the enemy base. Arena mode introduces area control strategies, 3 separate lanes of attack, fog of war, neutral threats to defeat, pet monster minions that bosses can claim, new boss abilities across a lush map that depicts the different realms of Battle Bosses. This mode offerers Kickstarter Exclusive neutral monsters, artwork, and expansions.

Key Points
● Fierce 1v1 tournament Mode with intuitive and tactical gameplay mechanics
● 3-6 Player battle royale variant available for exciting, extended multiplayer battles
● 4 or 6 Player Arena Mode inspired by popular MOBA games
● Highly detailed, collectable 70mm plastic Bosses and 30mm Minions
● E-Sport and Tournament Play ready
● 6 Collectable Bosses in starting roster (with more Bosses available to unlock in Kickstarter)
● Original lore for each boss that leads players into each boss’s exciting, unique game play.


Battle Bosses is for 2-6 players, ages 14 and older and takes approximately 15 minutes per player per game. Each retail-friendly Battle Bosses box comes with 1 large collectable 70mm Boss Miniature; 8 highly detailed 30mm Minion miniatures; 1 Boss Card; 1 Minion Card; 28 Ability Cards; 9 Hex Tiles; 6 Custom Dice;  and Tokens.

The Deluxe Box comes with all 6 collectible 70mm Bosses; 48 highly detailed 30mm Minion miniature; 6 Boss Cards; 6 Minion Cards; 168 Ability Cards; 54 Hex Tiles; 36 Custom Dice; 1 Double Sided Map Board (supports 3v3 and 2v2 Arena modes); full set of Neutral Camp Tiles and creatures; Tokens; and exciting unlocked stretch goals.

Kess Co will launch its Kickstarter for Battle Bosses on August 14th, 2018 at 10:30am PST and will offer exclusive pricing and content for backers and retailers worldwide.

boardgame: Wrath of Kings: House Nasier – Izari Warhounds
description:
It would seem that dogs would be quite out of place amongst the technomantic and steam-fueled horrors commonplace on the battlefields of Arikania, but through long research and dedicated training, the armies of House Nasier do just that. Some of the war masks of the Nasier allow for limited yet powerful telepathic communication, and it is through these masks that the masters are able to bond with the hounds and help them through the binding with their elemental masks, creating a wonderful blend of intelligence, stability, superior senses, and predatory ability.

Wrath of Kings: Izari Warhounds includes two characters and bases and a unit card for use with the base game.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Yaah! #11 makes a big splash with the issue game, Strike for Berlin, designed by Brian Train. If you've ever wondered what would have happened if the Red Army had managed to win the Battle of Warsaw in 1920 - and then decided to take the Revolution into Germany - this is the game you've got to get on the table.

This game is a drastic reworking of Brian Train's earlier and long out-of-print game Freikorps.
The subject matter is the same - the Red Army, after winning the Battle of Warsaw in August 1920, decides to keep going west and bring revolution to Germany on the points of Soviet bayonets.
But so much has changed: new map, new Orders of Battle, new mechanics and a surprising array of optional rules to keep play variable and interesting: Armoured Trains; the Trotsky Train; the Soviet Baltic Fleet; Tactical Doctrines; Marshal Pilsudski; Volunteer Legions; Entente units; the Virus of Revolt; Reichswehr variable deployment and construction; options for the Free City of Danzig; and Conscription on the March.

The game also mates with Red Horde 1920 (itself a reworking of Konarmiya) from Tiny Battle Publishing, so you can play one long campaign for Central Europe, during the fateful summer and fall of a 1920 that never happened....

176 die-cut, double-sided counters
17 x 22" hex map of western Poland/ eastern Germany
John R. Cooper did the art for both!

(from the Publisher)
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Outsmart is the fun and fast-paced family game featuring creative, engaging questions that all have multiple correct answers.

Playing Outsmart is simple:

Everyone takes an answer sheet; the dice is rolled to determine the number of answers required; a topic teaser and one question are read aloud; then players write answers to try and Outsmart their opponents by giving the most correct answers each round.

The trick to Outsmart is choosing between more popular and less obvious answers and then trying to say them, in turn, before other players do; since only original answers count.

Outsmart is knowledge, strategy, timing, luck and lots of humour all rolled into one.  And every Outsmart game you play is fun and truly unique.

PICKLE PICKLE PEPPER is a casual push-your-luck micro-game...which is a whole bunch of industry jargon that translates to, "This simple game turns a small number of components into a ridiculous amount of fun!" Designed to be playable by almost anyone in a very short period of time (5 to 15 minutes, on average), PICKLE PICKLE PEPPER is the perfect filler game, the perfect family game, and the perfect warm-up, cool-down, and/or in-between game for those marathon gaming days! It's also portable and fits in your pocket, so you can play it virtually anywhere.

Put plainly, PICKLE PICKLE PEPPER is the perfect pastime for people with a passion for point-based pick-up play!

(In this game, alliteration is part of the fun, so get used to it.)

The basics are simple. Players start their turn by shuffling the whole deck, then pledging how many cards they intend to draw; that's your Pickle Pledge. If you're able to draw a number of pickles equal to your pledge amount without drawing a pepper, you can choose to stop and bank your Pickle Points. However, if you're feeling bold, you can push your luck (which we call a Pickle Push). While you're pushing, pickle cards are worth twice as many points, but if you draw a pepper card, you not only score zero points this turn, you lose some of the points you've already banked! Once your turn is over, pass the deck to the next player and repeat. The first player to bank a total of 20 Pickle Points wins the game!

A three-dimensional version of checkers. There are three stacked 6x6 boards and each player begins with 18 men. Men can move up or down to an adjacent board in addition to the usual checkers moves, and the object of the game is to eliminate all the opponent's men.

This game is one of a series of three-dimensional traditional board game variants published by Tara Toy Corp.: others in the series were Tri-Chinese Checkers, Tri-Gammon and Tri-Tac-Toe.

A small shogi variant on a 4x6 grid. Each player has exactly one of each type of piece from standard shogi (one pawn, one knight, one lance, one silver, one gold, one bishop, one rook, one king), conveniently for beginners learning the moves of shogi pieces.

Pieces that reach the back two ranks of the board promote as in shogi, and captured pieces can be dropped back onto the board as one's own. The object of the game is to either get the king to the promotion zone, or capture the opponent's king.

A chess variant with four armies advancing into the board from four directions, but with adjacent pairs of armies allied. Thus White has a so-called "round front" and a "square front", advancing north and west respectively, while Black has a "round front" and a "square front" advancing south and east.

All four armies take turns to play, so each player on their turn first makes a move with one of their square-front pieces, then with one of their round-front pieces.

The object of the game is to capture both enemy kings. When one king is captured, the other pieces of the corresponding army disappear, so the player can play on but with only half their pieces.

From 2007 through 2010 the game was promoted through a website at playfrontiers.com.

User summary
In this game, the players, as kings of two neighboring countries, aim to quickly end the war between their countries. Deploy Soldier cards to the Field to reduce the cards in your hand. The last player to use up the cards in their hand wins.
—description from the publisher

In a turn, you either play a card to the field (if possible), or retreat and pick up all cards.

愚者の園 (Gusya no Sono) is Fool`s Field, a two player card game of hand managment.

Each player is dealt 6 cards at the beginning, and the rest forms the draw deck. Then, players do multiple battles. For each battle, players play cards to the 3x3 battlefield by matching the cards' icons. If you can't play legally, or wish not to play, you have to call a 'retreat' and lose the battle.

If you lose the battle, you must take all the played cards in your hand, and discard down to the hand size of 6 cards. If you win the battle, you draw from the deck up to 6, unless the deck is exhausted; then you won't draw further.

Then start the next battle, with the previous winner placing the first card from their hand to the center of the battlefield as the lead. Continue battle until one player has got rid of their cards. That player wins the game.

The cards have 3 kinds of icons on the 4 sides of the card: sword / shield / flower. When playing a card, a sword icon must be matched against another sword icon. A shield must be matched to a flower, and flower against a shield.

Since the battle loser must draw back their played cards, players build up partial information but with discards, there are still unknowns. At the start of the game, players can manage their hand from their draw deck. But as the deck is exhausted, it gets harder to control the battle.

Publisher's summary
「愚者の園」”Fools Field”は、すこし変わった2人用の対戦カードゲームです。
このゲームの目的は、いち早くゲームから抜けること。そのためには、相手よりも早く手札を使い切る必要があります。
手番では、場に札をプレイして手札を減らしていきます。
うまく札を配置し、相手を手詰まりに追い込めば、場の札を引き取らせることができます。
手札と山札の減り具合を読み、勝敗を決するフェーズを見越して、手札をうまく構築しましょう！

"Fool's Field" is a rather unusual kind of card game for two. The object of the game is to get rid of your hand faster than your opponent. On your turn, you play your cards to the battlefield to reduce your hand. Play wisely, and when you make your opponent short of legal plays, opponent is forced to claim all the cards played to the battlefield. Keep your eyes on your hand and remaining deck, plan ahead, time your moves, and construct your hand!

User summary
In this game, the player, as a child searching a space in darkness, aims to get out of there without getting caught by the roaming monster.
—description from the publisher

The monster follows a preset path unless it sees you. The player (child) can explore at most one new tile per turn. You lose if the monster catches you or if the monster exits the maze before you do.

かくされた部屋 (Kakusareta heya) is 'Hidden Rooms', a solitaire game where your objective is to escape from the dark room you are trapped in with a random monster. If you escape, you get a point. If the monster catches you, or the monster walks to the end of its path: you fail. Get 2 points for victory in the game, or fail 3 times and you lose.

The monster is determined randomly at the beginning of a round. The game has a small booklet, showing the monsters and their paths. The monster's movement depends on the monster's type, different monsters move differently. Although the monster has predetermined 'movement path', when the player is in close range of the monster, it ignores the path and starts to chase, until the player gets out of sight of the monster.

Each turn consists of the following phases: monster moves (2 steps), player moves (2 steps), player searches (1 tile). Monster moves are mandatory, the player's moves or searches are optional.

On the monsters turn, when in close range, monster moves to chase the player. Or else, it follows the predetermined path.

On the player's turn, first, you may move up to 2 tiles. Then, you may do a search. To search, you draw a tile from a face-down shuffled pile of tiles. If the tile drawn may be legally connected to the tile you are standing on, you may place that tile. It is legal when: the tile colors are the same or the number is greater than the tile you are on.

If you fail to connect, the tile drawn must be placed on a 'memory area'. In further turns, you may draw a tile from this area instead of drawing from the pile. But this area has a max capacity of 5, whenever you placed the 6th tile, it spills. You must then place all the 'memory tiles' back in the pile, face-down, and shuffle them.

You start from the bottom-right tile. You escape if you successfully reach the top-left tile. You fail when the monster catches you, or the monster reaches the end of its track.

Publisher's summary
「かくされた部屋」”Hidden Rooms”は、1人プレイ専用のすこし変わったボードゲームです。

ゲームの目的は、あなたを封じ込める忌まわしい暗闇から抜け出すこと。
あなたに与えられた行動範囲は、はじめこそ僅かなものですが、手番を消費し探索を行うことで、徐々にこれを拡張していくことが出来ます。
幸いゴールがどこかはわかっています、しかし注意してください。ただ出口を目指して突き進むだけでは、足元をすくわれることになるかもしれません。

なぜならこの空間にはもう一つの徘徊者が存在し、それはあなたを捉えようと蠢いているのです。
かれを振り切るには、ときには脇道を探し、自由に動ける空間を確保する必要があるでしょう。
あなたの知恵と勇気、機転と運を駆使して、光を掴み取るのです！

"Kakusareta Heya"(Hidden Rooms) is a unique solitaire board game.

Your objective is to get out of this hideous darkness. You may only move in a limited range at first, but by spending your "search actions" may expand your mobility. Fortunately, you know where the goal is. Unfortunately, rushing towards the goal may swipe your feet.

In this void however, there is also a prowler, wriggling around to get you. To outrun them, you may need to find some sideways, and secure some area to freely move around. Gather all your wisdom and might, wits and luck, to grasp the light and flee from darkness!

17(21) - Kriegspiel with cards for gentlemen
A card based Kriegspiel from the game of "21"

Goal of the game: In 17(21) players will take the role of Army generals in the Age of Reason, struggling to win battles with the proper use of terrain and the capability to understand how to maximize the army the King (or the Queen) mustered to their command. Think about 17(21) as a "divertissement du Roi" where the game of "21" gives an abstraction to play and pass those boring days at Kings's Court, waiting for news from the Danube or Low Countries campaigns.

Goal of the game: players will have to battle each other with an "age of reason" army, to win the battle controlling more ground than the opponent at the end of the day of battle.
The players must achieve this through proper use of terrain and military doctrines.

How the game is won or lost: At the end of the day of battle, the players will have to check how much control their army has on the field of battle. The battle might ends in a draw, or in a minor or major victory. So the players will have to control areas of the battlefiled with relevant units to have a good ground control and so to be declared as the winner of the day.

What pleyers do in their turn: Each player will first draft an army, then each player will decide how to implement the philosophy of his army through another draft of doctrine cards. 
In each battle turn the player will choose to move his units to cover empty spaces in the battlefield, to send orders activating a proper army doctrine (for example the oblique order) or to order units to attack enemy units in the battlefield.
Combat will be resolved without dice, the game of "21" will be used (the so called black jack)

In the end 17(21) would like to be a portable wargame to let the players experience a "miniatures wargame" in the mood it might be played at Kings' Court in the Age of Reason.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The City of Kings: Grimnir Costume Promo
description:
A promotional card originally given out during the 2018 ManvsMeeple Kickstarter campaign.

This promo introduces a new equipment card to the game and is part of the Promo Card: Costume Pack which includes all four Costume promotional cards.

Equiping this item will increase both attack and heal, along with health and worker movement making it a truly multi-role item.

Boogeynoris Wine is a game of chance and light management of resources and actions where you will have to challenge a series of characters in the ability to endure drinking wine. Some characters who shield the Boogeynoris, a man who came from the United States to Spain with the intention of governing the entire wine business. Luck is in ... a 30 side die.

To win in Boogeynoris Wine, in order of turn, each player will show a card from the deck and will have to show that he has more endurance in the challenge of wine through luck in the 30-sided die, which will determine according to luck if win the challenge, draw or lose, and this will cause some consequences with regard to obtaining resources or losing them, win an extra turn, reverse the direction of the turn, earn an extra bonus of punctuation ...

In the end, whoever gets 2 bottle of wine resources and 2 resources of grape clusters, will convert his figure of meeple by the purple meeple, which will award him the winner of Boogeynoris Wine.


Boogeynoris Wine es un juego de azar y gestión ligera de recursos y acciones donde habrá que retar a una serie de personajes en la capacidad de aguante bebiendo vino. Unos personajes que escudan al Boogeynoris, un señor que llegó de Estados Unidos a España con ánimo de gobernar todo el negocio del vino. La suerte está en... un dado de 30 caras.Para ganar en Boogeynoris Wine, por orden de turno, cada jugador irá mostrando una carta del mazo y tendrá que demostrar que tiene más aguante en el reto de vino a través de la suerte en el dado de 30 caras, lo cual determinará según suerte si gana el reto, empata o pierde, y esto provocará unas consecuencias respecto a la obtención de recursos o pérdida de los mismos, ganar un turno extra, invertir el sentido del turno, ganar un bonus extra de puntuación...

Al final, el que consiga obtener 2 recursos de botella de vino y 2 recursos de racimos de uva, convertirá su figura de meeple por el meeple de color morado, el cual, le otorgará ser el ganador de Boogeynoris Wine.

—description from the designer (Spanish)

Fallout: Adventures is a fan made card game set in the Fallout universe. Players traverse the lands of post-apocalyptic United States of America, visiting places known from all of the Fallout games. During their adventure, players will meet familiar characters from the game, create teams of followers and gather equipment to aid them in their quest.

The game features all original art by Oskar Potocki, which immitates the style of the PIP Boy 2000 in-game display.

2-4 players start the game by selecting their starting characters and equipment. The health of characters and the condition of equipment is determined by placing a D6 dice on the card with the amount of pips showing the current state of the character/equipment. Players also choose their starting perks, and mission cards.

Missions comprise of three elements: the mission, location and faction (i.e. Fight Ghouls at Tenpenny Tower). Each part of the mission has a check that must be completed by the player in order to complete the mission. If the player can't meet the conditions of the check, they can flee and discard the mission (then they choose a new one). Most of the checks are die rolls which use dice from a wide range i.e. D4, D6, D8, D10, D12 and D20.

Player parties traverse the radioactive lands of North America. The game map shows most of the locations from all of the Fallout games. Locations are interconnected by roads used by players to travel from one site to the other by means of spending action points. Each location lists all of the actions that can be performed there, ie. Trade, Gamble, Easy/Medium/Hard Mission, rest etc. A player may perform one of the actions in their current location, before their turn is over.

Travelling through locations, players will gather followers, who can be recruited in a number of ways, but most often by means of selecting a recruit action if it is available at the party's current location. Players will purchase party members with caps (the game's currency) and add them to their play area. A D6 dice will be then placed on the follower card, showing their current state of health. Players will also gain equipment the same way and will also level up their characters in order to prepare their team for the final mission. The final mission is hard to complete and will require a trully great ensemble of characters which are well equipped and levelled up in order to complete the assignment. Once a player completes their final mission, they win the game.

—description from the designer

description:
A dual-layered worker placement game for 1-5 people where players compete to build the best zoo, earning victory points for diversity of animals, interesting enclosures and fulfilling objectives, all while completing Conservation projects.

Each player will play as a different Zoo with variable player powers and will gradually improve their starting zoo. Will you recruit the best Gap Year Students? Will you upgrade your staff to make them more efficient or will you attempt to help your Elephants reproduce?

—description from the designer

description:
Magnanimous takes place in the middle of the 19th century during the second Brazilian reign, at the apogee of the emperor Pedro II nicknamed "the Magnanimous." The players represent Barons with large properties in the Brazil provinces, and will dispute their influence over the economy, politics and the military forces with the recently declared "War of Paraguay".

An average Euro themed with area control, resource management, trade and an unique voting system. An asymmetric game with different strategic paths: race to have a directly influence over "the Magnanimous" or even manage your resources, provinces and politicians to conspire against the empire until the arrival of the dreaded republic.

—description from the designer

Cat & Mouse is a simple roll-and-move game where players roll a die to move around a square board with an inward spiral track.

While in the lead, the other players are chasing you from the board, thus naming the lead marble the Mouse and all the other players the Cats. The Cats are chasing the Mouse!

With the luck of an opponent's roll, they can bump you back to the beginning and replace you as reigning Mouse of the game.

Abstract game set in a fantasy world.

Each player has 20 square tiles and each tile is numbered on all 4 sides. On your turn, you place a tile from your hand onto the board with a 5 by 5 grid. The placed tile must be adjacent to at least another tile already placed with all adjacent numbers matching (with the effect that you refill your hand) or (partly) with adjacent lower numbers (with the effect that all tiles with lower numbers are removed from the game).

Game ends after 20 turns. The winner has put down the most tiles in the corners of the game board.

A game about anthropomorphic animals pushing each other out of an arena.

2-4 players

Includes:
Cards
Miniatures
Hex Tiles
Hex Arenas

The game is played by rounds, where you move your character on an arena formed by hexes, trying to push out the opponents out of the arena. Use special abilities and hexes, take advantage of the terrain.

—description from the designer

Ladies and gentlemen, welcome to this wonderful description page! We plan to put on a really exciting show for you today! Are you ready for these girls to show you what they've got?! Here it is, the moment you've all been waiting for — it's STARLIGHT STAGE!

Starlight Stage is a fast-paced set-collecting card game in which you are playing a talent manager whose aim is to get the best talent of all your rival agencies. You start with the fresh talent of a model, an actress, and a singer. You help to build their careers by collecting lessons, events, and upgrades for them which earns them more fame. The manager with the most famous talent at the end of the game wins!

You start the game with three separate decks in a vertical row: the Idol deck, Fame deck, and Event deck. From the top of each deck, you draw a card for each player and lay them in a row next to the deck from which they were drawn. Each player starts with the beginning hand of new talent: the actress, model, and singer. To start the game, the first player has the option of drawing a card from one of the three stacks and adding it to the end of the row, increasing their options of what to purchase. They then use their accumulated talent points to either purchase an assignment card, acquire a fame card, or take a lesson. Play continues until the fame deck is empty. Then all players tally their fame points and whoever has the most points wins!

The English-language edition of Starlight Stage includes both the original Japanese base game as well as the fourteen cards of Starlight Stage: Expansion Card Set 1.

description:
Lab Partners is a dice & card game for 20-30 minutes of silly, sciencey fun for 2-5 players, ages 12+. Combining card drafting with a splash of risk-taking, Lab Partners adds an element of tension as each roll of the dice could result in a brand new invention or the destruction of your entire experiment.

Players draw chemical cards, combine these into compounds, mix these into solutions to create products, earning them points and special abilities. The first to $1 million in funding wins.

—description from the publisher

Promo tile for Orléans from Matagot Games

When you activate this building, you may move your merchant directly to Tours, or, if you merchant is on Tours, you may move it to any other city.

This promo card was included in online purchases at Elzra.com during late 2017 and early 2018.

This is an alternate stat card for the Venomous Spider monster. Whenever you would choose a Spider as part of dungeon setup, you can choose this card instead. Useful in Catacombs 3e only.

This hero can be used in both Catacombs Third Edition and Castles & Catacombs.

World's End is an apocalyptic horror survival baord game where deceiving your teammates may either save your life or be the end of you.

Players will be forced to work with and against each other to ensure they survive and have the best chance to be the first to the escape helicopter leaving the city.

There are a lot of decisions to make as you try to escape the city. Every choice you make may affect who survives the apocalypse…and who doesn’t.

Helping other players survive is a great way to make friends and may also help you along the way. Be warned: Lying to other players may give you a great advantage, however, it also may come back to bite you; depending on how good other players memories are and whether they desire vengeance!

—description from the designer

The game was Kickstarted and cancelled twice in April 2018 and August 2019.

description:
During design week, Milan becomes a chaotic city where hundreds of events take place at the same time; will you be able to visit them all? Study the best strategy to collect those that will make you gain the highest score. Choose the best daily route and collect design objects before anyone else, but remember you just have six days for that! Every false step could make you miss the most important events; every mistake in planning your itinerary could make you be in the wrong place at the wrong time. The winner is the player who best moves around the city finding the most important places and events according to the time of the day, just like it happens in real life.

Overview of the game

In the box we find a board summarizing the 68 Fuorisalone key locations of these past few years: La Triennale di Milano to Bar Basso, from BASE to the hidden historic buildings of the city of Milan. A dashboard/timetable subdivides the design week into three main phases: morning, afternoon and evening. Each time of the day will propose four events taking place at the same time in different locations: the objective of each player is to join at least one event to gain the corresponding card. Every location card represents a design icon-object; with more than one object you will be able to reach the targets that will allow you to achieve more points.

Redonkulary® is the hilarious nonsense word game where creativity and persuasion win!  This portable game includes 60 spiral-bound word-part cards with over 200,000 possible combinations.  Score sheets included.  Laughter guaranteed!

Ages: 9-99. Number of Players: 3-8. Play time: 10-45 minutes. MADE IN USA

Redonkulary® is Easy to Play!

One player goes first as the “Donkster” (judge) and invents a word by combining one word part from each of the three randomly chosen cards. The other players then create “definitions” for the made-up word and try to convince the Donkster to pick theirs. The player who persuades the Donkster to choose their definition earns a point. The player with the most points after everyone has had a turn as the Donkster wins! There is no “right” or “wrong” answer—just fun!

Redonkulary® is an original game bound to become a classic!

In the vastness of space, empires vie for control of strange, alien worlds. Using specialized armies of soldiers adapted to exotic environments, their war councils plan for the decisive moment that they will gain... PLANETARY CONTROL!

Planetary Control! is a fast-paced, competitive card game of planetary conquest set in the colourful space opera universe of "'Sword of the Stars'".

3-5 players compete with one another over several rounds to take control of 6 different alien homeworlds. Players take turns to do a number of actions, including conquering planets, stealing other players cards, and reinforcing one of their captured worlds. A round of Planetary Control! continues until someone draws the 'Ceasefire' card.

Every captured world is worth Victory Points, and the player with the most Victory Points at the end of a game of Control! wins!

—description from the publisher

Disaster at D-Day is a scenario for use as a variant for Mark Simonitch's Normandy '44 wargame (GMT).  
It is based on Peter Tsouras' book: "Disaster at D-Day: the Germans Defeat the Allies, June 1944".

Contains 46 counters from the C3i #31 countersheet.


It is published in C3i magazine #31, 2017.

description:
PAH! is a fast-paced American Sign Language Themed card game for 2-4 players. Cards use Sign language signs for Numbers 1-9 with the PAH representing 10. Players will try to rid themselves of their personal stack of cards before the other players to claim PAH! Rounds are scored for cards played into the scoring field, Tricks earned, and any losses for remaining cards. The first player to score 350 points wins the Game. The average game last 3- 5 rounds, or roughly 15-30 minutes.

—description from the designer

Primar is a set of three games inspired by the Bauhaus art school. Simple yet deep design is what all three games have in common. The artistic use of the three basic shapes and colors merges with the elegant design of each game.

The three micro games contained in the box are (text from the rulebook):

Primar Blue, by Martin Windischer
In this game you start with a victory card, which represents a state in the end of the game.
During the match you will build the board using shape cards and may change your victory card.
At the end, if you have the victory card representative of the board, you win the game.

Primar Red, by Odd Hackwelder
Players reveal and manipulate cards in order to form complete shapes. At the end of the game, the player with most complete shapes is the winner.

Primar Yellow, by Pablo Céspedes and Víctor Hugo Cisternas
The game plays in rounds, one player opens it revealing a card of their choice, and the opponent responding following some limitations. The objective of the game is to open a round in such a way that the opponent cannot respond. By doing so players are granted points, the first player to achieve 15 points wins the game.

All games have their origins in the annual 18 card microgame contest of the BGG site.

boardgame: Legend of the Five Rings: A Matter of Honor
description:
In the provinces of the Lion Clan, the peace of the plains is shattered by the appearance of an oni, a monstrosity from the Realm of Evil. Once the beast is defeated, at the cost of many samurai's lives, the Lion's ire turns to the Crab Clan. Are the Crab not the guardians who protect the Empire from such nightmares? Surely such a terrible thing would never have occurred if the Lion had stood guard over the Carpenter Wall. Ill will turns to bold words, and bold words soon turn to bloodshed. With the two greatest armies of the Empire at one another's throats, who will emerge victorious?

A Matter of Honor is the ultimate Learn to Play Set for the Legend of the Five Rings Collectible Card Game, featuring the streamlined and comprehensive rules of Ivory Edition. Discover the game and delve into its rich history as a member of the stalwart Crab Clan or the proud Lion Clan, and in doing so get ready to face the challenges that lay ahead of the samurai of the Emerald Empire of Rokugan in the years to come! In this set you will find:


     2 Ivory legal and ready to play L5R CCG decks (one Crab, one Lion)
     4 Ivory legal boosters to improve your deck
     A 16-pages story book detailing the events that led to the war
     A comprehensive and fully illustrated rulebook
     An envelope containing secrets to be discovered at your own risk!
family: Setting: Legend of the Five Rings

description:
Mini Expansion for Neuroshima Hex 3.0 Base game.
Only a one tile expansion.

Originally available at the Neuroshima Hex championship in Poland.

Munchkin Cheats is a 30-card expansion made up of various Cheats and other Cheat-themed cards. You can shuffle these cards into any Munchkin base game to put Treasure cards into play more often and make things more interesting for everyone at the table; the expansion also includes Monsters and Curses that interact with Cheat cards!

Squirrels is easy to learn and infinitely replayable.

Simple to win: collect 5 nuts. But along the way your friendships will be tested as other players try to steal your nuts and foil your chances of winning.

With a variety of wild cards to change your circumstances at any moment, there's no telling who will be the winner at the end of the game.

On your turn, you either play a card or discard a card. If a card was played, you resolve the effect and draw back up to 7 cards. If you discarded a card you simply draw back up to 7 cards. In either case, play continues to the next player in clockwise order. You win the game if you have 5 nut cards of the same color in your hand at the end of your turn.

Kalk - The Card Game by By Tommy & Jonathan

Fun & educational card game for kids and parents! Time to exercise our brain and reinforce our math skills.

There are 26 cards in total, full of numbers and colors:

0-10 card numbers x 2 
One of each magic cards. These are - square, square-root, timer (pauses the opponent for 15 seconds) and scissors (to remove one of the goal numbers).

Deal each player 5 number cards. Deal two cards between the players; this is the goal-number. They use minus, plus, multiply and divide to the get the two-digit goal number. Whoever gets the answer first, wins the game. You can replace two cards but this takes extra time. You can also use the six as a nine.

Unsuccessfully funded on Kickstarter March 2015. However, during the campaign, there was a print and play version available and also a few months after on their official website

Halali is a hunting/catching themed game from Swedish publisher Aristospel, in which the players tries to shoot or catch four (out of ten) domestic and/or international animals. According to the rules the game is intended for 4 or 5 players, but it is quite possible to be 2 or 3.

This is a fairly simple roll-and-go dice game. On each single track one animal is possible to shoot or catch with the help of the dice. If successful - that is if the dice rolls exactly the numbers needed - the hunter is awarded an animal card with varying victory points. Whoever manages to collect the most points wins.

Since alla players is supposed to go on the same animal in the same order, animal for animal, it is teorethically possible that player one shoots all four selected animals and thereby win even before player two (not to mention players three, four or five!) has had a go.

The animals and their value: Tiger (100), Elephant (75), Lion (50), Rhino (50), Capercaillie (40), Bear (30), Owl (30), Hyena (20), Crocodile (5) and Chimpanzee (5).

This game would probably be seen as politically incorrect nowadays and impossible to publish.

—user summary

Speed-Fighters is a motorsports game from Swedish publisher Aristospel.

A traditional roll-and-move dice game in which the players drives four heats of a motorsports contest. The first heat is called Racing, the second Speedway, the third Moto-Cross and the final heat Rally. (English names are of course more or less the same.)

During the four different heats a variety of things may happen - good or bad - depending on what square you're at.

The year 1965 is an approximation given after some correspondence with a relative of the designer and artist.

Holistic is an abstract sequencing game that combines set-collecting and trick-taking in a compact 81-card deck. Each card has a number from 1 to 9, and one of nine shapes. Win by taking the most quints, or placing the final matching card in a sequence.

The original name of this game is long lost and is mostly referred to as a holistic quint sequencing card game and is there for given the name Holistic for short. One text passage refers to the leviathan chain, but if this is the name of the game or some other principal is still unclear. A suggestion is that this game is better named Quint.

Holistic was according to Erik Magus Lång first played on scientific principles by a party of gentlemen who frequented the Holland House, Arkum Street, Hundon (Borough of St Edmundsbury) around 1728. but the game never became fashionable. To be a member of this group you had to write and published research, trivia games or myths of the souls journey, which is the cause records of it still exists today. The game was much later rediscovered and painstakingly recreated by a historian and game design researcher.

The game is party mention in the book “A Short Treatise on the Game of Yellow King” published in 1742, in “The Principles of holism and its Practice Illustrated on an Original System, by Means of Hands, Numbers and Symbols” published in 1862 and in the incomplete manuscripts and personal diary of the above mention Magus Lång. Holism is the belief that the parts of something are intimately interconnected and explicable only by reference to the whole, emphasizing the importance of the whole and the interdependence of its parts.

—description from the publisher

Even the longest nights do not last forever, a light will dawn on the horizon.
It is the end of what once was and the beginning of something new:
A Golden Age.

Golden Age is Buck: Legacy 2nd Edition's final expansion pack and features an incredible 27 cards, including:

Alicorn Race Cards!
Gauss Pony Race Cards!
Spirit Summoner Class Card!
Chronomancer Class Card!

—description from the publisher

Lunch Swap week has been declared! You and your friends gather around the table each day, trade lunches, try avoiding getting the worst lunch each day, while still accomplishing your dessert goals. At the end of the week a Lunch Swap winner is declared!  
The game is played over five rounds. On their turn a player plays a single card. Once each player has 5 cards in front of them the game is over. Players then tally their score to determine a winner.

—description from the designer

You are an adventurer setting out to find your fame and fortune in some fantastic adventure.  The only problem is, you don’t know where to find any adventures!  You have heard vague tales of various and sundry perilous exploits you might attempt, such as the Forbidden Forest, the Haunted Hamlet, and several others.  In order to find out more, you have halted your journey just outside the borders of your homelands in a tavern known for its unruly crowd of battle-worn adventurers, the Burning Phoenix Inn.

You hope to glean enough information from these experienced warriors and wizards to find out where to begin your adventures.  But someone knows what you are up to and is out to stop you.  Watch out for the mysterious Shadow Riders, trying to prevent you from going on your journey.

Burning Phoenix Inn is a set collection card game for 2-6 players for the whole family in which players will talk to inn patrons seeking rumors, gamble, and play other games like darts and arm wrestling.  It plays in about 30 minutes.

On their turn, players can play a card for its action, letting them interact with other players to gain other rumor cards, gamble by drawing cards and checking their gambling value to see if you win or lose, start a game such as darts or arm wrestling, or play a legendary adventurer card.

A legendary adventurer card may help free the player from Shadow Riders or let them draw cards, get money, or help them win playing darts at arm wrestling.

The games of darts, arm wrestling, and the drinking game are resolved by drawing and comparing cards to determine the winner.  Certain legendary adventurer cards can help a player win.

A player wins immediately when they have collected five matching rumor cards, five Shadow Riders, or has collected fifteen gold coins.

boardgame: Smash Up: The Bigger Geekier Box
description:
The box of the original Core Set could only accommodate the first 3 sets (up through The Obligatory Cthulhu Set), so The Big Geeky Box was created to accommodate more sets. But The Big Geeky Box will eventually run out of space, especially when you sleeve all the cards. Now The Bigger Geekier Box was designed to hold even more sets than The Big Geeky Box. It has four long troughs that can hold dozens of decks each and a closeable token holder, the previously-released Geeks and Smash Up All Stars factions with their corresponding bases. Also, unlike the Big Geeky Box, this set contains a comprehensive rule book compiling all the rules and card clarifications from all the sets created so far. The set also comes with dividers that name every faction made thus far and for Oops, You Did It Again. To accommodate partly full troughs, the box includes foam bricks that can be positioned anywhere along the troughs and will remain in place.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
Settlers arrived in a valley in the desert Colorado, San Luis Valley. They have to fight for everything, but eventually farms, rich grass pastures and pasturing cattles fill the unusual wasteland with life. 
Become the most skilled farmer! Get the most pastures for your cattle! With smart tactics, avoiding storms, using your golds well, with the Indians in peace, you will surely be the king of the prairie.

Objective of the game:
With the help of farms occupy areas and all five cows should be placed on occupied pastures. The first player places his fifth cow on the board wins the game.

How to play:
During the game, players must use pasture-shaped triangular cards for the game so that they can build farms in their corners. If one player is in the majority on a pasture - his own farm is in the two corners of the front leg - that player can place one of its cows on this pasture so can take it. The figure remains in the game until the end of the game, regardless of whether the player may have it the majority of the area around the area disappears on the way. Only one cow can be placed on a pasture.

The youngest player starts. From the 4 cards in his hand he can play as much as cards he can or want. There is only one limit: You can only play one card type in one turn (for example, there is a player holding two farm cards, only one of them can be placed in one turn). Exception is possible with golds.

The Holidays are a time for family and cheer, but we know that you're tired of that. So here's a holiday expansion to Superfight that mixes horribly offensive things with whimsical all-ages cards. Be warned though, the naughty cards in here are not for children.
WARNING: This deck contains XXX-Rated cards that are not appropriate for children.

—description from the publisher

Blue Deck 2 adds 100 more location cards to Superfight. Use these cards to take your fights to all kinds of crazy new places, like a hot air balloon, the Batcave, or a shark-infested swimming pool.

—description from the publisher

How long would you survive against a horde of infinite four-year-olds? Which fighter could sell the most girl scout cookies? The Challenge Deck 2 contains these and more in a set of 100 new challenge cards to test the limits of your imagination.

The Challenge Deck 2 is a modifier deck, and thus requires an additional game deck in order to be played.

—description from the publisher

From Alexander Hamilton and Harriet Tubman to Vlad the Impaler, the greatest heroes and villains from human history are ready to fight in this 100-card Superfight expansion. So get ready for the most absurd historical debates you've ever had!

—description from the publisher

Ancient dragons, powerful wizards, and deadly quests await you in this high-fantasy themed expansion for Superfight. Grab your wand and your sword and prepare for the most epic verbal battles you've ever encountered!

—description from the publisher

WHASSUP! A 100-card Superfight expansion full of dope 90's cards? And it comes with locations and scenarios too? Off the hook! All that and a bag of okay yeah you get it.

—description from the publisher

Gnarly cards, Brah! This Deck's got a totally righteous set of '80s cards to remind you of how rad things were back in the day. Go ahead and mix this deck with any of your other decks or play it on its own!

—description from the publisher

Purple Deck 2 adds 100 new scenario cards to Superfight from "LITERALLY RAINING MEN" to "FIGHTERS HAVE CARTOONISHLY LARGE HEADS" and more! Use these cards before or after fighters have been drawn, or as tie breakers in close fights.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Superfight: The Core Deck – Expansion Two
description:
100 New general cards for Superfight. New characters include: MEGASHARK, JEFF GOLDBLUM, and HERCULES. New attributes include: CAN SUMMON AND COMMAND SQUIRRELS, HAS GIANT MAGNETS FOR HANDS, and ARMED WITH A BAG OF INFINITE BEAR TRAPS.

—description from the publisher

New gameplay mode for Superfight: Argue to defend the most ridiculous fortress you can imagine from invaders.

—description from the publisher

Sea-themed game encourages kids to “catch” combinations of 10! Players take turns flipping cards to show numbers and shout out "I see 10!" when they see combinations of numbers that together make 10. Collect the most 10s and win! Beware of the Hungry Shark cards-pull one and your whole catch goes back!

This is an elementary wargame for beginners, included in Command Magazine Japan extra "Wargame Handbook 2017".

Covering the battle of Tabaruzaka (田原坂) in 1877, a climactic battle in the Satsuma Rebellion (西南戦争), Jan-Sep 1877.
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Tabaruzaka

The game uses a game system similar to Series: Panzer Corps (Epoch / Command Japan).
The victory is decided by how many hexes of main road (街道) each side controls at the end.
family: History: Boshin War & Meiji Era Rebellions

description:
"It is a truth universally acknowledged, that a single man in possession of a good fortune, must be in want of a wife." —Pride and Prejudice

It's the Regency Era, and you've set out to make the perfect match! Search for a love who is enthralled by your accomplishments and is agreeable to your financial situation. Forge good relationships with friends and family, then secure lasting affection with acts of courtship. Who will tie the knot first?

A Universal Truth is a strategic game of courtship set in the Regency era.  The game features include dice and card drafting, hand and pool management, tableau building, set collection, multi-use card combos, risk management, asymmetric abilities, and over two hundred unique characters from the period.

"Marriage is indeed a maneuvering business." Are you up for the challenge?

—description from the publisher

The War in Vietnam is a relatively simple yet accurate portrayal of the Vietnam War that features two decks of event cards, an area map of Southeast Asia (and parts of North Vietnam), deployment cards (for easy set-up), and dozens of specific and unique game piece sculptures at the regimental level in most cases (everything from mortars to aircraft carriers), plus special and optional rules and bonus material like a computer version and a strategy guide.

The game can be played as individual scenarios spanning different periods of the war or as the entire Vietnam War on an area map with ~800 miniatures of air, land, and sea units of all combatants in a set-up designed to be playable in a few weekends.
