boardgame: Land of Confusion Volume 2: The Battle for Luneburg Heath
description:
If the “balloon had gone up” in Cold War Europe during the 1980s an offensive by the Warsaw Pact and Soviet forces across the North German Plain in West Germany would occur. The central sector of the NATO front here was held by the British Army of the Rhine (BAOR) supported by West German forces.  The terrain along the frontier with East Germany was dominated by the Luneberg Heath, an area marked by swamps, rivers and forested terrain that favored the defenders. Once through here, however, the terrain opened up to flat land that stretched to the Dutch and Belgian borders. A breakthrough here by the Communist forces would also certainly lead to a collapse of any NATO defense of West Germany, and a likely defeat by the forces of the Free World.

Can you do as well or better than your historical counterparts? Learn and enjoy!

Each copy of Land of Confusion volume 2: The Battle for Luneburg Heath is composed of the following:

•    Two (one east, one west) 11 by 17 inch map sections.
•    112 un-mounted, double sided units.
•    One Players’ Aid Sheet.
•    Eight pages of rules.
family: Series: Standard Deck Activation System (High Flying Dice Games)

description:
2 or 4 players play the soccer game. All other players are betting on the outcome of the game, using the Toto-slip.

The pawns are placed on the spaces marked with an "A". Movement is only forward and is determined by the roll of a die. No pawn may land on a red space. A goal is achieved by entering the opposite goal with a pawn. If a pawn gets on a space that is occupied by an opposite pawn, the opposite pawn is taken from the board. It may reenter the game on an "A"-space by the roll of a 6. If a 6 isn't used to bring back a pawn, it can be used to move a pawn and roll again.

The game ends, when there is no pawn left on the playing field. The team with the most pawns in the opposite goal wins the game.

boardgame: Chronicles of Frost: All That Burns
description:
Steeped in the lore of Mistfall and Heart of the Mists, Chronicles of Frost: All That Burns introduces a new villain, a new hero, and a new mode of play: the cooperative campaign!

With 110 new cards, All That Burns allows you to play a string of five scenarios, unlock new abilities for your heroes, and face one of the iconic villains of Aestemyr: Sigraed the Mistflame. Face off against a potent flamecaster mage driven insane by her lust for revenge, then corrupted and reforged into a living weapon by the ravenous Mists. Foil her plans to set the world ablaze, desperately fight to loosen her grip on Valskyrr, and finally stand against her in battle as legendary heroes of the land.

Once brought down and redeemed, Sigraed is also a playable hero in All That Burns. Beat the cooperative campaign to add Sigraed to your hero roster, or simply play her from the start. After all, the Mists corrupt all, and their twisting power has made adventurers face shadows of their former comrades — or even twisted copies of themselves.

—description from the publisher

The players start at München. Movement is determined by one or two dice. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first player to arrive at space 102 (Rheinfall) wins the game.

The game was published about 1912.

1. Lustige Fahrt ins Schlaue
Each player gets 10 game token and a car to place on the starting-space. A quiz-card is placed on each grey space and a event-card on each red space. The movement of the cars is determined by a die. If a car lands on a pace neighbouring an event-card, the player has to follow the instructions printed on the card. If a car lands on a space besides a quiz-card the player has to answer the question on the card. If the answer is correct, he collects the card. All used cards are imediatly replaced by new ones.

The first player to reach the finish space receives 6 game token, the second gets 3 token. The game ends, when the second player reaches the finsh. All token and quiz-cards count for 1 point each. The player with the most points wins the game.

2. Quiz-Karussell (backside of the game board)

a) 6er-Karussell: The quiz master takes 40 quiz-cards and 10 event-cards from the deck and places 1 card on each colored space. The player rolls a die and has to answer the question on the quiz-card or follow the instruction on the event-card beside the rolled number. The player may chose betweent two permissible cards because there are two spaces for each number. The game ends, when all 50 cards have been used. Points are awarded for token and collected quiz-cards same as in the game "Lustige Fahrt ins Schlaue"

b) 12er-Karussell: In this game the player rolls a die (1-6) and can't chose between two cards. If he don't knows the answer to a quiz-card he has to pay a token and roll a second time. The second result is added to the first one to determine a second card. If he can't answer the question on the second card, there is no penality involved and his turn ends.

Many of the quiz- and event-cards are traffic related.

The King is old. The kingdom is divided and the great Wizard Morcai is mustering an army atop Fire Castle. From every town in the land the call goes out for heroes to take up the quest for the Five Weapons of Power. Ancient artifacts of immense power capable of giving their wielder the power to be...king! You must heed this call but your quest will not be easy. You must leave your home with only a small supply of modest provisions and brave the Overland's dangers as you seek news and information of the Five Weapons of Power. Along the way you will brave harsh weather, get lost in unfamiliar terrain, encounter powerful and sometimes hostile strangers, enter new towns, visit great castles, make loyal friends and do deeds that will make you a legend. And only if you survive all that will you be worthy to vie for one of the Five Weapons of Power.

Journey To The Overland is a solo tabletop role playing game where you assume the role of a lone traveler journeying throughout the Overland in search of Five Weapons of Power. Play begins in your hometown which will be determined by the race you choose for your character – human, dwarf, elf or native Overlander. From there you will determine your character starting characteristics, starting equipment and first daily action. Each day you must select a daily action that will either involve traveling, resting, working, training or visiting towns and castles to obtain news and information with the ultimate goal being to track down one of the five legendary weapons with the power to make you King!

In this revised 2nd Edition of Journey To The Overland the core rulebook has been completely reorganized for easier reference.  The rules themselves have been expanded to include all new content for Kings Harbor (News & Information, Tavern, Audience with the Harbormaster); new taverns, an expanded supply and equipment table, new distinct News and Information for each town or castle; optional Encumbrance rules to replace the seldom used 'weight' rules; new jobs; a new race (Halflings), a new section on 'Feats', new NPCs, an expanded Ocean Encounter Table, revised rules for falling unconscious and new audience tables for Audiences with King Overlord and the King Thorond of Dwarfhaven.

—description from the publisher

Fang den Hut: Das Kartenspiel is a card game version of Trap the Cap. Players can either claim a stack or play a card. A card can be played on a stack if it is the same number or if it shares a color with the top card and is higher. A stack can be claimed if it is a player's turn and his card is on top of stack. After all cards have been played, points are scored based on the number of cards players have claimed. The player with the most points wins.
____________
This is not a separately sold game . These 33 cards are an addition to the Classic edition of Fang den Hut!. They only were included in this special 2005 version !

There was a moment when the proud Japanese, who had been humiliated and treated like second-class citizens by the StateEmpire for far too long, rose up against their oppressors and claimed their freedom. The Japanese Uprising was a brief but terribly intense and savage conflict for which the Japanese paid a high price in blood for their victory but finally managed to tear away from Yu Jing. After the Uprising, the Japanese Secessionist Army, or JSA, has been reformulated based on the figure of the servo-powered samurais, as natural leaders both in spirit and in combat. However, the JSA maintains its nature of being primarily an assault force, because the Bushido doctrine requires no less.

A box with 9 new and incredible miniatures belonging to JSA, a Japan completely independent of Yu Jing, part of the Non-Aligned Armies (NA2). It includes an introductory manual, scenery, dice... everything you need to start with Infinity or expand your collection!

—description from the publisher

This is a new expansion book that will mark a before and after in the history of the Infinity Universe. Through the long-awaited JSA and Mercs, the Infinity background goes forward in a way unexpected for many. The Japanese Uprising and their schism from Yu Jing bring forth the Non-Aligned Armies (NA2), a new faction that encompasses JSA, plus StarCo, the Ikari Company, and the Druze Bayram Security, the new Mercenary Companies. Also, Infinity Uprising contains all the Dire Foes, with new material that reveals how their stories are linked and contributed to the Japanese Uprising. After Infinity Uprising, the Infinity Universe will never be the same!

This is a new book for Infinity that provides all the details about the new Non-Aligned Armies (NA2), including the mercenary companies StarCo, the Ikari Company, and the Druze Bayram Security. It also contains first-hand information regarding the Japanese Uprising and the fate of the JSA.

In this book, you will also find the background of all the Dire Foes and their seven new, ready-to-play scenarios, in normal or narrative mode and adapted to the ITS format, and the Sectorial Army Charts of the new JSA and the new mercenary companies.

A bloody war between two mighty kingdoms has reached an impasse. Each ruler now turns to the northern tribes in an attempt to form an alliance, and turn the tides. Will you be able to capture the favor of the Four Tribes?

In Four Tribes, you and one other player will be placing cards, according to the rules, to either side of a row of six village cards. Most of these cards you are placing represent provisions that each village is lacking. Your goal is to appease the elder of each village more than your opponent by giving them higher ranked goods.

In addition to provisions, there are a host of special cards that you may employ that might be defensive in nature, or be a devastating blow to your opponent. Hand-management and timing is the key!

Four Tribes is a game of strategy and tactics that is balanced with a bit of luck. Each player draws from their own deck of cards, but each player has the same deck, so while you may draw on the weak-side early on, you will know that powerful cards are coming up that you can capitalize on.

Four Tribes can be setup, played, and put away in about 30 minutes, making it a great lunchtime or filler game. I specifically designed this game to be a game that I can play with my wife and since then have heard from dozens of couples who play Four Tribes regularly.

This newest edition takes everything that was loved about the original version, that was very successful on KickStarter, and boils it down and redevelops it into something that feels much like the original, but is new at the same time.

Contents

28 Card Orange Army Deck 28 Card Purple Army Deck 6 Double-sided Standard Village Cards 6 Double-sided Winter Village Cards 6 Fire Expansion Cards 6 Flood Expansion Cards 3 Longhouse Cards 1 Blizzard Token 12 Village Elder Pawns 42 Wooden Houses (10 each of Blue, Green, Red, & Yellow, and 2 Black) 1 drawstring bag Instruction Booklet

You are a Privateer in an alternate history Steampunk world, sailing the seas of New Caribbean for wealth and fame. The Royal family, no longer interested in directly governing, have decided to appoint a Governor. Through piracy, pillaging and politics will you amass Loyalists and take your place as leader of New Caribbean.

Plunderous is a game of area control, resource management, hand building, dice manipulation and negotiation. Each round you must strategically assemble a unique Crew to carry out your orders, navigate and explore the islands, manipulate resources and politics, parley with other Privateers, complete personal Patron requests and defend and conquer Ports to acquire resources, influence, rewards and Loyalists. The Privateer with the most Loyalists at the end of the game will be appointed the first Governor of New Caribbean by the Royal family.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Warhammer 40,000: Heroes of Black Reach – Cassius and Kragott Promo
description:
This promo was only available through purchasing the Feb 2018 issue of White Dwarf magazine.

This promo includes the tiles required to add two new characters:
- Cassius: Master of Sanctity (Space Marines - Ultramarines)
- Kragott: Wannabe Boss (Orks)

Text says:

Flexible
Discard your private petition. Draw a new petition from the deck.

Play an influence card

2 copies of this card were included inside AEG's version of Patronize

Both cards were included inside AEG's version of Patronize

Shipment: 
Auction the top card of the shipment deck without looking at it

Fleeting: 
Severin & Odette
Each player with an agent assigned to Skulduggery loses two crowns. Remove all ages from Skullduggery and place them in the unassigned agents area

No Thumbs Challenge is the hilarious game that calls for fast-moving teamwork creative thinking and maximum dexterity as you and your team members try to execute simple tasks without the use of your thumbs! Using thumb straps, players “attach” their thumbs to their palms and then try to perform tasks like opening a water bottle, applying lip balm, or tying a tie.

boardgame: Mexican Standoff: The Black Scorpion Promo Card
description:
JEFE ESCORPIÓN ALFREDO is the wicked wraith of the infamous bandit JEFE ESCORPIÓN NEGRO appearing in the base game of Mexican Standoff. Due to his unpredictable and testy personality only the bravest dare to enter into an alliance with him.

Instead of shuffling into the deck, put the promo card on the table within easy reach. Before playing the first card of the round the player who was dealt Jefe Escorpión Negro of the base game may replace Jefe Escorpión Negro with Jefe Escorpión Alfredo (the one with the black background).

The promo card has two special features:

-    After losing a duel in which the promo card was played as a base card the player may retrieve the promo card and put it back into his hand by discarding one card from his hand. The promo card may not be retrieved if the player has no cards left to discard.
-    The player who won the card of Jefe Escorpión Alfredo with a trick, scores 25 bonus points. In case the player who got the card originally cannot keep it by taking a trick, i.e. another player takes it, the original owner of the card gets -25 points.

After each round replace the promo card with the card of Jefe Escorpión Negro of the base game, put the promo card back on the table and reshuffle the deck to start a new round.

Tjugo vita kossor ("Twenty White Cows") or, formally, Fäbodsspelet Tjugo vita kossor ("The Chalet Game of Twenty White Cows")is a fairly simple roll-and-move game wherein the players try to catch as many white cows as possible. There is different ways to go between the start and the goal and on some spots things will happen - delivered in rhymes - that means additional or fewer cows. Artist were famous Swedish designer Bertil Almqvist (he is also probably the designer of the game).

The publication year is an approximation, estimated on biographic, historic and artistic grounds.

Sverige-loppet is a bicycle racing game played in segments (or heats) from Haparanda in northern Sweden to Ystad in the south, via Stockholm and Huskvarna and a few other places. There is also penalty rounds in the form of diversions. During the race things will happen, and they are delivered in rhymes. Winner is calculated on somewhat complicated mathematical grounds. It must be considered modern for its age.

The game actually has a real life original race as inspiration, from which it also borrowed its name, Sverigeloppet. (Note that Aristospel calls the game Sverige-loppet (with an hyphen) while in real life (see Community Wiki section) the name was Sverigeloppet (in one word). Both means "The Sweden Race".

Bland Blommor och Blad ("Among Flowers and Leafs") is an educational game that via dice rolls and set collecting intends to teach the latin names of domestic Swedish flowers. Collecting flowers and learning their names was a compulsory school subject (and considered very important) on the national curriculum in Sweden up to about the 1960's.

This fact, and also that the name of the artist - I-L Biltman - appears on the box makes it fairly safe to date the game to about 1955.

Pussla i kapp ("Jigsaw Puzzle Contest") is a very simple game from Swedish publisher Aristospel, but the idea was probably not theirs originally. The idea is to be first to complete your puzzle. There is four different motifs to choose from.

From the simple components (only paper) and some of the puzzle motifs and the make of the Aristospel logotype it is safe to assume that this is a war era game.

The persons on the lid might very well be real life persons, but they have not been identified.

Another branch in the Silhouette-Family of Games. This time the cards are substituted with dice, which shows different silhouettes on their faces.
Six Variants to play with them. Common denominator is that you try to come up with a word that combine sthe images of two dice thrown.

This board-game is inspired to the famous Italian tv-show, which aired in the '90s.

It's a simple quiz game about traditional Italian proverbs.

Italian slogan: 
« Io so chi sei, e se tu vuoi, ti dico che destino avrai, buona sorte o guai.
Luna Nera sì o no lo vedremo fra un po' con le carte che la Zingara ti fa »

Koncern ("Company Group") or, formally, Aktiespelet Koncern ("The Company Group Stock Game") was Aristospel's contribution to the financial games of Sweden in the period 1950 to 2010. Alga held the licence for Monopol and also published their own Finans. From Lemeco came Kapital and Kärnan would eventually publish Börsspelet/Aktiespelet while Olsen decided to publish Yuppie. A similar game was Marketing from Tre Ringar and also Invest from Harlekin. And this is not the definitive list! All in all, a self-respecting Swedish games publisher needed a financial game.

Koncern is the usual Monopoly-like game were players move around by dice rolls and buys and sells stocks and bonds etc. The publishing year is an estimate made on company historic grounds (the companies that features in the game, that is).

An interesting feature is that the companies in this game were to an high degree part of the Handelsbanken sphere of influence, while competitor Lemeco's contemporary game Kapital connected to the sphere of S-E-Banken.

Auktionsspelet ("The Auction Game") from Swedish publisher Aristospel is a WWII era game in which the players is given some money and then participates in an auction.

The winner is the player with the most numbers of complete sets of bought items. The auction items is a deck of cards. The game also includes rules, money and a small, very handsome, auctioneer's gavel. All in a small box, that together with the old Aristospel logotype and the absence of expensive or complicated components, makes it fairly easy to date the game to the war years.

Designer and artist is, sadly, not known.

Saxtorpsloppet ("The Saxtorp Race") is a motorcycle racing and betting game from Swedish publisher Alga. It is one of Alga's first games ever.

It has an unusual mechanic since one person handles all six motorcycles and an infinite number of participants can take part and place bets. (It is also possible to be only sex players, each with one motorcycle and in that case it is a normal racing game.)

The game consists of six small wooden motorcycles (with drivers), each mounted on a small sheet of carton, some playing card-size pieces to lay out as the track, and the rules. The track pieces have numbers on both sides, 1 to 6 in different random orders.

When playing the track pieces is laid out in a straight fashion in a random order (which makes the number of possible tracks very high) and one playing piece (motorcycle) is choosen to start. That piece will, when placed on the side of the first track piece, point towards one of the sex numbers (digits) and that motorcycle is then moved along the first. Now the second motorcycle will point towards a number, and so a third motorcycle is moved forward (pointing to a number). And so it goes on.

Eventually one of the already moved motorcycles will be moved a track piece ahead and point to another number on that track piece. In a few moves' time all six motorcycles will be engaged in the race, "promoting" another (or ocassionally itself) to further pieces on the track. Winner is the playing piece (motorcycle) that reaches the last track piece first. The track pieces are double-sided so the race takes place along 24 track sections.

The first in a three module suite for use with the Advanced Tobruk System, depicting small-unit actions in the Pacific Theater during WWII.

Leatherneck I contains three 11 x 16" non-historic geomorphic maps as well as two new map overlays.  All of the combat and special counters needed for play are included (one is expected to already have ATS system markers) on five half-sized countersheets.  Finally, there are eight new scenarios, all of which employ the provided maps and counters, as well as two sheets of AFV Cards.

Gamers must also own the ATS System Rules and playaids to make use of these products.

boardgame: Architects of the West Kingdom: Extra Buildings
description:
These 3 additional cards were a free promo for the 2018 Kickstarter campaign as part of the Architects of the West Kingdom 2018 Promo.

Museum - Gain 1 Virtue at the end of the game for every 2 Buildings you have constructed.

Graveyard - Immediately discard 1 of your hired Apprentices.

Crane - Immediately place another Worker in the Guildhall to either construct another Building or advance work on the Cathedral.
family: Series: The West Kingdom Trilogy (Garphill Games)

boardgame: Architects of the West Kingdom: Extra Apprentices
description:
3 additional cards released during the 2018 Kickstarter as part of the Architects of the West Kingdom 2018 Promo.

Peddler - Gain 2 Silver on each Black Market Reset.

Overseer - Always treat the King's Storehouse as if you had 1 additional Worker there.

Enforcer - Gain 1 Gold when sending all captured Workers to Prison.
family: Series: The West Kingdom Trilogy (Garphill Games)

boardgame: Architects of the West Kingdom: Extra Player Boards
description:
5 additional player boards released during the 2018 Kickstarter as part of the Architects of the West Kingdom 2018 Promo.

Lothaire - Pay 1 less Wood or Stone when advancing work on the Cathedral or constructing a Building.

Clovis - Gain 1 Building card anytime you construct a Building.

Bertha - Gain 2 additional Silver when sending all captured Workers to Prison.

Fara - You may release 1 of your imprisoned Worker during each Black Market Reset.

Hugo - You may spend 2 Silver at any time to avoid taking a Debt.
family: Series: The West Kingdom Trilogy (Garphill Games)

boardgame: Raiders of the North Sea: Outsiders
description:
3 new cards released during the 2018 Kickstarter for Architects of the West Kingdom.

These 3 cards feature characters from Architects that can be added into Raiders of the North Sea. Outsider cards introduce a new crew mechanism: Once hired, players may activate the play ability of an Outsider card at the Town Hall, rather than playing a card from their hand. Doing so does not require them to discard the Outsider from their crew.

Swindler - Gain 1 Gold or 3 Silver
Trickster - Draw 1 Card and play 1 Card
Squire - Gain 1 Armour
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Series: The North Sea Trilogy (Garphill Games)

description:
Sussed! is a personality quiz game.

Your aim is to score the most points by answering questions about other players' likes and dislikes, reactions to things, wishes and opinions. To start, all you need is something for each player to write with. It only takes a few minutes to learn.

Players take it in turns to read a question on their card and the three possible answers out loud. They then secretly choose the answer that best suits them and other players attempt to guess which they chose for points.

The winner is the player with the most points after everyone has asked all 5 questions on their cards.

Talisman (Revised 4th Edition): The Lost Realms combines the two small expansions The Nether Realm and The Deep Realms in one set.

Journey through the realm of Talisman on your quest for the Crown of Command but beware the fiery Pyrochanter, fearsome Titan Wraith, and other Nether Deck enemies that seek to thwart your plans in The Nether Realm expansion. The Nether Realm — designed by Jon New, the man behind the Talisman-dedicated fansite Talisman Island - offers three new alternative endings for Talisman, along with new Nether cards. Fate also plays a part in this expansion, helping against some of the larger foes found in the Nether deck, but it could also prove to be your downfall.

Talisman: The Deep Realms allows you to travel between the Dungeon and the City regions by venturing into the dark, dank, and haunted passages that connect them. You can cross through the two corpse-filled and rat-infested Deep Realms by means of a perilous bridge or venture even deeper into an ancient system of tunnels inhabited by rats, feral pets, and much worse monsters. In the gloomy and perilous Deep Realms you will certainly encounter tremendous danger, but you may also find tantalizing wealth — or even a Talisman.

Enclose is played on a 19by19 toroidal Go board with Go stones. If a player encloses an area with their stones they capture all the enemy stones in that area. Players may not place their stones onto intersections on area that is enclosed by their opponent. In order for a stone formation to enclose area, the stone formation must be able to have imagined a string with the shape of a polygon connecting the centers of all of its stones so that each center is connected by the string to exactly two other centers of adjacent enclosing formation stones, and that string must divide the toroid into exactly two regions so that it is not possible to move from one region to the other without going through the string. Of these two regions of the toroid that are divided by the string, the one that is enclosed is the one which has a combined value of the measures of the angles of its side (its side being at all points adjacent to the string), that is smaller than that of the other region. Players may pass their turn without placing a stone, if both players pass this way consecutively, the game ends, and the winner is the player who has the most empty intersections enclosed.

boardgame: AFFLICTION: Salem 1692 – Tactics Card Add-on Pack
description:
This Kickstarter bonus pack of eight cards (plus one rule card) gives players the option to purchase colonist enhancements.

The eight cards are shuffled and two are turned face up. A player may send a messenger to assign a tactic card to a colonist.

Cards include such traits as Opposition, Rumors, Political Maneuvering, Petition, Rousing Speech, and Pardon from the Governor.

They were initially added to Kickstarter copies of the base game in a separate package. They are still handed out by the publisher at conventions.

Panic Mode! is a cooperative Disaster Recovery card game for up to 8 players. In 25 minutes your doors will open for business, your website will start to hit peak hours, newspapers will be published, blogs will be posted and it will be another day. The problem is your systems are down!

The Mainframe, Morale and Customer Satisfaction tracking cards start at zero. You and your team must work together to recover all three to their safe zones by playing cards. Cards will either increment or decrement one or more tracking cards, and cards may align with your assigned role to have an additional effect.

If players can get all tracking cards to their safe zones in 25 minutes, they win!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Combining elements of RPGs, deck-building, and traditional board games, the Mage Knight Board Game puts you in control of one of seven powerful Mage Knights as you explore (and conquer) a corner of the Mage Knight universe under the control of the Atlantean Empire. Build your army, fill your deck with powerful spells and actions, explore caves and dungeons, and eventually conquer powerful cities controlled by this once-great faction! In competitive scenarios, opposing players may be powerful allies, but only one will be able to claim the land as their own. In cooperative scenarios, the players win or lose as a group. Solo rules are also included.

Mage Knight: Ultimate Edition contains the base game; The Lost Legion, Shades of Tezla, and Krang Character expansions; comprehensive integrated rules text; five new cards; and miniatures with alternate paint jobs.

description:
Basketball Duel is a fast card game for 1/2/4 players that simulates a basketball game. Players attack and defend in turns and following basketball possession rules. It is an action/reaction game, with bluffing, hand management and luck.

Cards are divided in two parts, an attack action and a defensive action. The basic layer of the game is using colors, each attack has a color and defensive cards cancel one or more colors. But there is much more to it, you are able to move to different positions, some cards have effects when or when not canceled. Most basketball elements are simulated too: rebounds, fast breaks, interceptions, fouls, bad shots, dunks, blocks...

Each round feels different with all the possible combinations and players have few important decisions to take in each turn that can change completely the result of each round.

Basketball Duel thread for contest

—description from the designer

Dinosaur Island: Totally Liquid adds components needed to play Dinosaur Island with up to five players, in addition to adding water dinosaurs to the base game, new parkboard extensions, executive worker meeples that give unique worker abilities to each player, a blueprints module that rewards players when they build certain park layouts, PR events that create hidden scoring opportunities, and more.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
You are Eldenia's hero and adventurer. They prepare to go on adventure by polishing swords and purchasing items in the village. If you're ready, let's go hunting monsters!

In order to gain more fame, you will compete fiercely with the adventurers you join. Sometimes it helps and sometimes it is a stumbling block. Let's be a stronger warrior and defeat the dragon!

In World of Eldenia, each player starts with level 1 first. As you level up your experience(=Power) by defeating a small monster like a rabbit, you can become a more powerful hero. However, you may lose points because of the injuries you suffer from defeating monsters. Do not forget to treat!

New adventurers and heroes may be needed to play more games. For more fun, Eldenia is a constant adventurer.

—description from the designer Yun Young-jin.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: End of the Trail: Outlaw Mini-Expansion
description:
A mini-expansion consisting of an additional face card, the Outlaw, an Outlaw meeple, and four new, optional Joker cards.

As face cards, Outlaws rank above 10 and below Jack.  When an Outlaw is played while Prospecting, players place the Outlaw meeple on any tile or, if already in play, move the Outlaw to a new tile. At game end, the tile containing the Outlaw meeple scores only half its value in gold.

Each Outlaw card features an illustration based on historic outlaws of the California Gold Rush, chosen by backers of the Kickstarter Project.  These include: Joaquin Murrieta, Eleanor Dumont, Black Bart, and Jack Powers.  Illustrations for the Jokers were likewise selected by backers and include depictions of a card shark and a town drunk.

The mini expansion is packaged separately from the base game and will be available to non-Kickstarter backers.

Players draft dice in an attempt to create the ultimate beer flight.  Dice represent beer color, primary flavor, and glassware. Each player has a flight paddle with dice slots for four beers. Drafted dice are placed in the appropriate slots. Grab as many victory coasters as you can along the way.

—description from the designer
A quiz game about handball.

boardgame: Railways of the World: Nippon Expansion
description:
This entry is for the expansion. It has game components needed to play this map with a Railways of the World base set. Railways of Nippon is the full version that is a stand-alone game.
—user summary

Railways of the World: Nippon Expansion expands Railways of the World with a new map of Japan designed by the Japanese game designer Hisashi Hayashi. The game takes place during Japan's rapid industrial growth during the Meiji era, where you take the role of one of the entrepreneurial railroad baron of that time.

You plan and lay track, upgrade your trains, sell goods to distant cities, and use operation cards to become the richest railroad baron of Japan! Balance your budget wisely, since taking too many bank bonds can ruin your business over the course of the game. Also, watch the competition closely because you can only use the track that you laid down personally to trade.

Since the island of Japan is mountainous and narrow there is often not enough space for everyone to lay their track where they want. Foreseeing your competitors moves and well thought out moves will ensure you an undisputed railroad empire!
—description from the publisher

In I'll Take the Case you are an attorney and take cards into your hand trying to gather clients, evidence, witnesses and paperwork to solve cases available to all players at the table. The more difficult the case you solve, the more reputation you gain. When all the cases are solved, the attorney with the highest reputation wins!

Each card in the player's hands has law related trivia or facts based relevant to Bar Exam questions. Great as a learning-break for law students or kids interested in the law.

Players are given a starting hand and a selection of cases are available on the table for anyone to solve. By taking a single action during rotating turns, players either take a card, perform an action card, or solve a case. With a hand limit, and ability to take cards from the discard, there is strategy in what card you may or may not want to discard.

Cases require a mix of evidence, paperwork and witness cards to solve. Collect the correct mix in your hand and you can solve the case and gain the reputation. When all the cases are solved, the player with the most reputation wins!

-description from designer

Have you heard the rumour that's been running around lately? It seems that the Black Baronet, one of the most fearsome pirates, died and hid his treasures on the Skull’s floating islands. Hurry up and steer the ship to get your hands on the loot, as you might run into some greedy freebooters.
​
Move pirates from island to island to find treasures or pick a special action. Victory belongs to the player who discovers first three treasures or who eliminates the opposing crew. A captivating, straightforward game with a surprising level of strategy.

1973 was possibly the nearest that the world came to World War Three. Just following the U.S. withdrawal from Vietnam, and during the height of the Watergate scandal, the Americans were both war weary and disillusioned. The stock market crash of that year only made the situation worse, and when the Yom Kippur War erupted in October, the Israelis began to prepare thirteen of their nuclear weapons to halt the invading Arab armies! In response, the Soviet Union threatened to intervene, thereby causing the United States to alert its forces worldwide to prepare for a war against Russia. Such a war would have occurred when the United States was the most vulnerable and divided, but, ironically, the Americans would have had a vast reservoir of combat veterans from the Vietnam War to commit to action.

The Dangerous Year: 1973 uses a standard area movement system but includes a deck of 54 event cards that add a chaotic element to the game, such as "Denmark Withdraws from NATO" which prevents units from entering the Denmark space that turn, or "Hotline" which allows a player to prevent his opponent from conducting tactical nuclear attacks for the current game turn, and so forth. Event cards represent the various occurrences that could happen in the midst of a sudden war, and they ensure that this game gives the feeling of World War III. One moment, a U.S. aircraft carrier may be sunk by a Soviet submarine, but the next moment, a Soviet ballistic missile may explode on the launch pad. The randomness of a shuffled deck each game ensures that no two games are the same, and there is no perfect strategy...only a sudden execrable war of the worst variety.

This game uses a very simple combat system that is designed to resolve combat quickly, and includes the full panoply of combat units, armor, mech, infantry, artillery, SAMs, ships, fighters, bombers, et cetera, and not to mention the inveterate threat of tactical nuclear weapons. One thing is for sure, if the players do not trigger Armageddon, casualties will nevertheless be high for both sides.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
1859: The Railroad Card Game is a pure card game, comprised solely of 72 “protean pieces of pasteboard.” In that year, the railroad industry in the United States was still in its infancy. Your simple goal is to be a better railroad baron than your opponent. You do this by winning contracts to build track which connects one USA city to another in a series of competitions. The major elements you need are materials, labor and cash, although interesting people (contacts, investors, etc.) and effects such as material shortages and labor unrest will come your way. Engaging in a little trickery won’t hurt you, either.

A game of 1859 is played over eleven competitions (rounds). At the beginning of each competition, one player will choose a card from two which show the possible routes to be contested. Each player has a hand of five cards.

After a player plays a card, a replacement is drawn from the deck, replenishing his hand back to five cards. After a number of competitions, usually eleven, the game ends. The player who earned the most profits wins the game.

—description from the designer

Be the last one in the game with this ever changing game of colors. As you give and take colors be careful not too collect to many or you are out of the game.

—description from the designer

The Human Experiment is a strategic card drafting game for 4-8 players in which a deadly extraterrestrial virus has invaded earth threatening to end all human life as we know it. Players take on the role of scientists working in an underground biosafety level 4 laboratory desperately trying to create a cure in this hidden team vs team game of social deduction.

A select number of players will be playing as Hosts, vessels for the virus, who are secretly trying to sabotage the mission and facilitate the spread of the virus on earth. The human players must use wits and cunning in order to deduce who is a Host and who they can trust before it is too late.

—description from the publisher

In Exodus: War of Gods you will assume the identity and power of one of the great gods. Fight to recover your followers and destroy your enemies in the battle to become the greatest and most powerful divinity of all time.

We started the game with 2 action cards in hand and 2 Villagers, 1 Fanatic and 1 Prophet as devotees, each with a value of 1, 2 and 3 devotional points respectively.

At the beginning of the turn we will draw a card from the common stock deck: if we draw a devotee we will be obliged to put it among our devotees and pass the turn; if it is an action card we can choose between playing one of the action cards of the hand or the ability of the god we represent.

Whoever gets to 15 points of devotion will win, who will be the last god with devotees, or who has more points of devotion at the end of the deck of actions
A dexterity game where you balance your frying pan on top of your 'grill' and then try to complete recipes by scooping food items into your pan without toppling it.

boardgame: The Gathering of the Four Buried Secrets: A Solo Adventure for a Cleric in Four Against Darkness
description:
A Chaos sorceress is on the loose.
Dark clouds gather over the countryside.
Undead monsters are everywhere.
And there's a buried treasure that must not fall into the wrong hands.

Buried Secrets is a Four Against Darkness adventure for a single third level cleric. It is part of the Gathering of the Four campaign -- a series of five scenarios, four to be played as stand-alone adventures with a single hero, and the last to be played with the four heroes united against a common enemy.

An Ultimate Werewolf Legacy: Replay Pack contains a new diary and an additional set of family cards for use with Ultimate Werewolf Legacy, thereby allowing you to use the components of the base game to play through the entire campaign again. Maybe different things will happen this time; maybe you won't be killed at the hands of your fellow villagers...

boardgame: Dracula's America: Shadows of the West – Forbidden Power
description:
Since Dracula's rise to power a shadow has swept across the nation, but nowhere is it darker than in the Deep South. Throughout the plantations, swamps, and cities, rumours abound of grotesque rituals, hooded figures, and bizarre creatures. Most terrifying of all, however, are the whispers of ancient magic - unspeakable arcane rituals and occult powers that can lead those who wield them towards mystical supremacy... or reduce them to gibbering wrecks.

This new supplement for Dracula's America: Shadows of the West introduces two new factions: the corrupt cultists of the Church of Dagon and the Salem Sisterhood, occult practitioners whose history dates back to the early Colonies. New stealth rules allow for all manner of sneaky and underhanded tactics, while expanded rules for arcane powers offer glory but could cost you your sanity. Alongside these are a host of new scenarios, Hired Guns, monsters, skills, and gear to challenge or assist those who dare venture into the Deep South of Dracula's America.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Angrath Minotaur Pirate
description:
Part of the Rivals of Ixalan line.
A Planeswalker deck that is ready for play out of the box, and can be used by new players as a starter deck. It revolves around red and black mana, with mainly pirates as creatures. 
The leader of the band is Angrath, a Planeswalker that inflicts damage on multiple opponents, resurrects pirates, and can take control of the opponent's creatures.

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Vraska Scheming Gorgon
description:
Part of the Rivals of Ixalan line.
A Planeswalker deck that is ready for play out of the box, and can be used by new players as a starter deck. It revolves around white and black mana, with mainly vampires as creatures. 
The leader of the band is Vraska, a Planeswalker that lends strength to her allies and destroy enemy creatures. She can also give the player's creatures the ability to win the game by inflicting damage on the opponent.

"You’re Stuck in the Muck and there’s only one way out … lay out a series of stepping stones and run your way across. Easier said than done, for in this game you are your own worst enemy … well OK, you’re the only player, so just try not to goof it up too much! A puzzling challenge for the analytical thinker – or a mindless diversion for the rest of us."

A solitaire game for the piecepack game system. Win by moving all four of your pawns safely over the Stepping Stones and back to their home tile. The game is played in two phases. The first phase uses the results of dice rolls to move and flip coins. When all the coins of a given color have been flipped, the die of that color is used in the second phase to move the pawn and coins of that color.

boardgame: The Valiant Card Game: Ninjak vs. The Valiant Universe
description:
The greatest heroes in the VALIANT UNIVERSE must team up to stop one of their own! NINJAK, MI-6's deadliest operative, has stolen an object of immense power and you must build up your power in order to survive against all the gadgets and threats he will throw at you while working towards your hidden agenda.

It won't be easy... are you up for the challenge?

Throughout the game, you will collect various tactics and artifacts that both make you more powerful so you can fight Ninjak while also earning you points towards your hidden agenda. The game ends after all of Ninjak's threats have been eliminated. You will need to work together with the other players to survive, but only the one who best completes their hidden objective will win the game.

Warning: Contains violent images.

—description from the publisher

In 2009, Avalon Games released it first Mini-Game, Graveyard Dash, and since then we have released a new Mini-Game every month.  Mini-Games can be anything—a simple dice game, card game, or print-to-play board game. Avalon has always published these simple but fun games to explorer different types of games and genre, often allowing us, the game designers, the opportunity to develop a game without the hassle of dealing with a full-blown game of complicated rules and components.

This month, Avalon is releasing our 200th Mini-Game and, as a reward for all the loyal fans, we are offering it for free.

S&G Dungeon is a simple but fun game of dungeon exploration.  Using the powerful S&G game engine, you can play several heroes as they delve into a dungeon full of danger and rewards.

Full expandable, and every few months this year we will offer new dungeon to explore and new heroes to play.

So, gather up your gear, get your sword and a few fools…um, friends, and go explore a dangerous hole in the ground.

User Extras:

This consists of three PDFs, the main, 68 page rulebook, the 38 page Book of Monsters and a sample adventure, Goblin Tower.

Inside Book:

Steel & Glory Dungeon is an expansion to the S&G system, allowing you to, as a group or through solo play, run through a random dungeon-like adventure. This RPG-light add-on to the system allows for you to advance your characters to higher levels and thus experience more dangerous adventures.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Steel and Glory System (Avalon games)

description:
Star-Hammer is a set of miniatures rules based on the "Star Wars" movies, using alterations to the Warhammer Ancient Battles rules, so you will need a copy to play them.

The rules are a tactical level game over the vastness of space.    It is a game of movement and not about getting your Squadrons facing the right direction (so no need to turn or wheel).  The Shooting Phase been removed and all combat takes place in the Hand to Hand Phase, because Fighters only can shoot at each other and cannot fire over the great distance of space.  Full Army Lists included, which includes; X-Wings, Tie Fighters, and the Millennium Falcon, Also statics for ships from the new movies (The Forces Awakens)

The author designed the rules to use his collection of “Hot Wheels Star Wars Starships” toys, through any Star Wars miniatures could be used.

—description from the publisher

(Star-Hammer is not based on, or associated with the video game Star Hammer)

You are a daring adventurer who has decided to head out on a dangerous voyage across the seven seas in search of treasure, islands, and fame!

Crossing oceans in a rowboat is difficult, and you will face hunger, sickness, and a plethora of unhappy dice rolls, but you will go on with no fear! It is a board game, after all.

After setting the amount of turns in the game, you start. Each turn is divided into three phases, Fate, Action, and Stats, in which you see if you lose items or gain items decide your course of action, and then see if you’re starving to death. If you loose all your health, you lose, but if you complete all the turns, you win!

The ideal game for the lonely, poor and speedy gamer!

—description from the designer

Kaukopartio is a competitive 2-4 player game based on the war between the Finns and the Soviets in World War 2.

In this game, the players control their Finnish squad in the Soviet territory. The game objective is to complete five missions (sabotage, capture a prisoner etc).

The players have to gather squad members and look for equipment. Each soldier has their individual skills. The skills and the equipment help to accomplish the specific missions. Missions are accomplished with dice.

During the search of the resources the players may confront random Soviet units. The enemy units force the players to do a diversion before they can continue their objectives.

The game ends when one of the players completes five missions.

A storytelling party game that encourages creative writing, inside jokes and laughter. Each player starts with a blank game sheet. Everyone completes the first starter sentence and folds over the top section to hide their response. Once all players have finished the game sheets are passed to the left. Once all sections have been completed, players take it in turns to read their unique story. There are no winners or losers. It's a fun game anyone can play.

Soul Puzzler - Turbo Edition is the All-in-One Expansion for Soul Puzzler, a 2-player, head-to-head, tile placement puzzle game inspired by the puzzle-based video games of the 90's to now. Soul Puzzler - Turbo Edition is easily accessible to all players with added characters and abilities to add increased depth that will please the more experienced gamer and past Soul Puzzler players.

During each turn a player may take one of two actions, that of playing a Soul Token or switching an existing Soul Token's Position. Once the Soul Token is placed or a Soul Token is switched, the turn then goes to the next player. When you have 3, 4, or 5 of your Soul Tokens in a Row or Column, you remove them and score points equal to the number of tokens you remove. All Tokens above the removed Soul Tokens fall to the bottom most portion of the board. The first player to reach 15 points wins.

boardgame: Shadows over Normandie: Cthulhu Mythos Call #3
description:
A set of four fan-made 'Print & Play' punchboards that expand Shadows over Normandie and introduce the FFI army into the Cthulhu world. Made by the Devil Pig Games French forum moderator, Guillaume Duprez. Approved by Devil Pig Games' Yann and Clem as an 'official product'.

				
				
					Two punchboards for the FFI army:• Recruitment tile for Julie Aubrac.
• Recruitment tile for Michel and his 'Cellule de Guesclin'.
• PIAT squad
• Side-car motorcycle squad
• Mortar squad
• 'Traction à Gaz' car with the scout ability
• Citroën T23 Transport Trucks (without weapons) 
• Recruitment options: 'Bicycles', 'Knife', 'Tricky' and 'Concealed' to improve your FFI troops.

				
				
					One punchboard with the evil version of Heinrich Von Kamptz:• Heinrich Von Kamptz recruitment tile as a Black Sun hero
• FFI Civilians and FFI Civilians at Arms support options
• Bloodborn units and spell
• Claus Feuerhart, Black Sun Feldwebel hero
• A few new recruitment options: Arcane Fire, ...

				
				
					One punchboard with N°666, Black Sun Executioner• N°DCLXVI, Black Sun Executioner recruitment tile and unit
• Black Sun Flammwerwerfer Group.
• Claws recruitment option
• Pyramid of Leng tokens
• Recruitment options to introduce the FFI army into the world of Shadows over Normandie, with the Mythos Fighter, Insiders, Watchers and Insiders abilities
______________________________

Quoting GDuprez, January 2018:
"As you know, during the past months the Pigs have been extremely busy (that’s putting it mildly): finalisation of the card game KS as well as that of Age of Towers, add to that of course, the creation of Black Reach.

Meanwhile, since HoN and SoN were lying somewhat dormant, I tried to create units, tiles, markers and other options. Thanks to Clem and Yann who generously gave precious advice and granted access to the rich graphic library of Devil Pig, I have been equiped with both intel and means to embark on this adventure which is in all quite new to me.

But where to begin? Clearly, the problem was not finding an idea but choosing the right one from my vast amount of ideas… The arrival of the FFI dice and order tokens tilted the balance. Reading up on the subject along with finding several internet images pertaining to the French Resistance provided inspiration. My vision on how this page of our history is intertwined with the secret war in the universe of Achtung Cthulhu prompted me to create a new FFI unit and different options.

For a long time I wanted to expand this faction so as to turn it into a complete and in certain cases independent army. Initially made to represent only the French Resistance, an FFI army can be used to depict any group of civilians that has taken up arms, regardless of their nationality in the scenario or whether they are deployed in the HoN or SoN universe. I spent many hours in front of my screen, with trials and errors, corrections, new attempts… So finally today, I have the pleasure of presenting to you, my first punchboard. It is approved by Yann and Clem and validated as an official DPG product."
family: Series: Heroes System Tactical Scale World War 2

description:
From The Sea is a pirate themed board game for 2 to 5 players set at the very end of the Golden Age of Piracy in the first quarter of the 18th century. You take on the role of some of the most famous pirates in history, Bartholomew “Black Bart” Roberts, Henry Avery, Anne Bonney, and of course Edward “Blackbeard” Teach to name a few. Recruit your crew, take down merchant ships and their escorts, sell your plunder, build your fleet, and survive the British Navy as they attempt to exterminate the free brotherhood of pirates.

Act I Recruit Your Crew
Recruit a hearty crew of hardened sailors to help you become the greatest pirate in history. Be careful though, each crew member will have a specialty, gunnery, navigation, and morale. You’ll need their help to succeed, and when you take more ships, they are the ones you’ll depend on to captain them.
You’ll need to keep your crew active to prevent a mutiny while you build your strength, and your fleet. Capture merchant ships or use your wealth to purchase ships and add them to your fleet or capture and sell cargo to keep them busy. Too long idle at sea and they’ll mutiny!

Act II Build Your Fleet
You can build your fleet by building your wealth and purchasing new ships, or the tried and true Pirate way, take them. You’ll need a strong fleet to fend of the Navy. You can have up to three ships under your control that you can send on missions to sell your goods, capture ships, visits ports to find treasure, or harass your enemies. You can even send them in search of a Letter Of Marque, just in case you want to hedge your bets and maybe switch sides when convenient of course.

Act III Survive The British Navy
As the sun sets on the Golden Age of Piracy, the British Navy comes to exterminate the pirate scourge. Are you and your band of fellow pirates enough to fight them off? Who will be the last pirate standing? Did you uncover the Letter Of Marque and will you switch sides and fight for the Crown at the very end to save your skin? The game concludes with an epic battle to see who will survive to the end!

—description from the publisher

Hands of Time is a one player card game. You're trapped inside time! To escape you must fix the clock before the hour strikes twelve, without being caught by Father Time, or you'll be trapped in time forever.

There are only 54 cards in this solitaire game. You use these cards to build a clock board upon which are the twelve number cards of a clock. Arrange the clock numbers in their correct order to fix the clock, while keeping your player character ahead of Father Time.

When you draw a bell card the clock hands move forward an hour and the bell tolls. You must fix the clock before it strikes twelve!

The movement of the player and Father Time cards around the outside of the clock creates a tense cat and mouse mechanic that perfectly fits the game's theme.

Originally created for the 2018 54-Card Game Design Contest, it is available as a free print and play game.

—description from the designer

Awards:

From the 2018 54-Card Game Design Contest
-Best Solo Game: Second Place (Tied)
-Best Artwork: Fifth Place (Tied)
-Best New Designer: Second Place
-Best Overall Game: Fourth Place

boardgame: Batman: Gotham City Chronicles – Versus Expansion
description:
Good versus evil. Light versus darkness. Heroes versus villains. Opposition is the core of the Batman universe. Sometimes villains turn to heroes and heroes to villains, blurring the lines, but the dichotomy is always there.

To represent this opposition, we developed a whole new mode which pits one player against another, each of them controlling their forces through their own command board, giving a more “skirmish” feel to the game. This mode adds an activation pool with a recovery system (very much like the Reserve and Fatigue zones work), dictating the pace of the game by allowing the player to decide how many tiles they want to activate each turn.

This expansion includes an additional plastic command board, 20 activation disks, and 55 tiles to play the Hero’s characters. It also adds 12 ally miniatures on the heroes’ side, boosting their forces to face the already numerous goons of the villain’s side. Finally, the expansion comes with 8 dedicated scenarios and, of course, the rules to play the Versus mode.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Tezzeret, Master of Metal
description:
Part of the Aether Revolt line for Magic: The Gathering. 
This is a Planeswalker deck that is ready to play out of the box, and can be used as a starter deck. The set also includes two sealed boosters.

Tezzeret is a builder of machines who uses blue and black mana. He relies heavily on artifacts and costructs. As a Planeswalker, he can extract artifacts from the player's library, deal damage equal to the number of artifacts he controls, and take control of the opponent's artifacts.

The Fa Mel Ruins Arena offers new objectives, new challenges and a brand new strategy set in a glass-cannon capture the flag battle!

There is a new way to earn victory on this map and an added benefit/threat to all opponents. The task is to get a stripped down archer from your end of the battlefield to the other side. But before you can move him, you need to get the crowd involved. As you gain favor, your archer gains the ability to move...and more. Eventually, if protected, it becomes as strong an asset as your champion! Losing your archer does not lose the game but it's a lot like losing your queen in chess. It might be hard to recover. With center pillars blocking movement, you will need to rely on your city's specific abilities and tactics to work your way past the beasts and engage your opponent. Plenty of new challenges await you in the FaMel Ruins!

This arena fits right inside the Hoplo TLC or RoR boxes and will work with units from all 3 Hoplo games!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Colosseum Alter of the Sun is the sequel of Colosseum. This game is basically the same game to the previous game only with different characters and skills. You can even play with character cards and overdrive cards of both games mixed together.

Colosseum is a one-on-one duel card game. This game reflects many features seen in fighting video games, such as character-specific abilities, distance control, anticipating opponent's moves, three-way-deadlock skill interactions, and overdrive skills. With these features, this game provides an environment where players' risk and return fluctuate throughout the game.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
In the back alleys, boardrooms, and nightclubs are business men, ladies, and thugs. Everyone wants a piece of the pie and It’s all for the taking if you have the guts to grab it.

In Welcome To The Jungle, you take on the role of a crew leader using cash and resources to take over territories, and become kingpin of the jungle. But beware. Your opponents are furthering claims, and tightening their grasp on territories they already control. Only you can gather the crew, weapons, and abilities to battle them by drafting cards in your time of need. First player to become kingpin of the most territories in the jungle wins, so what are you waiting for?

Will you rise or will you fall? There’s no surprise, we’ve seen it all… in the jungle. Welcome.
______________________________

Welcome to The Jungle is a 2-4 player game, where the goal is to become Kingpin of the majority of territories in The Jungle. Players will do this by gaining resources and money through Gambling, drawing Smash and Grab cards, selling weapons, and winning "Turf Wars".

In a Turf War, players fight each other for territories in control. When a player enters a Turf War they draw 15 cards from the "Crew" deck, choosing 10 they would like to keep. Players can also use Weapons and special ability cards to strengthen their deck. Turf wars are 3 rounds, with the player that wins two out of the three rounds winning the turf, and winning the resources and money from the territory.

There's a lot of ways Welcome to The Jungle can be played so read the Rules and Play section below for more details.

Game summary
    - A board game for 2-4 players
    - Run time is 30 min per player
    - Combination of area control, resource management, card battling, deck building, and gambling bring a unique player experience
    - "Street Cred" card leveling up system makes each play through unique 
    - Each crew character has a backstory which builds on The Jungle lore
    - 3 different game modes available to play

boardgame: Apothecaries and Tithes (fan expansion for Carcassonne)
description: This expansion adds apothecaries to the world of Carcassonne- prepare concoctions to improve your realm, or destroy that of your enemies...

This expansion adds breweries that increase the score of a completed cloister, and the ability to declare cloisters heretical sects, which negates these bonuses.

Approved by Carcassonnecentral and carcassonne-forum

A corn circle tile allows a player to place a second follower on a feature that they have already-claimed or return an already-placed follower back to his supply.

Approved by carcassonnecentral and carcassonne-forum

boardgame: Drought and Pestilence (fan expansion for Carcassonne)
description:
1. Place a tile
The tiles are placed in accordance to the normal rules. The tiles depict the following new element:
Rat infestation
The tile has rats in the city and a skull-and-crossbones icon.
Drought
The tile has a dried and cracked piece of land and a crossed water-droplet icon.
2. Deploy a follower
Followers are deployed to the rat-infested city as a knight
per the usual rules. Followers may not be deployed
specifically to the drought area but may be deployed to
the farm surrounding the drought area as usual.
If a barn1 is played to a farm that has one or more drought
icons, or if a farm with a farmer is joined to a farm with a
barn and the resulting farm has a drought icon, farmers in
that farm score one point less per city prior to being
removed from play.

Approved by carcassonnecentral and carcassonne-forum

boardgame: Fields and Vineyards (fan expansion for Carcassonne)
description:
Based on the original idea by wellidesign, and tiles by Gwommy, which enables double turn for shrines.

Approved by carcassonnecentral and carcassonne-forum

Based on the original idea by wellidesigns, and tiles by Chris Lynn, which enable double turn for cloisters.

Approved by carcassonnecentral and carcassonne-forum

The Fishermen series of expansions changes the river such that it becomes like any normal tile feature.  The 60-tile Fishermen expansion may be added to a game of Carcassonne independently of the others.  All the other expansions in the series require that the Fishermen expansion be played with.  This series was developed by Novelty

Approved by carcassonnecentral and carcassonne-forum

description:

boardgame: Fisherman: Swan Lake (fan expansion for Carcassonne)

This expansion by hester adds 3 new waterfall tiles.

The Forests expansion is a mammoth 150-tile expansion and may be added to a game of Carcassonne independently of the others. All the other expansions in the series require the Forests expansion to be played with. The series were developed by Scott and Novelty.

boardgame: Forests: An Apple a Day (fan expansion for Carcassonne)
description:
This expansion by Cappy adds magical apples trade goods.  Some of these apple trade goods have special abilities which may be used by the player who has obtained the apple trade goods.

description:
This expansion adds a Forest fire tile that wrecks havoc on forests.

Approved by carcassonnecentral and carcassonne-forum

Adds wood trade goods on Forest tiles.

Approved by carcassonnecentral and carcassonne-forum

This expansion adds a new landscape feature and gold trade goods. (Several years before the official Goldmines expansion.)

Approved by carcassonnecentral and carcassonne-forum

The thrill of the hunt... a winter's chill... the dreams we have... and even the radishes we enjoy. Each of these forces affect the world around us and are in fact personified by a spirit. These Kami work together to form the natural order of things, but each aspires for influence over those that worship them.

Kami-sama is a beautiful and intuitive strategy game set in rural Japan during the Edo period. You assume the role of one of the many Kami, the spirits of the land. Using a combination of asymmetrical powers, area control, set collection, pattern building, and light card drafting, you will work to balance your Favor with the people and your connection to Nature in order to be crowned Kami-sama, the chief deity of the land.

Kami-sama: Spirit Way contains expansion content for Kami-Sama. Featuring 4 all new Kami, and introducing 4 Merchant Villager cards to add to your game! Spirit Way requires Kami-sama to play.

boardgame: In the Stocks (fan expansion for Carcassonne)
description:
When a player draws a tile with stocks he must play it to an existing city. The player is free to deploy his follower  to the city or he may place a follower into the stocks.  A player is not obligated either to deploy a follower to the city or detain a follower in the stocks. If the tile cannot be played, it is removed from the game and a new tile drawn to replace it.

Approved by carcassonnecentral and carcassonne-forum

When a player draws the jousting tile he must play it to an existing city. The player is free to deploy his follower to the city (if legal) or he may attempt to become jousting champion. If the tile can’t be played, it is removed from the game, the player drawing a new tile as a replacement from the supply.

Approved by carcassonnecentral and carcassonne-forum

Music, travel, love and luck… green tea by the light of a lantern… secrets and frogs. Each of these forces are in fact personified by a spirit. These Kami work together to form the natural order of things, but each aspires for influence over those that worship them.

Kami-sama is a beautiful and intuitive strategy game set in rural Japan during the Edo period. You assume the role of one of the many Kami, the spirits of the land. Using a combination of asymmetrical powers, area control, set collection, pattern building, and light card drafting, you will work to balance your Favor with the people and your connection to Nature in order to be crowned Kami-sama, the chief deity of the land.

Kami-sama: The Awakened is a Limited Quantity expansion to Kami-sama that contains all the unlocked stretch goal content from the 2018 Kickstarter. Included in this expansion are 8 new Kami, 8 new Villager cards, and 4 new Kamidana shrines each with rules of their own!

The Awakened requires Kami-sama to play.

Key Points
- Beautiful and intense area control game with set collection and light card drafting.
- Asymmetric  Kami abilities allow you to play with a different strategy and tactics every game!
- Unique rotating board with elegant gameplay.

boardgame: Lord of the Manor (fan expansion for Carcassonne)
description:
It adds castles to cities and farms.

Approved by carcassonnecentral and carcassonne-forum

boardgame: Mills and Bakeries (fan expansion for Carcassonne)
description:
It introduces mills and bakeries.

Approved by carcassonnecentral and carcassonne-forum

The Mountains series of expansions introduces Mountains as a new landscape feature.  Mountains are not scored like the normal landscape feature, but are instead scored when the tile is played.  All the other expansions in the series require that the Mountains expansion be played with.

Approved by carcassonnecentral and carcassonne-forum

The player may elect to deploy a follower to the city instead of hanging a follower. If he wishes to do this he uses the tile as a regular tile and according to the usual rules of deployment. A deployed follower in this case must be placed on the tile but outside of the gallows area and on the city segment of the tile. This tile with gallows takes no further part in the game, and no player is able hang a follower using this tile with gallows on any subsequent turn. If there is a hanged follower in the gallows area, it is possible to deploy a follower to the city segment of the tile, as a hanged follower cannot control the city.

Approved by carcassonnecentral and carcassonne-forum

boardgame: The Grim Reaple (fan expansion for Carcassonne)
description:
When the grave yard tile is laid, the Grim Reaple comes into play. The Grim Reaple reaps one meeple. This is done in a certain order. First, the Reaple takes the closest robber on a road that touches the grave yard. If there is no robber, then the Reaple takes a knight or mayor from a city that is contingent to the graveyard or is connected to the graveyard by road. If there is no knight or mayor in a city connected to the graveyard, then the Reaple takes a monk from a cloister or abbey that is contingent to or in the same field as the grave yard. Lastly, as a professional courtesy to a meeple that uses the same tool of the trade, and a monk is not present in a contingent cloister/abbey, the Reaple takes a farmer from a field that touches the graveyard.

Approved by carcassonnecentral and carcassonne-forum

The Oceans series of expansions introduces Oceans as a new landscape feature.  Oceans divide the playing area into two and allows for swashbuckling adventure.  All the other expansions in the series require that the Oceans expansion be played with.  The series may also be played as a standalone (i.e. without the other Carcassonne expansions).

Approved by carcassonnecentral and carcassonne-forum

boardgame: The Orders of Chivalry (fan expansion for Carcassonne)
description:
If the player places a chivalry tile onto a city that he has occupancy in, he may exchange one follower of his own colour for another of his own colour. For example, a Yellow regular Knight is deployed in a city. On his turn, Yellow draws and lays a chivalry tile that extends his already occupied city. He may now exchange the regular Knight in this city for another legal follower.
1,
2 It is irrelevant how many players occupy the city or who has the majority in that city at the time of exchange. An exchange occurs instead of ‘moving the wood’
3 and must take place at the time of laying the chivalry tile.
The idea of exchange is one of promotion or demotion. Knights who served their position well might be honoured to a higher Order. Those who neglected their duties could be stripped of their title.

Approved by carcassonnecentral and carcassonne-forum

boardgame: The Pope of Avignon (fan expansion for Carcassonne)
description:
The Avignon City tile is mixed in with the other land tiles.

The Avignon City tile is placed in the same way as a normal tile. When the Avignon City tile is drawn and laid, the player who laid the tile deploys the Pope onto the city segment of the Avignon City tile. The player can place the tile and connect it to another city that already holds a follower.

1 point is added to every tile of the completed city if it holds the Pope.

Adding a Cathar tile or a siege tile to the city that holds the Pope will allow an opportunity to redeploy the Pope to any incomplete city on the playing area.  Then later in the game another city may get the bonus points for the Pope.

Quotable Rules

Objective: This is a game full of funny/insightful/awful quotes—players rarely know who actually said these quotes, because honestly, can you name a single quote by Kim Kardashian? The objective is to either write the correct answer or trick your friends into thinking your answer is the right person.

Setup:

Players: 3-8 players

Phones: *A companion app is in the works* For now exchange phone numbers with the other players. Players will text answers to the Reader each round.

Scorekeeper: Designate a Scorekeeper—this person needs to pay special attention to “Scoring” Section…obviously.

Reader: Player with birthday closest to January 1 begins the game as Reader—the Reader will facilitate each round.

Gameplay:

1. The first Reader draws a card and reads the quote without revealing author.

2. TEXTING ROUND: Players text Reader a guess of who said the quote (at this point, players either think they know the answer or are trying to think of a fake author to trick their friends)

3. Reader re-reads quote after all texts are received

4. Reader announces all the options including correct author in any order. If 2 options are the same, only read that option once. (This includes repeat texts, and someone texting the correct answer.)

5. VOTING ROUND: After announcing all options, Reader calls for votes on each option. (Players MUST vote, and may not vote for their own answer, even if they believe it to be correct.)

6. Scorekeeper tallies/announces scores. Then player to left of Reader is now Reader and round is complete

Scoring: 
Player texts correct author of quote on answer card (5 Points)
Player receives vote for their texted answer, if it was not correct. (1 Point each)
Player votes for correct author of quote in voting round (2 Points)
No player votes (or writes) correct author (3 Points for READER)
First Player to 21 Wins

It adds different levels to cities.
